text: At least one binary feature in the dataset is associated with `objective_response` at p<0.001 in a univariate test (chi-square).
rationale: Generic existence claim about any binary feature is not specific to the treatment-by-mutation-conjunction subgroup effect.

text: At least one continuous feature differs in mean value between responders (`objective_response`=1) and non-responders (`objective_response`=0) at p<0.001 (Welch t-test).
rationale: Targets continuous features and a marginal mean difference, not the treatmentÃ—mutation-conjunction subgroup effect on objective_response.

text: At least one multi-level ordinal feature is monotonically associated with `objective_response` (Spearman rho non-zero, p<0.001).
rationale: Targets ordinal features with monotonic association, unrelated to the binary treatment and mutation variables in the ground truth.

text: Response rate falls monotonically as `feature_057` increases from 0 to 2 (i.e., `feature_057` behaves like a risk-stratification variable, with each unit increase reducing the log-odds of `objective_response`).
rationale: Concerns feature_057, which is not among the ground-truth variables (treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine, idh1, npm1, complex_karyotype, tp53).

text: Patients with `feature_035`=1 have higher `objective_response` than those with `feature_035`=0 (positive main effect on the response probability).
rationale: Proposes a main effect of idh1_mutation alone, omitting the treatment variable and the npm1/karyotype/tp53 conjunction that defines the active subgroup.

text: Higher values of the continuous biomarker `feature_011` are associated with lower probability of `objective_response` (negative slope on the log-odds scale).
rationale: Concerns continuous feature_011, which is not part of the ground-truth variable set.

text: Higher values of `feature_006` (range 20-100) are associated with lower probability of `objective_response`.
rationale: Concerns feature_006, which is not part of the ground-truth variable set.

text: In a multivariable logistic regression of `objective_response` on all 124 features (with continuous predictors standardized), feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, and feature_035 each retain independent statistically significant effects (p<0.001), implying their univariate associations are not fully confounded by other variables.
rationale: Names independent main effects of feature_057/011/006/035 and omits treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine plus the npm1/karyotype/tp53 conjunction required for the subgroup-conditional effect.

text: Beyond the four headline predictors, additional continuous biomarkers (feature_099, feature_063, feature_092) and at least one binary (feature_093) have independent associations with `objective_response` at p<0.05 in the full multivariable model.
rationale: Lists unrelated features (099, 063, 092, 093) rather than the ground-truth treatment and mutation variables.

text: The favorable effect of `feature_035` on `objective_response` differs across `feature_057` strata (i.e., the log-odds increment from `feature_035`=1 is larger or smaller in higher-risk strata than in lower-risk strata).
rationale: Proposes a feature_035 Ã— feature_057 interaction, but feature_057 is not in the ground-truth set and the treatment variable plus npm1/karyotype/tp53 conjunction are absent.

text: The negative slope of `feature_011` on `objective_response` is steeper at higher levels of `feature_057` (i.e., `feature_057` modifies the `feature_011` effect).
rationale: Targets feature_011 and feature_057, neither of which are the ground-truth variables (treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine, idh1, npm1, complex_karyotype, tp53).

text: The negative effect of `feature_006` on `objective_response` is concentrated within higher `feature_057` strata.
rationale: Targets feature_006 and feature_057, not any of the ground-truth variables.

text: The favorable effect of `feature_035` is attenuated in patients with high `feature_011` (i.e., negative interaction between `feature_035` and `feature_011`).
rationale: Includes idh1 (feature_035) but pairs it with feature_011 instead of the required treatment, npm1, complex_karyotype, tp53 conjunction, and proposes a negative direction contrary to the positive ground-truth effect.

text: Crude `objective_response` rates differ across the five `feature_005` (race/ethnicity) categories.
rationale: Examines race/ethnicity (feature_005), which is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: After adjusting for the top clinical predictors (feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, feature_035, feature_078), Black patients (`feature_005`='black') still have lower `objective_response` than White patients (`feature_005`='white').
rationale: Focuses on race effect after adjustment; does not target the venetoclax/azacitidine Ã— IDH1 Ã— NPM1 Ã— non-complex-karyotype Ã— TP53-WT subgroup.

text: Crude `objective_response` rates differ across `feature_087` (insurance) categories.
rationale: Examines insurance (feature_087), not any ground-truth variable.

text: The negative slope of `feature_057` on `objective_response` differs across `feature_005` strata (i.e., race modifies the cytogenetic-risk gradient).
rationale: Tests interaction between cytogenetic risk (feature_057) and race (feature_005); neither is in the ground-truth variable set as specified.

text: The favorable effect of `feature_035` differs across `feature_005` strata (i.e., race modifies the `feature_035` benefit).
rationale: Includes idh1 but interacts it with race rather than the required treatment + npm1 + non-complex-karyotype + TP53-WT subgroup.

text: Receipt of `feature_035` differs by `feature_005` (i.e., the prevalence of `feature_035`=1 is different across racial groups).
rationale: Outcome here is receipt of feature_035 by race, not objective_response, and does not address the ground-truth subgroup-conditional treatment effect.

text: `feature_078` (range 18-92, plausibly age in years) is associated with `objective_response` either linearly or with a non-linear (quadratic) shape.
rationale: Examines age (feature_078) main effect on objective_response; age is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: `feature_099` (range 1.7-5.5) is positively associated with `objective_response` (i.e., higher values predict higher response).
rationale: feature_099 is not among the ground-truth variables (treatment_014, idh1_035, npm1_027, complex_karyotype_013, tp53_009).

text: `feature_063` (skewed continuous, range 0.03-300) is inversely associated with `objective_response`.
rationale: feature_063 is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: `feature_092` (range 0.5-500) is inversely associated with `objective_response`.
rationale: feature_092 is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Beyond feature_035, additional binary features (feature_093, feature_121, feature_014, feature_012) carry independent information about `objective_response` after adjustment for the four headline predictors (feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, feature_035, feature_099).
rationale: Mentions feature_014 and feature_035 but tests independent main effects after adjustment, not the four-way subgroup-conditional interaction with feature_027, feature_013, and feature_009.

text: Patients with `feature_093`=1 have higher `objective_response` than patients with `feature_093`=0, both crudely and after adjustment.
rationale: feature_093 is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Patients with `feature_121`=1 have lower `objective_response` than patients with `feature_121`=0 (negative main effect).
rationale: feature_121 is not among the ground-truth variables, and the direction is wrong.

text: Receipt of both `feature_035`=1 and `feature_093`=1 produces additional benefit beyond the sum of their individual effects (positive interaction on log-odds scale).
rationale: feature_093 is not among the ground-truth variables, and the interaction omits treatment_014, npm1_027, complex_karyotype_013, and tp53_009.

text: Joint stratification by `feature_057` and `feature_035` produces a 6-cell risk grouping with a >2.5-fold spread in `objective_response` rates from the most-favorable cell (feature_057=0, feature_035=1) to the least-favorable cell (feature_057=2, feature_035=0).
rationale: feature_057 is not among the ground-truth variables, and treatment_014, npm1_027, complex_karyotype_013, and tp53_009 are absent.

text: There is a significant negative interaction between `feature_011` and `feature_063` on `objective_response` (i.e., the combination of high feature_011 and high feature_063 produces a worse response than either alone would predict).
rationale: Neither feature_011 nor feature_063 is among the ground-truth variables.

text: A parsimonious 9-feature multivariable logit model (feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, feature_035, feature_099, feature_063, feature_092, feature_093, feature_121) discriminates responders from non-responders with AUC > 0.59.
rationale: Includes feature_014 and feature_035 but omits npm1_027, complex_karyotype_013, and tp53_009, and a main-effects logit cannot capture the four-way subgroup-conditional structure.

text: Across all univariate and multivariable analyses, exactly four predictors (feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, feature_035) emerge as the dominant independent drivers of `objective_response` (each p<1e-15 in the multivariable model).
rationale: Names only feature_035 from the spec, omits the treatment (feature_014), npm1 (feature_027), complex_karyotype (feature_013), and tp53 (feature_009), and frames the relationship as additive main effects rather than the subgroup-conditional treatment effect.

text: There is a residual Black-vs-White disparity in `objective_response` that is not explained by the measured clinical features in this dataset.
rationale: Concerns a race-based residual disparity and does not reference the treatment or any of the four mutation/karyotype variables that define the ground-truth subgroup.

text: After controlling for clinical predictors and risk strata, neither `feature_078` (likely age) nor `feature_087` (insurance) shows a significant independent association with `objective_response`.
rationale: Addresses age and insurance as null predictors and does not point at the treatment, IDH1, NPM1, complex karyotype, or TP53 variables or their conjunction.

text: No 2-way interaction among the top predictors (feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, feature_035) reaches statistical significance at p<0.05 - effects are well-described as additive on the log-odds scale.
rationale: Examines interactions only among feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, and feature_035, missing the treatment and the NPM1/complex_karyotype/TP53 variables, and asserts additivity rather than the subgroup-conditional effect.

text: Among the 125 candidate features, at least one ordinal performance-status-like variable (feature_057, with levels 0/1/2 and prevalence 39%/49%/12%) is inversely associated with objective_response: response rates decrease monotonically as feature_057 increases.
rationale: Targets feature_057 (performance status); does not involve treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine or the IDH1/NPM1/karyotype/TP53 subgroup.

text: Higher values of the continuous variable feature_011 (range 0-23.6) are associated with lower probability of objective_response.
rationale: Targets continuous feature_011; not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Higher values of the continuous variable feature_006 (range 20-100, plausibly a clinical performance/index measure) are associated with lower probability of objective_response.
rationale: Targets continuous feature_006; not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Patients with feature_035=1 (a rare binary marker, prevalence 7.3%) have a higher probability of objective_response than patients with feature_035=0.
rationale: Posits a main effect of feature_035 (IDH1) alone and omits treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine plus the NPM1/complex_karyotype/TP53 conditioning, so it does not identify the subgroup-conditional treatment effect.

text: feature_092 (continuous, range 0.5-500, plausibly a high-skew lab value) is inversely associated with objective_response.
rationale: Targets continuous feature_092; not among the ground-truth variables.

text: feature_063 (continuous, wide-range lab) is inversely associated with objective_response.
rationale: Targets continuous feature_063; not among the ground-truth variables.

text: feature_099 (continuous, range 1.7-5.5) is positively associated with objective_response.
rationale: Targets continuous feature_099; not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Objective response rates differ across categories of feature_005 (race: white, black, hispanic, asian, other), with white patients having higher response than non-white patients.
rationale: Targets feature_005 (race); not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Objective response rates differ across categories of feature_087 (insurance: medicaid, medicare, private, uninsured); uninsured patients have lower response than privately insured patients.
rationale: Targets feature_087 (insurance); not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Patient age (feature_078, range 18-92, mean 68) is inversely associated with objective_response.
rationale: Targets feature_078 (age); not among the ground-truth variables.

text: In a multivariable logistic model that includes feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, feature_063, feature_092, feature_099, feature_035, feature_078, race (feature_005), and insurance (feature_087), the apparent univariate race association (white > non-white) attenuates and becomes non-significant, indicating that race-level differences in response are explained by clinical/lab features rather than race itself.
rationale: Focuses on race attenuation in a main-effects logistic model and does not address the treatment-by-mutation subgroup interaction or include treatment, NPM1, complex karyotype, or TP53.

text: After full clinical adjustment, the strongest independent predictors of response remain feature_057 (negative), feature_035 (positive), feature_011 (negative), feature_006 (negative), feature_099 (positive), and feature_063 (negative).
rationale: Lists main-effect predictors and omits treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine and any interaction structure, so it cannot identify the subgroup-conditional treatment effect.

text: The positive effect of feature_035=1 on objective_response is heterogeneous across feature_057 strata, with a larger absolute response-rate gain in patients with feature_057=0 (better performance) than in feature_057=2.
rationale: Proposes heterogeneity by feature_057 (performance status), not by treatment, NPM1, complex karyotype, or TP53, so it targets the wrong effect modifier.

text: The effect of feature_035=1 on response is modified by age (elderly = age>=75): the OR is smaller in elderly patients than in younger patients.
rationale: Proposes age as the effect modifier rather than the treatment-by-mutation-profile structure specified by the ground truth.

text: The effect of feature_035=1 on response is heterogeneous across race (feature_005): the OR varies by race.
rationale: Proposes race as the effect modifier of feature_035, which is not the relationship in the spec.

text: Among 80 binary features (prevalence 2-98%), at least one shows a strong multiplicative interaction with feature_035 such that feature_035=1 gives a much larger OR for response when that other feature equals 1 than when it equals 0.
rationale: Generic two-way interaction screen with feature_035 does not specify treatment or NPM1 as the partner and ignores complex karyotype and TP53, so it would not identify the multi-way subgroup-conditional treatment effect.

text: feature_027 (binary, prevalence 30%) has minimal main effect on response after controlling for feature_035 because its apparent effect operates almost entirely through interaction with feature_035.
rationale: Restricted to a feature_035 x feature_027 interaction, missing the treatment variable and the complex-karyotype/TP53 subgroup conditions central to the DGP.

text: feature_014 (binary, prevalence 40%) similarly has minimal main effect once interaction with feature_035 is modeled.
rationale: Restricted to feature_035 x feature_014, missing NPM1 and the complex-karyotype/TP53 subgroup conditions, so it cannot capture the spec's subgroup-conditional effect.

text: The two two-way interactions (feature_035 x feature_027 and feature_035 x feature_014) are not redundant: both terms remain significant when included in the same model.
rationale: Tests two pairwise interactions but omits complex_karyotype and TP53 and does not posit the synergistic subgroup-conditional structure of the DGP.

text: There is a synergistic three-way interaction between feature_035, feature_027, and feature_014 such that the response rate is dramatically higher when all three equal 1 than would be predicted from main effects plus pairwise interactions alone.
rationale: Proposes a positive synergistic three-way interaction among treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine, IDH1, and NPM1 with response dramatically higher when all three equal 1, which corresponds to the core treatment-by-mutation-profile synergy in the ground truth (the TP53/complex-karyotype exclusions further refine but do not change the captured DGP signal).

text: After adjusting for the triple interaction (feature_035 x feature_027 x feature_014) and for the main clinical predictors (feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, feature_092, feature_063, feature_099, feature_078), race (feature_005) and insurance (feature_087) remain independently NON-significant, i.e., demographic disparity is not present in this cohort's response outcome after appropriate clinical adjustment.
rationale: Hypothesis is about demographic (race/insurance) non-significance after adjustment, not about the treatment-biomarker subgroup effect itself.

text: feature_093 (binary, prevalence 8%) and feature_121 (binary, prevalence 8%) are independently associated with response after full adjustment (positive and negative direction respectively).
rationale: Targets unrelated features (feature_093, feature_121), not the venetoclax+azacitidine/IDH1/NPM1/karyotype/TP53 variables.

text: Patients with the joint phenotype (feature_027=1 AND feature_014=1 AND feature_035=1) constitute a small subpopulation (<1% of cohort) whose objective_response rate (>=50%) is several-fold higher than that of the rest of the cohort, consistent with a biomarker-defined subgroup that is highly responsive to a specific therapy or vice versa.
rationale: Identifies a high-response biomarker-defined subgroup combining IDH1+NPM1+treatment with positive direction, capturing the core data-generating process via subgroup analysis.

text: Outside the (feature_027=1, feature_014=1, feature_035=1) subgroup, feature_035 has effectively no main effect on response, so the apparent univariate OR=1.44 for feature_035 (Iteration 2) is entirely driven by this small high-response subgroup.
rationale: Proposes the IDH1 effect is concentrated in the IDH1+NPM1+treatment subgroup with elevated response, consistent with the ground-truth subgroup-conditional positive effect.

text: Among the 80 binary features, at least one shows a clinically meaningful difference in objective_response rate between feature-positive and feature-negative patients (chi-square p < 0.001).
rationale: Generic claim about any binary feature; does not identify the specific treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine Ã— idh1 Ã— npm1 Ã— complex_karyotype Ã— tp53 subgroup interaction.

text: Among the 35 continuous features, at least one shows a difference in mean value between responders and non-responders (Welch t-test p < 0.001), indicating prognostic value.
rationale: Targets continuous features, but the ground-truth variables (treatment, IDH1, NPM1, complex karyotype, TP53) are all binary.

text: Among the 10 multi-category features (feature_057, feature_086, feature_096, feature_064, feature_018, feature_045, feature_042, feature_125, feature_005, feature_087), at least one stratifies objective_response rate (chi-square p < 0.001).
rationale: Targets multi-category features, none of which correspond to the binary treatment or mutation/karyotype variables in the ground truth.

text: Higher values of feature_057 are associated with lower odds of objective_response in an ordinal-linear fashion (logistic regression OR per level < 1, p < 0.001).
rationale: Concerns feature_057 only and proposes a linear ordinal effect, not the treatment Ã— four-mutation subgroup interaction.

text: After mutual adjustment in a multivariable logistic model, feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, feature_035, feature_099, feature_063, and feature_092 each retain independent associations with objective_response (each adjusted p < 0.01).
rationale: Multivariable main-effects model that includes feature_035 (IDH1) but omits the treatment (feature_014), NPM1, complex karyotype, and TP53, and does not capture the subgroup interaction.

text: Patient age (feature_078) is independently associated with objective_response after adjusting for the top clinical predictors (adjusted p < 0.05).
rationale: Concerns age, which is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: feature_057 has differential effect on objective_response across feature_035 strata: the negative slope of feature_057 is steeper in feature_035-positive patients than in feature_035-negative patients (interaction p < 0.05).
rationale: Proposes a feature_057 Ã— IDH1 interaction; feature_057 is not in the ground truth and the treatment, NPM1, complex karyotype, and TP53 are absent.

text: feature_057 has differential effect on objective_response across feature_011 strata (interaction p < 0.05).
rationale: Interaction between feature_057 and feature_011, neither of which is in the ground-truth variable set.

text: feature_057 has differential effect on objective_response across feature_006 strata (interaction p < 0.05).
rationale: Interaction between feature_057 and feature_006, neither of which is in the ground-truth variable set.

text: feature_035 (prevalence 7.2%) is associated with higher objective_response rate within every level of feature_057 (consistent positive effect across risk strata).
rationale: Asserts a consistent main effect of IDH1 across feature_057 strata; ignores the treatment and the joint NPM1/complex-karyotype/TP53 conditioning that defines the true subgroup.

text: Adjusting for feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, feature_099, feature_063, feature_092 (the dominant prognostic predictors), feature_035 retains a positive independent association with objective_response (adjusted OR > 1, p < 0.001).
rationale: Claims a main-effect adjusted association of IDH1 only; omits treatment, NPM1, complex karyotype, and TP53, and does not capture the subgroup-conditional structure.

text: Race (feature_005) is associated with objective_response at the unadjusted level, with white patients having the highest response rate and other/black patients the lowest (chi-square p < 0.05).
rationale: Concerns race (feature_005), which is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Race-based differences in objective_response are explained by differential distribution of clinical predictors (feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, feature_035) across racial groups; after adjustment, no race coefficient differs from white at p < 0.05.
rationale: Hypothesis is about race effects after adjustment; not about the venetoclax/IDH1/NPM1/karyotype/TP53 subgroup-conditional relationship.

text: Insurance type (feature_087: medicare, private, medicaid, uninsured) is associated with objective_response (unadjusted chi-square p < 0.05).
rationale: Concerns insurance type (feature_087), which is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: feature_035's effect on objective_response differs in magnitude between white and black patients (interaction p < 0.05).
rationale: Posits IDH1 Ã— race interaction; race is not in the ground-truth set and treatment/NPM1/karyotype/TP53 are absent.

text: The relationship between feature_011 and objective_response is monotonic but possibly non-linear; including a quadratic term improves fit (likelihood-ratio p < 0.05).
rationale: Concerns feature_011 functional form, unrelated to the ground-truth variables.

text: feature_092's relationship with objective_response is non-linear (LR p<0.05 for quadratic term).
rationale: Concerns feature_092 non-linearity, unrelated to the ground-truth variables.

text: Patients in the lowest quintile of feature_011 have at least 50% higher response rate (relative) than patients in the highest quintile.
rationale: Concerns feature_011 quintiles, unrelated to the ground-truth variables.

text: Among the top 10 predictors (feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, feature_035, feature_099, feature_063, feature_092, feature_093, feature_014, feature_121), at least one pairwise interaction term is significant at p<0.001 in a 3-parameter logistic model.
rationale: A generic 'some pairwise interaction exists' claim that does not target treatment Ã— IDH1 Ã— NPM1 Ã— karyotype Ã— TP53 and omits NPM1, complex karyotype, and TP53 entirely.

text: feature_035 and feature_014 show a positive multiplicative interaction: feature_014's effect on objective_response is larger in feature_035-positive patients than in feature_035-negative patients.
rationale: Captures only the IDH1 Ã— venetoclax 2-way interaction; misses NPM1, complex karyotype, and TP53 that jointly define the ground-truth subgroup.

text: Across all 3,038 binary-binary interaction tests, additional significant interactions involving feature_035 with other binary features exist beyond feature_035 x feature_014 (Bonferroni-significant at p<0.05/3038 ~= 1.6e-5).
rationale: Posits additional unspecified interactions involving feature_035 beyond the 035Ã—014 pair, not the specific subgroup-conditional treatmentÃ—IDH1Ã—NPM1 effect.

text: feature_014, feature_027, and feature_035 show a synergistic three-way interaction: response rate is dramatically elevated only when all three are positive simultaneously.
rationale: Identifies the feature_014Ã—feature_027Ã—feature_035 three-way synergy with positive direction, capturing the core treatment-biomarker subgroup-conditional effect.

text: The three-way interaction feature_014 x feature_027 x feature_035 retains significance after adjusting for all main effects, all two-way interactions, and the dominant clinical predictors (feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, feature_099, feature_063, feature_092).
rationale: Tests the feature_014Ã—feature_027Ã—feature_035 three-way interaction in an adjusted model, an interaction-aware specification appropriate for the subgroup-conditional ground truth.

text: In the fully adjusted model containing all main effects, the three-way interaction, and demographic covariates, the dominant clinical predictors (feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, feature_099, feature_063, feature_092) all retain p<0.001.
rationale: Concerns significance of unrelated prognostic predictors (feature_057, 011, 006, 099, 063, 092), not the treatmentÃ—biomarker subgroup-conditional effect.

text: In the fully adjusted model, no demographic covariate (race, insurance, age, feature_007) reaches p<0.05.
rationale: About null demographic covariates, unrelated to the treatment-biomarker subgroup-conditional effect on objective_response.

text: Patients positive for the triple feature_014/feature_027/feature_035 have substantially higher absolute response rates than the overall cohort (~17%); the absolute treatment effect compared to the rest of the cohort is at least +30 percentage points.
rationale: Identifies the feature_014/027/035 triple-positive subgroup as having substantially higher absolute response rates, matching the positive subgroup-conditional effect.

text: The pattern of effects (modest gradient by feature_057, individual contributions of feature_011/feature_006/feature_099/feature_063/feature_092, and the dominant feature_014 x feature_027 x feature_035 synergy with no race/insurance/age effects after adjustment) is consistent with: (a) feature_057 being a prognostic risk-stratification variable; (b) feature_011 a pre-treatment laboratory marker (e.g., bilirubin/blast burden); (c) feature_035 a predictive biomarker; (d) feature_014 and feature_027 two treatments whose joint efficacy is unlocked by the biomarker.
rationale: Explicitly casts feature_035 as a predictive biomarker unlocking joint feature_014+feature_027 efficacy on response, matching the subgroup-conditional treatment effect.

text: Restricting analysis to patients with feature_035=1 (n=3,624) preserves the three-way pattern: the joint effect of feature_014 + feature_027 lifts response rate above the feature_035-positive baseline.
rationale: Proposes stratifying on feature_035=1 and examining the joint feature_014+feature_027 lift in response â€” a subgroup/HTE analysis appropriate for the subgroup-conditional ground truth.

text: feature_057 (a 3-level ordinal feature, values 0/1/2) is associated with objective_response, with higher values associated with lower response probability.
rationale: feature_057 is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Higher feature_011 (continuous, range 0-23.6) is associated with lower probability of objective_response.
rationale: feature_011 is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Higher feature_006 (continuous, range 20-100) is associated with lower probability of objective_response.
rationale: feature_006 is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: feature_035 (binary, prevalence ~7%) is associated with HIGHER probability of objective_response.
rationale: Proposes only a main effect of idh1_mutation; does not reference treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine, npm1, complex_karyotype, or tp53, nor the subgroup-conditional structure.

text: Higher feature_099 (continuous, range 1.7-5.5) is associated with HIGHER probability of objective_response.
rationale: feature_099 is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Higher feature_092 (continuous, range 0.5-500) is associated with lower probability of objective_response.
rationale: feature_092 is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Higher feature_063 (continuous, range 0.03-300) is associated with lower probability of objective_response.
rationale: feature_063 is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: In a multivariable logistic model, feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, feature_063, and feature_092 each retain a negative independent association with objective_response while feature_035 and feature_099 retain positive independent associations.
rationale: A multivariable main-effects model that omits treatment, npm1, complex_karyotype, and tp53 cannot capture the 5-way subgroup-conditional relationship.

text: Patient age (feature_078, range 18-92) is NOT independently associated with objective_response after adjusting for the strong main-effect predictors.
rationale: Concerns age, none of the ground-truth variables or the subgroup relationship.

text: There is a positive interaction between feature_035 and feature_027 (binary, prevalence ~30%): the marginal effect of feature_027 on objective_response is larger (more positive) when feature_035=1 than when feature_035=0.
rationale: Identifies only an idh1Ã—npm1 interaction; omits treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine, complex_karyotype, and tp53, so it does not point at the same variable set or the full subgroup-conditional effect.

text: There is a positive interaction between feature_035 and feature_014 (binary, prevalence ~40%): the marginal effect of feature_014 on objective_response is larger when feature_035=1 than when feature_035=0.
rationale: Identifies only IDH1 x treatment; omits NPM1, complex_karyotype, and TP53 from the subgroup-defining variable set.

text: The full three-way combination feature_035=1 AND feature_027=1 AND feature_014=1 produces a substantially higher objective_response rate than predicted from main effects and pairwise interactions alone.
rationale: Names IDH1, NPM1, and treatment but omits the complex_karyotype=0 and TP53=0 predicates that are part of the ground-truth variable set.

text: Among the ~50 low-prevalence binary features (<20%), feature_035 is the unique biomarker for which the (feature_027=1 AND feature_014=1) treatment combination produces a clinically meaningful (>40%) response rate.
rationale: Frames the effect as IDH1+NPM1+treatment only and does not point at complex_karyotype or TP53.

text: The simplified construct 'received both feature_027 and feature_014 (both_tx)' interacts strongly with feature_035: in feature_035=1 patients, both_tx raises response by ~45 percentage points; in feature_035=0 patients, both_tx has no effect.
rationale: Posits a two-way interaction with IDH1 only; omits complex_karyotype and TP53 entirely.

text: Race (feature_005) is associated with objective_response: white patients have a higher response rate than non-white patients.
rationale: Concerns race/objective_response, none of the ground-truth variables.

text: Insurance type (feature_087: medicaid/medicare/private/uninsured) is associated with objective_response.
rationale: Concerns insurance type, unrelated to any ground-truth variable.

text: The benefit of the (feature_035=1 AND feature_027=1 AND feature_014=1) triple does NOT differ by race; i.e., there is no race x triple interaction.
rationale: Asserts no race interaction; about an absence of a different effect, not the IDH1/NPM1/karyotype/TP53/treatment relationship and still omits two key variables.

text: Patient age (feature_078) does not interact with feature_057: the negative effect of feature_057 on objective_response is similar across ages.
rationale: Concerns age and feature_057, none of the ground-truth variables.

text: Patient age (feature_078) does not interact with the triple (feature_035=1 AND feature_027=1 AND feature_014=1): triple benefit is similar across the age range.
rationale: A null hypothesis about age moderation; doesn't claim or detect the subgroup-conditional treatment effect itself, and omits complex_karyotype and TP53.

text: Among the 5- and 11-level ordinal features (feature_018, feature_096, feature_064, feature_045, feature_042, feature_125), none is meaningfully associated with objective_response after Bonferroni adjustment.
rationale: Concerns ordinal features unrelated to any ground-truth variable.

text: Disease-burden continuous features (feature_011, feature_006, feature_063, feature_092) do NOT meaningfully modify the marginal effect of feature_027 or feature_014 on response (no continuous-biomarker x treatment interaction).
rationale: Concerns absence of interaction between continuous disease-burden features and treatment/NPM1, not the IDH1/NPM1/karyotype/TP53/treatment subgroup-conditional effect.

text: The triple-positive response rate (~60%+) is preserved across quartiles of feature_011 and feature_006, with at most modest attenuation at the highest disease-burden levels.
rationale: Claims robustness of the triple-positive rate across disease-burden quartiles rather than proposing to identify the IDH1+NPM1+non-complex+TP53-WT subgroup effect of venetoclax+azacitidine.

text: Within the feature_035=1 subgroup, the f014 x f027 interaction effect is strongly positive (additional response benefit when both treatments given), while in feature_035=0 it is essentially zero.
rationale: Identifies the IDH1Ã—NPM1Ã—treatment subgroup but omits the required conditioning on complex_karyotype=0 and tp53_mutation=0, so it does not point at the same set of variables.

text: Within the feature_035=1 subgroup, no other binary feature produces a comparably large response benefit beyond feature_027 and feature_014.
rationale: A negative claim that no other binary feature beats NPM1+treatment within IDH1-mut; does not propose to identify the IDH1+NPM1+non-complex+TP53-WT subgroup-treatment effect.

text: After fully adjusting for feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, feature_063, feature_099, feature_092, race, and main effects of feature_035, feature_027, feature_014 plus all 2-way interactions, the triple-product (feature_035 x feature_027 x feature_014) remains a strong independent positive predictor of objective_response.
rationale: Tests a 3-way feature_035Ã—feature_027Ã—feature_014 product but does not include feature_013 or feature_009, so it cannot recover the full quadruple-conditioned subgroup effect.

text: After full adjustment, race (feature_005) does not remain a significant predictor of objective_response.
rationale: Concerns whether race remains a predictor and is unrelated to the venetoclax+azacitidine subgroup association.

text: The dataset's response heterogeneity is best summarized by: (a) seven independent prognostic main-effect features (feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, feature_063, feature_092 each negative; feature_035, feature_099 each positive) and (b) one strong predictive triple (feature_035 x feature_027 x feature_014) where benefit is concentrated.
rationale: Names a predictive triple of feature_035Ã—feature_027Ã—feature_014 but omits feature_013 and feature_009, so the variable set does not match the ground-truth subgroup definition.

text: After accounting for prognostic main effects and the predictive triple, no further binary, ordinal, demographic, or continuous feature in this dataset adds substantial explanatory power for objective_response.
rationale: A residual-explanatory-power claim about no further features mattering, not a hypothesis identifying the IDH1/NPM1/non-complex/TP53-WT subgroup benefit of venetoclax+azacitidine.

text: feature_035 (binary, ~7.2% prevalence) is associated with a higher rate of objective_response than its absence.
rationale: Identifies only idh1_mutation; omits the treatment and the npm1/complex_karyotype/tp53 conjunction defining the subgroup-conditional effect.

text: feature_057 (3-level ordinal) is associated with objective_response, with higher levels having lower response rates (a monotonic gradient).
rationale: feature_057 is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: feature_011 (continuous, range 0-23.6) is negatively associated with objective_response: higher values correspond to lower response.
rationale: feature_011 is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: feature_006 (continuous, 20-100) is negatively associated with objective_response.
rationale: feature_006 is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: feature_005 (race; categorical white/black/asian/hispanic/other) shows differences in objective_response across categories.
rationale: Race (feature_005) is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: feature_087 (insurance; medicaid/medicare/private/uninsured) shows differences in objective_response across categories.
rationale: Insurance (feature_087) is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: After mutual adjustment in a multivariable logistic regression of objective_response on all features (one-hot for categoricals/multi-level ordinals), feature_057, feature_035, feature_011, feature_006, feature_099, feature_063, and feature_092 will remain independently significant predictors.
rationale: Specifies a main-effects logistic regression that omits treatment_014, npm1_027, complex_karyotype_013, and tp53_009 and cannot capture the four-way subgroup-conditional effect.

text: feature_099 (continuous, 1.7-5.5) is positively associated with objective_response after adjustment.
rationale: feature_099 is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: feature_063 (continuous, range 0-300, mean 6.1) is negatively associated with objective_response after adjustment.
rationale: feature_063 is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: feature_092 (continuous, 0.5-500, mean 20) is negatively associated with objective_response after adjustment.
rationale: feature_092 is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: The positive effect of feature_035 on objective_response is consistent across feature_057 strata (no qualitative interaction): feature_035=1 raises response in feature_057=0, feature_057=1, and feature_057=2 alike.
rationale: Concerns feature_035 main effect across feature_057 strata; does not address the treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine effect within the IDH1+NPM1+non-complex+TP53wt subgroup.

text: On the multiplicative (log-odds) scale, the interaction feature_035 x feature_057 is not statistically significant.
rationale: Tests feature_035 x feature_057 interaction, which involves neither the treatment variable (feature_014) nor the full subgroup-defining mutations.

text: The 'treatment-like' binary feature_093 (~8% prevalence) is also independently positively associated with objective_response.
rationale: Refers to feature_093, not feature_014 (venetoclax+azacitidine), and proposes a main effect rather than the subgroup-conditional treatment effect.

text: The benefit of feature_035 is preserved across tertiles of feature_011 (continuous biomarker), with no significant feature_035 x feature_011 interaction.
rationale: Examines feature_035 x feature_011 (a continuous biomarker not in the ground truth) and ignores treatment and the other subgroup predicates.

text: There is no significant feature_035 x feature_006 interaction; the treatment effect of feature_035 is independent of feature_006.
rationale: feature_006 is not part of the ground-truth subgroup, and the hypothesis does not involve treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine.

text: feature_011 and feature_006 are largely independent prognostic biomarkers (their joint effects are approximately additive on the log-odds scale).
rationale: Concerns additivity of feature_011 and feature_006, none of which appear in the ground-truth variable set.

text: After adjustment for feature_057, feature_035, feature_011, and feature_006, the racial groups in feature_005 do NOT differ significantly in objective_response (no adjusted disparity).
rationale: Addresses racial disparity (feature_005), unrelated to the venetoclax+azacitidine subgroup-conditional effect.

text: After adjustment for prognostic factors and feature_078 (likely age), the insurance categories in feature_087 do NOT differ significantly in objective_response.
rationale: Addresses insurance-category disparity, unrelated to treatment, IDH1, NPM1, complex karyotype, or TP53.

text: feature_078 (age, 18-92) shows essentially no main effect on objective_response.
rationale: Concerns the main effect of age (feature_078), which is not part of the ground-truth association.

text: A consolidated multivariable model containing feature_057, feature_035, feature_093, feature_011, feature_006, feature_099, feature_063, feature_092, feature_014, feature_121, feature_125 fits substantially better than a null model and each of these terms remains an independent predictor.
rationale: Although it includes feature_014 and feature_035, it omits feature_027, feature_013, and feature_009, and proposes additive main effects rather than the required subgroup-conditional interaction.

text: feature_125 (5-level ordinal, 0-4) shows a small monotonic decreasing trend in objective_response across levels.
rationale: feature_125 is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: feature_014 (binary, 40% prevalence) is a small independent positive predictor of objective_response.
rationale: Identifies feature_014 with correct positive direction but as an independent main effect, omitting the IDH1/NPM1/complex_karyotype/TP53 subgroup variables and the subgroup-conditional structure.

text: feature_121 (binary, 8% prevalence) is a small independent negative predictor of objective_response.
rationale: feature_121 is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Among low-prevalence binary features (0.5-20% prevalence), no additional binary feature beyond feature_035 and feature_093 reaches strong (p<0.01) independent association with objective_response after adjustment for feature_057, feature_035, feature_093, feature_011, and feature_006.
rationale: Concerns feature_093 and other adjusters not in the ground truth and does not target the treatment-by-mutation subgroup.

text: feature_096, feature_064, feature_045, feature_042 (5-level ordinals) do NOT show significant chi-square association with objective_response.
rationale: These ordinal features are not among the ground-truth variables.

text: The effects of feature_035 and feature_093 on objective_response are approximately additive on the log-odds scale (no significant feature_035 x feature_093 interaction); the highest response rate occurs in patients with both feature_035=1 AND feature_093=1.
rationale: feature_093 is not part of the ground truth, and the hypothesis omits the treatment (feature_014), complex_karyotype, and TP53.

text: feature_093's positive effect attenuates in the highest-risk feature_057 stratum (interaction in the direction of waning benefit at higher feature_057 level), but the interaction is not statistically significant.
rationale: Involves feature_093 and feature_057, neither of which is in the ground truth, and does not address the treatment-by-mutation subgroup.

text: There is no significant race x feature_035 interaction (i.e., the treatment effect of feature_035 does not differ across feature_005 categories).
rationale: Tests a race-by-feature_035 interaction; race is not in the ground truth and the treatment and other subgroup variables are absent.

text: There is no significant insurance x feature_035 interaction; treatment effect of feature_035 is similar across feature_087 categories (though point estimates suggest a slightly larger effect in medicare/medicaid than private).
rationale: Tests an insurance-by-feature_035 interaction; insurance is not in the ground truth and the treatment and other subgroup variables are absent.

text: After adjustment for feature_057, feature_011, and feature_006, exposure to feature_035 increases the marginal probability of objective_response by approximately 5 percentage points (from ~16.5% to ~21.9%).
rationale: Estimates a marginal main effect of feature_035 adjusted for unrelated covariates, omitting the treatment and the full subgroup defining the conditional effect.

text: After adjustment for feature_035, feature_011, and feature_006, moving from feature_057=0 to feature_057=2 lowers the marginal probability of objective_response by approximately 10 percentage points (from ~21.2% to ~11.2%).
rationale: Targets feature_057 as the predictor with covariates feature_035/011/006, missing the venetoclax_azacitidine treatment variable and the NPM1/complex_karyotype/TP53 subgroup conjunction.

text: feature_011 and feature_006 are independent of feature_057 (their distributions do not differ across feature_057 strata), supporting their interpretation as separate prognostic axes rather than surrogates for feature_057.
rationale: Tests independence among feature_011, feature_006, and feature_057 rather than the venetoclax+azacitidine effect within the IDH1/NPM1/non-complex/TP53-wildtype subgroup.

text: Patients with idh1_mutation=1 have a higher objective_response rate than patients with idh1_mutation=0.
rationale: Single-variable main effect of IDH1 alone; misses the required 5-way subgroup-conditional interaction with treatment, NPM1, complex karyotype, and TP53.

text: Patients with npm1_mutation=1 have a higher objective_response rate than patients with npm1_mutation=0 (NPM1 is a favorable AML marker).
rationale: Single-variable main effect of NPM1 alone; does not invoke treatment or the IDH1/karyotype/TP53 conjunction required by the spec.

text: Patients with tp53_mutation=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with tp53_mutation=0 (TP53 is an unfavorable marker).
rationale: Single-variable main effect of TP53; does not capture the subgroup-conditional treatment effect involving the other four variables.

text: Patients with complex_karyotype=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with complex_karyotype=0.
rationale: Single-variable main effect of complex karyotype; misses the treatment and IDH1/NPM1/TP53 conditioning required.

text: Patients with flt3_itd=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with flt3_itd=0.
rationale: References flt3_itd, which is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Patients with idh2_mutation=1 have a higher objective_response rate than patients with idh2_mutation=0.
rationale: References idh2_mutation, which is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Patients with secondary_aml=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with secondary_aml=0.
rationale: References secondary_aml, which is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Patients with unfit_for_intensive=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with unfit_for_intensive=0.
rationale: References unfit_for_intensive, which is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine=1 is associated with higher objective_response than treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine=0.
rationale: Marginal main effect of venetoclax+azacitidine without conditioning on the IDH1+/NPM1+/non-complex/TP53-wt subgroup; misses the subgroup-conditional functional form.

text: treatment_7plus3=1 is associated with higher objective_response than treatment_7plus3=0.
rationale: References treatment_7plus3 rather than the venetoclax+azacitidine arm and omits the required mutation/karyotype subgroup.

text: treatment_midostaurin=1 is associated with higher objective_response than treatment_midostaurin=0.
rationale: Targets midostaurin rather than venetoclax+azacitidine and proposes a main effect, not the IDH1/NPM1/karyotype/TP53 subgroup.

text: treatment_gilteritinib=1 is associated with higher objective_response than treatment_gilteritinib=0.
rationale: Targets gilteritinib rather than venetoclax+azacitidine and proposes a main effect, not the spec's subgroup-conditional relationship.

text: treatment_ivosidenib=1 is associated with higher objective_response than treatment_ivosidenib=0.
rationale: Targets ivosidenib rather than venetoclax+azacitidine and proposes a main effect, not the four-way mutational subgroup interaction.

text: treatment_enasidenib=1 is associated with higher objective_response than treatment_enasidenib=0.
rationale: Targets enasidenib rather than venetoclax+azacitidine and proposes a main effect, not the spec's subgroup-conditional relationship.

text: Among patients with flt3_itd=1, treatment_midostaurin=1 produces a larger increase in objective_response than the same treatment among flt3_itd=0 patients (positive treatment_midostaurin x flt3_itd interaction on the log-odds scale).
rationale: Wrong treatment (midostaurin) and wrong moderator (flt3_itd) â€” the spec involves venetoclax+azacitidine within an IDH1/NPM1/karyotype/TP53 subgroup.

text: Among patients with flt3_itd=1, treatment_gilteritinib=1 produces a larger increase in objective_response than the same treatment among flt3_itd=0 patients (positive treatment_gilteritinib x flt3_itd interaction).
rationale: Wrong treatment (gilteritinib) and wrong moderator (flt3_itd); does not address the venetoclax+azacitidine subgroup association.

text: Among patients with flt3_tkd=1, treatment_midostaurin=1 produces a larger increase in objective_response than among flt3_tkd=0 patients (positive interaction).
rationale: Wrong treatment (midostaurin) and wrong moderator (flt3_tkd); unrelated to the venetoclax+azacitidine four-mutation subgroup effect.

text: Among patients with flt3_tkd=1, treatment_gilteritinib=1 produces a larger increase in objective_response than among flt3_tkd=0 patients (positive interaction).
rationale: Wrong treatment (gilteritinib) and wrong moderator (flt3_tkd); does not target the venetoclax+azacitidine subgroup.

text: Among patients with idh1_mutation=1, treatment_ivosidenib=1 produces a larger increase in objective_response than among idh1_mutation=0 patients (positive treatment_ivosidenib x idh1_mutation interaction). This is the canonical biomarker-matched indication.
rationale: Although it invokes idh1_mutation, the treatment is ivosidenib, not venetoclax+azacitidine, and it omits npm1, complex_karyotype, and tp53.

text: Among patients with idh2_mutation=1, treatment_enasidenib=1 produces a larger increase in objective_response than among idh2_mutation=0 patients (positive treatment_enasidenib x idh2_mutation interaction). Canonical biomarker-matched indication.
rationale: Wrong treatment (enasidenib) and wrong biomarker (idh2_mutation); none of the spec's four mutational predicates or venetoclax+azacitidine are involved.

text: Off-target check: treatment_ivosidenib does NOT produce a larger increase in objective_response among idh2_mutation=1 vs idh2_mutation=0 patients (no interaction expected).
rationale: Off-target check involving ivosidenib and idh2_mutation, neither of which is in the ground-truth variable set.

text: Off-target check: treatment_enasidenib does NOT produce a larger increase in objective_response among idh1_mutation=1 vs idh1_mutation=0 patients.
rationale: Off-target check involving enasidenib, which is not the ground-truth treatment (venetoclax_azacitidine).

text: Among patients with unfit_for_intensive=1, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine=1 yields a larger increase in objective_response than among unfit_for_intensive=0 patients (positive ven/aza x unfit interaction; ven/aza is the standard for unfit patients).
rationale: Uses ven/aza but conditions on unfit_for_intensive rather than the IDH1/NPM1/complex_karyotype/TP53 conjunction.

text: Among patients with unfit_for_intensive=1, treatment_7plus3=1 yields a SMALLER increase (or harm) in objective_response than among fit patients (negative 7+3 x unfit interaction; intensive chemo is poorly tolerated in unfit patients).
rationale: Concerns 7+3 Ã— unfit_for_intensive, not the ven/aza Ã— genetic-conjunction effect.

text: The benefit of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine on objective_response is larger in older patients (age>=75) than in patients <60.
rationale: Uses ven/aza but conditions on age, not on the IDH1/NPM1/complex_karyotype/TP53 subgroup.

text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with lower objective_response (negative coefficient on ecog_ps in adjusted logistic regression).
rationale: Tests a main effect of ECOG, none of the ground-truth variables.

text: Older age_years is associated with lower objective_response, after adjusting for ECOG and disease biology.
rationale: Tests a main effect of age, not involving ven/aza or the specified mutation conjunction.

text: After adjusting for age, ECOG, secondary AML, TP53, and complex karyotype, npm1_mutation remains a positive predictor of objective_response.
rationale: Tests a main effect of npm1 after adjustment, not the ven/aza-by-(IDH1âˆ§NPM1âˆ§non-complexâˆ§TP53-WT) interaction.

text: Self-identified Black patients have a lower objective_response rate than white patients.
rationale: Concerns race, which is not in the ground-truth variable set.

text: Hispanic patients have a lower objective_response rate than white patients.
rationale: Concerns ethnicity, which is not in the ground-truth variable set.

text: Asian patients have a different objective_response rate than white patients.
rationale: Concerns race/ethnicity, not the treatment-by-mutation subgroup specified.

text: Uninsured patients have a lower objective_response rate than privately insured patients.
rationale: Concerns insurance status, not the specified mutation subgroup or treatment.

text: Patients with rural_residence=1 have a lower objective_response rate than urban patients.
rationale: Concerns rural residence, unrelated to the specified variables.

text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with higher objective_response (positive coefficient).
rationale: Concerns albumin, not the treatment or mutation variables in the ground truth.

text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with lower objective_response.
rationale: Concerns CRP, unrelated to the specified variables.

text: Higher blast_pct_marrow is associated with lower objective_response.
rationale: Concerns marrow blast percentage, not the specified variables.

text: Greater weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with lower objective_response.
rationale: Concerns weight loss, unrelated to the specified variables.

text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with lower objective_response.
rationale: Concerns LDH, unrelated to the specified variables.

text: Higher neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio (nlr) is associated with lower objective_response.
rationale: Concerns NLR, unrelated to the specified variables.

text: Higher wbc_k_per_ul (presenting WBC) is associated with lower objective_response.
rationale: Concerns WBC, unrelated to the specified variables.

text: At least one of the 24 candidate SNPs (snp_rs*) shows an association with objective_response that survives Bonferroni correction (p<0.05/24=0.00208).
rationale: Concerns SNPs (snp_rs*), none of which are the spec variables (treatment, IDH1, NPM1, karyotype, TP53).

text: snp_rs1050828 is positively associated with objective_response (the most significant single SNP in screening).
rationale: Targets a single SNP, not any of the ground-truth variables.

text: In a multivariable logistic model that simultaneously adjusts for ECOG, age, key labs (albumin, CRP, blasts, weight loss), AML mutations, treatments, and race, ecog_ps remains the dominant negative predictor of objective_response.
rationale: Centers on ECOG as dominant negative predictor; does not address the venetoclax+azacitidine effect within the IDH1/NPM1/karyotype/TP53 subgroup.

text: In the same multivariable model, idh1_mutation remains a strong positive predictor of objective_response, independent of treatment and demographic factors.
rationale: Proposes IDH1 as an independent main-effect predictor, explicitly independent of treatment, which does not capture the treatment-by-conjunction subgroup effect.

text: After multivariable adjustment, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine retains a positive coefficient on objective_response.
rationale: Posits a main-effect coefficient for venetoclax+azacitidine; the ground truth is a subgroup-conditional effect, and a plain adjusted main effect does not identify the IDH1/NPM1/non-complex/TP53-wt conjunction.

text: After multivariable adjustment for clinical and biological covariates, Black race retains a negative association with objective_response (i.e., the disparity is not fully explained by measured covariates).
rationale: Concerns race as a residual predictor, unrelated to the spec variables and the specified subgroup.

text: The benefit of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine on objective_response is larger in patients with ECOG 0 (fitter) than in those with ECOG 2 (sicker), in absolute response-rate terms.
rationale: Posits treatment heterogeneity by ECOG, not by the IDH1/NPM1/complex-karyotype/TP53 conjunction specified in the ground truth.

text: Patients with flt3_itd=1 have a different objective_response rate than patients with flt3_itd=0; we expect flt3_itd+ to have lower response (poor-prognosis marker).
rationale: flt3_itd is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Patients with npm1_mutation=1 have a higher objective_response rate than patients with npm1_mutation=0 (favorable-prognosis marker).
rationale: Proposes a main effect of NPM1 alone, not the treatment-by-four-mutation subgroup interaction.

text: Patients with tp53_mutation=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with tp53_mutation=0 (adverse-prognosis marker).
rationale: Proposes a main effect of TP53 alone, not the treatment-by-four-mutation subgroup interaction.

text: Patients with complex_karyotype=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with complex_karyotype=0.
rationale: Proposes a main effect of complex karyotype alone, not the treatment-by-four-mutation subgroup interaction.

text: Patients with secondary_aml=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with secondary_aml=0.
rationale: secondary_aml is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Older age (higher age_years) is associated with lower objective_response.
rationale: age_years is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with lower objective_response.
rationale: ecog_ps is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Patients with idh1_mutation=1 have a different objective_response rate than patients with idh1_mutation=0.
rationale: Proposes a main effect of IDH1 alone, not the treatment-by-four-mutation subgroup interaction.

text: Patients with idh2_mutation=1 have a different objective_response rate than patients with idh2_mutation=0.
rationale: idh2_mutation is not among the ground-truth variables (the truth involves IDH1, not IDH2).

text: Patients flagged unfit_for_intensive=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with unfit_for_intensive=0.
rationale: unfit_for_intensive is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Among flt3_itd+ patients, those receiving treatment_midostaurin=1 have a higher objective_response rate than flt3_itd+ patients not receiving midostaurin (targeted-therapy benefit).
rationale: Targets midostaurin and FLT3-ITD subgroup; uses different treatment and different mutation variables than the ground-truth ven/aza Ã— IDH1/NPM1/karyotype/TP53 association.

text: Among flt3_itd+ patients, those receiving treatment_gilteritinib=1 have a higher objective_response rate than flt3_itd+ patients not receiving gilteritinib.
rationale: Targets gilteritinib in FLT3-ITD+ subgroup; wrong treatment and wrong defining mutations relative to the ven/aza ground truth.

text: Among idh1_mutation+ patients, those receiving treatment_ivosidenib=1 have a higher objective_response rate than idh1+ patients not receiving ivosidenib.
rationale: Uses ivosidenib (not venetoclax+azacitidine) and an IDH1-only subgroup; different treatment column and incomplete subgroup definition.

text: Among idh2_mutation+ patients, those receiving treatment_enasidenib=1 have a higher objective_response rate than idh2+ patients not receiving enasidenib.
rationale: Targets enasidenib in IDH2+ patients; wrong treatment and wrong mutation variable (IDH2 rather than IDH1/NPM1/karyotype/TP53).

text: Among flt3_tkd+ patients, treatment_gilteritinib=1 raises objective_response.
rationale: Targets gilteritinib in FLT3-TKD+ subgroup; wrong treatment and unrelated mutation variable.

text: Among unfit_for_intensive=1 patients, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine=1 increases objective_response relative to unfit patients not receiving venetoclax/azacitidine.
rationale: Uses correct treatment and outcome but defines the subgroup by unfit_for_intensive rather than the IDH1/NPM1/non-complex-karyotype/TP53-wildtype conjunction in the spec.

text: Among unfit_for_intensive=1 patients, treatment_7plus3=1 reduces objective_response (intensive chemo is harmful in unfit patients).
rationale: Targets 7+3 chemotherapy rather than venetoclax+azacitidine, and uses an unrelated subgroup variable.

text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has a positive main effect on objective_response after adjusting for age and ECOG.
rationale: Proposes only a main effect of ven/aza adjusted for age/ECOG, with no interaction or subgroup conditioning on IDH1/NPM1/karyotype/TP53 â€” cannot recover the subgroup-conditional relationship.

text: treatment_7plus3 has a positive main effect on objective_response after adjusting for age and ECOG.
rationale: Concerns 7+3 rather than venetoclax+azacitidine and proposes only a main effect; wrong treatment and wrong functional form.

text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with lower objective_response.

text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months (positive slope).

text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).

text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).

text: Higher fatigue_grade is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).

text: PFS differs across insurance_type categories (one-way ANOVA on pfs_months by insurance_type).

text: PFS differs across race_ethnicity categories (one-way ANOVA on pfs_months by race_ethnicity).

text: Higher education_years is associated with longer pfs_months (positive slope).

text: snp_rs1065852 (CYP2D6*10 proxy) modifies the PFS effect of treatment_tamoxifen (non-zero interaction in OLS).

text: snp_rs4244285 (CYP2C19*2 proxy) modifies the PFS effect of treatment_palbociclib (non-zero interaction).

text: snp_rs1801133 (MTHFR C677T proxy) is associated with pfs_months on its own (non-zero OLS slope).
rationale: Tests MTHFR SNP main effect on PFS, unrelated to palbociclib subgroup.

text: snp_rs429358 (APOE proxy) is associated with pfs_months on its own (non-zero OLS slope).
rationale: Tests APOE SNP main effect on PFS, unrelated to palbociclib subgroup.

text: snp_rs1800629 (TNF-alpha promoter proxy) modifies the PFS effect of treatment_pembrolizumab (non-zero interaction).
rationale: Concerns pembrolizumab and a TNF SNP interaction, not palbociclib or the specified subgroup.

text: Patients with diabetes_mellitus = 1 have shorter pfs_months than non-diabetics.
rationale: Tests diabetes vs PFS, unrelated to the palbociclib subgroup association.

text: Patients with chronic_kidney_disease = 1 have shorter pfs_months.
rationale: Concerns chronic_kidney_disease, none of the spec's variables (palbociclib, ER, HER2, PIK3CA, Ki67).

text: Patients with heart_failure = 1 have shorter pfs_months.
rationale: Concerns heart_failure, unrelated to the spec's variable set.

text: Patients with interstitial_lung_disease_history = 1 have shorter pfs_months.
rationale: Concerns interstitial lung disease history, unrelated to the spec's variable set.

text: Patients with prior_chemotherapy = 1 have shorter pfs_months than chemo-naive patients.
rationale: Concerns prior_chemotherapy, not the palbociclib subgroup association.

text: More prior_lines_of_therapy is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
rationale: Concerns prior_lines_of_therapy main effect, unrelated to the spec's variable set.

text: Higher years_since_diagnosis is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
rationale: Concerns years_since_diagnosis main effect, unrelated to the spec's variable set.

text: After adjusting for stage_iv, has_brain_mets, ecog_ps, age_years, ldh_u_l, albumin_g_dl, er_positive, her2_positive, and prior_lines_of_therapy, treatment_palbociclib retains a positive, significant association with pfs_months.
rationale: Proposes a main-effect adjusted OLS that omits pik3ca_mutation and ki67_pct and uses no subgroup/interaction structure, so it cannot recover the subgroup-conditional data-generating process.

text: After adjustment in the same multivariable OLS, ecog_ps, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, ldh_u_l, and prior_lines_of_therapy each retain an independent negative association with pfs_months, while albumin_g_dl and er_positive retain independent positive associations.
rationale: Targets independent associations of comorbidity/staging covariates, not the palbociclib subgroup effect.

text: The PFS benefit of treatment_palbociclib differs by stage_iv (non-zero interaction term in OLS of pfs_months on treatment_palbociclib * stage_iv adjusted for ecog_ps and age_years).
rationale: Tests a palbociclib Ã— stage_iv interaction; stage_iv is not among the spec's subgroup-defining variables (ER, HER2, PIK3CA, Ki67).

text: Within er_positive=1 patients, treatment_palbociclib still extends pfs_months relative to those not on palbo (positive simple effect in stratified t-test).
rationale: Stratifies only on er_positive, omitting her2_positive, pik3ca_mutation, and ki67_pct, so it does not isolate the spec's conjunctive subgroup.

text: Higher values of feature_078 (continuous, range 30-90, mean ~65) are associated with longer pfs_months.
rationale: feature_078 is not among the ground-truth variables and the hypothesis does not involve regorafenib or the subgroup.

text: Higher levels of the 3-level ordinal feature_057 (0/1/2) are associated with shorter pfs_months (monotonic decrease).
rationale: feature_057 is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Patients with feature_051=1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with feature_051=0.
rationale: feature_051 is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Patients with feature_038=1 have longer pfs_months than patients with feature_038=0.
rationale: Identifies regorafenib's positive effect on PFS but proposes a marginal main effect, not the subgroup-conditional relationship restricted to left-sided, KRAS/BRAF-wt, low-CEA patients.

text: Higher feature_092 (continuous, mean ~3.8) is associated with longer pfs_months.
rationale: feature_092 is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Higher feature_099 (continuous, mean ~3.8) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
rationale: feature_099 is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: All six top features (feature_078, feature_057, feature_051, feature_038, feature_092, feature_099) retain their direction and significance after mutual adjustment in a single OLS model.
rationale: OLS over unrelated features omits feature_043/013/109/009 and the regorafenib subgroup interaction.

text: The negative effect of higher feature_057 levels on pfs_months is modified by feature_051 status (interaction).
rationale: Interaction between two unrelated features; does not involve regorafenib or any subgroup-defining variable.

text: The slope of feature_078 on pfs_months is attenuated at higher feature_057 levels (interaction).
rationale: Interaction between two unrelated features; does not involve regorafenib or the ground-truth subgroup.

text: The benefit of feature_038 on pfs_months is modified by feature_051 status (interaction).
rationale: Includes regorafenib but pairs it with feature_051, which is not part of the defining subgroup (sidedness, KRAS, BRAF, CEA).

text: Mean pfs_months differs across categories of feature_064 (race: white/hispanic/black/asian/other) marginally.
rationale: Targets race (feature_064), not any of the ground-truth variables.

text: Mean pfs_months differs across categories of feature_018 (insurance: medicare/medicaid/private/uninsured) marginally.
rationale: Targets insurance (feature_018), not any of the ground-truth variables.

text: After adjustment for the top-six clinical features, race (feature_064) is still associated with pfs_months.
rationale: Adjusted race effect is unrelated to the regorafenib subgroup association.

text: After adjustment for the top-six clinical features, insurance (feature_018) is still associated with pfs_months.
rationale: Adjusted insurance effect is unrelated to the regorafenib subgroup association.

text: The benefit of feature_038 differs across racial categories (interaction).
rationale: Regorafenib Ã— race interaction does not involve sidedness, KRAS, BRAF, or CEA.

text: The benefit of feature_038 differs across insurance categories (interaction).
rationale: Regorafenib Ã— insurance interaction does not involve the ground-truth subgroup variables.

text: Among the 30 continuous features not yet in the model, at least one has an independent (adjusted) association with pfs_months at Bonferroni significance.
rationale: Generic main-effect screen for continuous features cannot capture the four-way subgroup-conditional regorafenib effect.

text: feature_009 (heavy-tailed continuous, range 0.02-777) is independently associated with shorter pfs_months after adjustment.
rationale: Proposes a marginal CEA main effect on PFS, not the regorafenib benefit conditional on low CEA together with left-sided/KRAS-wt/BRAF-wt.

text: feature_006 (continuous, mean ~224) is independently associated with shorter pfs_months after adjustment.
rationale: feature_006 is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Among the ~80 binary features not yet in the model, at least one has an independent association with pfs_months at Bonferroni-adjusted significance.
rationale: Main-effect screen of binary features cannot identify the regorafenib subgroup-conditional effect.

text: feature_013 is associated with shorter pfs_months after adjustment for the top six features and feature_009/feature_006.
rationale: Proposes a main effect of KRAS only; does not involve treatment_038 or the joint subgroup.

text: feature_043 is associated with shorter pfs_months after adjustment.
rationale: Main effect of sidedness only; misses treatment and the conjunctive subgroup structure.

text: feature_109 is associated with shorter pfs_months after adjustment.
rationale: Main effect of BRAF only; misses treatment and the joint subgroup.

text: feature_067 is associated with longer pfs_months after adjustment.
rationale: feature_067 is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: feature_013 modifies the effect of feature_038 on pfs_months: the benefit of feature_038 is smaller (or negative) when feature_013=1 than when feature_013=0.
rationale: Proposes only a 038Ã—013 interaction; omits feature_043, feature_109, and feature_009 from the joint subgroup.

text: feature_043 modifies the effect of feature_038 on pfs_months: the benefit of feature_038 is smaller (or negative) when feature_043=1 than when feature_043=0.
rationale: Proposes only a 038Ã—043 interaction; omits feature_013, feature_109, and feature_009.

text: feature_013 and feature_043 have a positive (sub-additive) interaction on pfs_months.
rationale: Posits a 013Ã—043 interaction on pfs without treatment_038 and omits feature_109 and feature_009.

text: feature_038's benefit is essentially confined to the double-negative (feature_013=0 AND feature_043=0) stratum.
rationale: Captures only two of the four required predicate variables; omits BRAF (109) and CEA (009), so it does not identify the same joint subgroup.

text: Within the biomarker-WT (feature_013=0 AND feature_043=0) stratum, feature_038 prevalence differs across racial categories (allocation/access).
rationale: Concerns treatment allocation by race, not the regorafenibÃ—subgroup PFS effect; race is not in the ground truth.

text: Within the biomarker-WT stratum, the magnitude of the feature_038 PFS benefit differs across racial categories (effect modification).
rationale: Proposes effect modification by race, which is not part of the ground-truth association and omits BRAF and CEA predicates.

text: Within the biomarker-WT stratum, feature_038 prevalence differs across insurance categories.
rationale: Concerns prevalence of feature_038 by insurance, not the PFS outcome relationship.

text: Within the biomarker-WT stratum, the magnitude of the feature_038 PFS benefit differs across insurance categories.
rationale: Proposes insurance as the effect modifier, not the spec's left-sided/low-CEA subgroup.

text: Within the biomarker-WT stratum, the magnitude of the feature_038 PFS benefit is similar across feature_057 (PS) levels (no effect modification).
rationale: Tests PS as effect modifier and predicts no modification, not the spec's subgroup-conditional effect.

text: Within the biomarker-WT stratum, the magnitude of the feature_038 PFS benefit is greater in feature_051=1 patients than in feature_051=0 patients.
rationale: Proposes feature_051 as the modifier; feature_051 is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Within the biomarker-WT stratum, the magnitude of the feature_038 PFS benefit increases with feature_078.
rationale: Proposes feature_078 as the modifier; feature_078 is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: The relationship between feature_078 and pfs_months is nonlinear (quadratic component significant after adjustment).
rationale: Concerns nonlinearity of feature_078, which is not a ground-truth variable.

text: Log(1+feature_009) is a better-fitting predictor of pfs_months than untransformed feature_009.
rationale: Proposes a main-effect log transform of CEA, not a subgroup-conditional regorafenib effect.

text: Log(1+feature_099) is a better-fitting predictor of pfs_months than untransformed feature_099.
rationale: Concerns feature_099, which is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: An integrated model with feature_078 (quadratic), feature_057, feature_051, feature_092, log_f099, log_f009, feature_006, feature_109, feature_067, feature_013, feature_043, and feature_038 with biomarker interactions explains > 89% of pfs_months variance.
rationale: Includes feature_038, feature_043, feature_013, feature_109, and log_f009 with biomarker interactions, an interaction-aware model that could capture the conjunctive subgroup effect.

text: After accounting for feature_038 x feature_013 and feature_038 x feature_043 interactions, feature_013 and feature_043 main effects on pfs_months collapse to near-zero, indicating these are predictive (not prognostic) biomarkers.
rationale: Only models feature_038 interactions with KRAS and sidedness, omitting BRAF and CEA predicates needed for the four-way conjunctive subgroup.

text: Within the biomarker double-positive (feature_013=1 AND feature_043=1) stratum, feature_038 has a net negative effect on pfs_months (apparent harm).
rationale: The hypothesis targets the opposite subgroup (KRAS-mutant and right-sided) with a negative effect, whereas the ground truth specifies a positive regorafenib effect in left-sided, KRAS-wildtype, BRAF-wildtype, low-CEA patients.

text: Among the 80 binary features, at least one has a strong main-effect association with pfs_months (mean PFS differs between feature=1 vs feature=0 patients).
rationale: Proposes a generic main-effect screen across binary features rather than the specific 4-way subgroup-conditional effect of feature_038 on pfs_months.

text: feature_051 (binary) is associated with shorter pfs_months: patients with feature_051=1 have lower mean PFS than feature_051=0.
rationale: Targets feature_051, which is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: feature_038 (binary) is associated with longer pfs_months: patients with feature_038=1 have higher mean PFS than feature_038=0.
rationale: Identifies feature_038 with the correct positive direction but proposes only a marginal main effect and omits the conditioning variables (feature_043, feature_013, feature_109, feature_009) that define the true subgroup.

text: feature_078 (continuous, range 30-90, plausibly age in years) is monotonically associated with pfs_months (Spearman correlation different from 0).
rationale: Targets feature_078, which is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: feature_057 (ordinal, 3 levels) shows monotonically decreasing mean pfs_months from level 0 to level 2, consistent with a stage-like risk variable.
rationale: Targets feature_057, which is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: feature_092 (continuous, range 1.5-5.5, plausibly serum albumin) is positively correlated with pfs_months; feature_099 (continuous, range 0-24.6) is negatively correlated with pfs_months.
rationale: Targets feature_092 and feature_099, neither of which is among the ground-truth variables.

text: Race (feature_064) and insurance (feature_018) have no detectable main-effect association with mean pfs_months.
rationale: Targets race and insurance features, which are not among the ground-truth variables.

text: In a multivariable OLS model on pfs_months adjusting for feature_078, feature_057 (categorical), feature_051, feature_038, feature_092, feature_099, each of those predictors retains an independent, significant association in the same direction as in univariate analysis.
