rationale: Includes feature_038 in a main-effect OLS but omits the subgroup-defining variables (feature_043, feature_013, feature_109, feature_009) and proposes an additive linear model that cannot capture the 4-way conjunction.

text: Together feature_078, feature_057, feature_051, feature_038, feature_092, feature_099 (and a few other binaries) explain a large fraction of variance in pfs_months (R^2 > 0.5).
rationale: Proposes a variance-explained claim with a covariate set missing the conditioning variables (feature_043, feature_013, feature_109, feature_009) and does not investigate a subgroup-conditional effect.

text: Receipt of feature_038 (the apparent beneficial treatment) differs by race (feature_064): Black, Hispanic, Asian, and Other patients have lower prevalence of feature_038=1 than white patients.
rationale: The outcome here is treatment receipt by race, not pfs_months, so it does not investigate the ground-truth association.

text: Stage distribution (feature_057) and prevalence of the negative-prognostic feature_051 differ across race and insurance categories.
rationale: Concerns demographics/insurance vs stage and a prognostic feature; does not involve feature_038 or pfs_months as outcome.

text: After adjustment for feature_078, feature_057, feature_051, and feature_038, race (feature_064) and insurance (feature_018) remain non-significantly associated with pfs_months.
rationale: Tests race/insurance association with pfs_months; not the regorafenib subgroup effect on the four specified subgroup variables.

text: The PFS benefit of feature_038 differs across stages (feature_057 levels), with smaller absolute benefit at more advanced stages: feature_038 x feature_057 interaction is significant.
rationale: Proposes a feature_038 x stage (feature_057) interaction; stage is not among the subgroup-defining variables (feature_043/013/109/009).

text: feature_038's PFS benefit is similar in patients with feature_051=1 vs feature_051=0 (no feature_038 x feature_051 interaction).
rationale: Tests interaction of feature_038 with feature_051; feature_051 is not part of the ground-truth subgroup.

text: The PFS benefit of feature_038 (treatment-like marker) differs by race: at least one race x feature_038 interaction term is significant after adjustment.
rationale: Proposes race x feature_038 interaction; race is not among the subgroup-defining variables.

text: The PFS benefit of feature_038 differs by insurance type (medicaid/medicare/private/uninsured).
rationale: Proposes insurance x feature_038 interaction; insurance is not among the subgroup-defining variables.

text: feature_092 (positive prognostic, albumin-like) modifies the PFS benefit of feature_038: in multivariable OLS, the feature_092 x feature_038 interaction is non-zero.
rationale: Proposes feature_092 x feature_038 interaction; feature_092 is not among the subgroup-defining variables.

text: After adjusting for the top covariates, no further binary feature beyond feature_013, feature_043, feature_109, feature_067 retains a clinically meaningful (|effect|>0.1 month) association with pfs_months.
rationale: Asserts only main effects of feature_013/043/109/067 matter; does not propose the subgroup-conditional regorafenib effect (omits feature_038 and feature_009 and the conjunction).

text: feature_099 (negative prognostic continuous) has the same per-unit slope on pfs_months across all 3 levels of feature_057 (no feature_099 x feature_057 interaction).
rationale: Tests feature_099 x feature_057 interaction; involves neither feature_038 nor any subgroup-defining variable.

text: Patients receiving feature_038 (treatment) experience a smaller per-unit harm from feature_099: feature_099 x feature_038 interaction is positive (toward zero from a negative main slope).
rationale: Proposes feature_099 x feature_038 interaction; feature_099 is not among the subgroup-defining variables (feature_043/013/109/009).

text: feature_007 (binary, prevalence ~45% — a balanced binary, plausibly sex) is associated with pfs_months either as a main effect or via interaction with feature_038.
rationale: feature_007 is not part of the ground-truth subgroup variables and a main-effect or single-interaction hypothesis with feature_038 does not capture the four-way conjunction subgroup.

text: The final adjusted multivariable model (feature_078 + C(feature_057) + feature_051 + feature_038 + feature_013 + feature_043 + feature_109 + feature_092 + feature_099 + feature_006) achieves R^2 > 0.85.
rationale: A global multivariable R^2 claim does not test the subgroup-conditional effect of regorafenib within the left-sided/KRAS-wt/BRAF-wt/low-CEA conjunction.

text: Sensitivity check: stratifying the final model by feature_064 (race), the coefficient on feature_038 remains significantly positive in every race stratum.
rationale: Stratifying by race (feature_064) does not isolate the ground-truth subgroup defined by sidedness, KRAS, BRAF, and CEA.

text: feature_071 (an 11-level ordinal 0-10, plausibly performance status or similar) is not associated with pfs_months after adjustment, contrary to clinical expectation.
rationale: feature_071 is unrelated to the ground-truth variables and the hypothesis does not address regorafenib benefit within the specified subgroup.

text: At least one binary feature in the dataset is associated with mean pfs_months at p<0.05 (univariate Welch t-test screen across all 80 binary features).
rationale: A univariate binary screen tests main effects on PFS and would not identify the 5-way subgroup-conditional regorafenib benefit specified by the ground truth.

text: At least one continuous feature in the dataset is correlated with pfs_months at p<0.05 (univariate Pearson correlation screen across the 34 continuous features).
rationale: A univariate Pearson correlation on continuous features tests main effects only and cannot capture the subgroup-conditional interaction involving regorafenib, sidedness, KRAS, BRAF, and CEA.

text: At least one ordinal feature in the dataset is associated with pfs_months (univariate Spearman correlation across 7 small-cardinality integer features).
rationale: A Spearman screen across ordinal features does not target the regorafenibÃ—sidednessÃ—KRASÃ—BRAFÃ—CEA subgroup and tests only marginal monotonic associations.

text: Higher feature_078 (continuous, range 30-90, mean 65) is associated with longer pfs_months in a roughly linear fashion (linear OLS slope > 0).
rationale: feature_078 is not among the ground-truth variables and the hypothesis describes an unrelated linear main effect.

text: Patients with feature_051 = 1 (prevalence ~55%) have shorter mean pfs_months than patients with feature_051 = 0, by about one month.
rationale: feature_051 is not among the ground-truth variables (treatment_regorafenib, sidedness, KRAS, BRAF, CEA).

text: Patients with feature_038 = 1 (prevalence ~20%) have longer mean pfs_months than feature_038 = 0 patients, by about one month.
rationale: Although it names regorafenib (feature_038) with the correct direction, it claims a marginal main effect of ~1 month rather than the subgroup-conditional effect restricted to left-sided KRAS/BRAF-wildtype low-CEA patients, and omits feature_043/013/109/009.

text: feature_057 (0/1/2) shows a monotonic dose-response negative association with pfs_months: each higher level reduces mean PFS by ~1 month.
rationale: feature_057 is not among the ground-truth variables and the hypothesis posits an unrelated ordinal main effect.

text: Higher feature_092 (continuous, range 1.5-5.5, mean 3.8) is associated with longer pfs_months (positive slope).
rationale: feature_092 is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Higher feature_099 (continuous, range 0-25) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
rationale: feature_099 is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: The top eight univariate predictors (feature_078, feature_051, feature_038, feature_057, feature_092, feature_099, feature_013, feature_043, feature_109, feature_009, feature_006) each retain independent associations with pfs_months in a multivariable OLS model (each |t|>5).
rationale: A multivariable OLS without interaction terms tests independent main effects and cannot recover the 5-way subgroup-conditional regorafenib benefit; the hypothesis explicitly predicts main-effect significance, not the conjunction.

text: A multivariable OLS regression on these top features explains a large share of variance in pfs_months (R-squared > 0.80).
rationale: A global OLS R-squared claim does not identify the subgroup-conditional regorafenib benefit defined by the four predicates.

text: After fitting the multivariable model on the top features, the remaining ~110 features add little additional explanatory power (kitchen-sink R-squared barely exceeds the top-feature model R-squared).
rationale: A claim about residual explanatory power of other features does not target the specified variables or the subgroup-conditional relationship.

text: Mean pfs_months differs across the five categories of feature_064 (race: white, hispanic, black, asian, other) -- one-way ANOVA p<0.05.
rationale: Race (feature_064) is not among the ground-truth variables and the test ignores regorafenib and the subgroup predicates.

text: Mean pfs_months differs across the four categories of feature_018 (insurance: medicare, private, medicaid, uninsured) -- one-way ANOVA p<0.05.
rationale: Insurance (feature_018) is not among the ground-truth variables and the test ignores regorafenib and the subgroup predicates.

text: feature_051's effect on pfs_months is modified by feature_064 (race x feature_051 interaction) -- joint F-test of interaction terms is significant.
rationale: Involves race and feature_051, neither of which is part of the ground-truth variable set.

text: feature_078's effect on pfs_months is modified by feature_064 (race x feature_078 interaction) -- joint F-test is significant.
rationale: Involves race and feature_078, neither of which is part of the ground-truth variable set.

text: feature_038 and feature_013 have a multiplicative interaction on pfs_months: the benefit of feature_038 is attenuated when feature_013=1 versus feature_013=0.
rationale: Captures only a regorafenib-by-KRAS interaction and omits right-sidedness, BRAF, and CEA, so it does not identify the four-way subgroup.

text: feature_038 and feature_043 have a multiplicative interaction on pfs_months: the benefit of feature_038 is attenuated when feature_043=1 versus feature_043=0.
rationale: Captures only a regorafenib-by-sidedness interaction and omits KRAS, BRAF, and CEA, so it does not identify the four-way subgroup.

text: feature_038 and feature_109 have a multiplicative interaction on pfs_months: the benefit of feature_038 is attenuated when feature_109=1 versus feature_109=0.
rationale: Captures only a regorafenib-by-BRAF interaction and omits sidedness, KRAS, and CEA, so it does not identify the four-way subgroup.

text: Treatment-like-feature feature_038 produces a clinically meaningful PFS benefit only in patients negative for all three resistance-marker-like features (feature_013=0 AND feature_043=0 AND feature_109=0); the benefit is essentially zero when any of these is positive.
rationale: Identifies the joint negativity of KRAS, sidedness, and BRAF as gating regorafenib benefit but omits the low-CEA predicate required by the ground-truth subgroup.

text: Restricting to patients negative for both feature_013 and feature_043, feature_038 still produces a >2 mo PFS benefit; in any cell with at least one of the markers positive, the feature_038 effect is approximately zero.
rationale: Identifies only feature_013 and feature_043 as subgroup-defining markers; omits feature_109 (BRAF) and feature_009 (CEA), so the variable set does not match the ground truth.

text: feature_051 is purely prognostic, not predictive: its negative effect on pfs_months is the same in feature_038=0 and feature_038=1 patients (no feature_051 x feature_038 interaction).
rationale: Concerns feature_051, which is not part of the ground-truth variable set.

text: Among the remaining low-prevalence binary features (n1 between 100 and 5000), none produces a positive PFS shift larger than +0.25 mo after the dominant signals are accounted for.
rationale: Targets unspecified low-prevalence binary features rather than the named ground-truth variables.

text: Adding the feature_038 x (NOT any_resistance) interaction term to the multivariable model, the main effects of feature_013, feature_043, feature_109, and feature_038 alone all collapse toward zero, while the interaction coefficient is large and highly significant (>+2.5 mo).
rationale: Names feature_013, feature_043, feature_109 and feature_038 but omits feature_009 (CEA) from the model/subgroup definition, so the variable set is incomplete.

text: Among biomarker-eligible patients (any_resistance=0), feature_038 efficacy varies across feature_057 levels (worse performance status reduces treatment benefit) -- significant treatment x feature_057 interaction within the eligible subgroup.
rationale: Posits a treatment x feature_057 interaction, introducing a variable not in the ground truth.

text: Among biomarker-eligible patients, feature_038 efficacy varies with feature_078 (older patients gain less benefit) -- significant treatment x feature_078 interaction.
rationale: Posits a treatment x feature_078 (age) interaction, a variable not in the ground truth.

text: Receipt of treatment-like feature_038 differs by feature_064 (race) -- chi-square test on the cross-tabulation is significant.
rationale: Tests treatment receipt vs race rather than the effect on pfs_months and uses feature_064, which is not in the ground truth.

text: Receipt of treatment-like feature_038 differs by feature_018 (insurance) -- chi-square test is significant.
rationale: Tests treatment receipt vs insurance rather than the effect on pfs_months and uses feature_018, which is not in the ground truth.

text: Among biomarker-eligible patients (any_res=0), feature_038 receipt differs by race -- a marker of disparity in access to indicated therapy.
rationale: Concerns treatment access by race, not the effect of feature_038 on pfs_months in the specified subgroup.

text: Among biomarker-eligible patients, feature_038 receipt differs by insurance.
rationale: Concerns treatment access by insurance, not the effect of feature_038 on pfs_months in the specified subgroup.

text: Prevalence of any of the resistance markers (feature_013, feature_043, feature_109) differs by race.
rationale: Tests marker prevalence by race, not regorafenib's subgroup-conditional effect on pfs_months.

text: Among biomarker-eligible patients, the magnitude of feature_038's benefit on pfs_months differs across race categories.
rationale: Examines treatment effect heterogeneity by race rather than by the left-sided/KRAS-wt/BRAF-wt/low-CEA molecular subgroup.

text: feature_057's negative effect on pfs_months is uniform across race categories (no feature_057 x feature_064 effect modification).
rationale: Concerns feature_057 and feature_064, none of which are the spec's variables.

text: After adjusting for the dominant 12-term model, no remaining binary feature has a residual effect larger than 0.05 mo with p<0.001.
rationale: A residual-effect screen for binary features after a fixed model does not isolate the specified four-way subgroup-conditional treatment benefit.

text: feature_009 (long-tail continuous, range 0.02-777) shows a stronger linear association with pfs_months on a log-transformed scale than on its raw scale.
rationale: Compares functional forms of CEA's main effect on PFS, not its role as a threshold within the regorafenib-benefit subgroup.

text: A 12-term OLS model containing feature_078, feature_051, feature_057, feature_092, feature_099, feature_009, feature_006, feature_013, feature_043, feature_109, feature_038, and the feature_038*(no-resistance-marker) interaction explains >90% of variance in pfs_months in this 50000-patient cohort.
rationale: The proposed interaction is feature_038 x no-resistance-marker, omitting the CEA<=5 condition, so the model would not capture the four-way conjunction subgroup effect.

text: Across all the disparity-related tests in iterations 4 and 8 (race main effect, insurance main effect, race x feature_051, race x feature_078, treatment receipt by race, treatment receipt by insurance, marker prevalence by race, treatment efficacy by race, treatment efficacy by insurance), none reaches the threshold of clinical or statistical significance.
rationale: Concerns race/insurance disparity tests, unrelated to the regorafenib molecular-subgroup PFS benefit.

text: Higher values of the continuous covariate feature_078 (range 30-90, mean 65, plausibly age or another scalar baseline characteristic) are associated with longer pfs_months.
rationale: feature_078 is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Patients with feature_051 = 1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with feature_051 = 0.
rationale: feature_051 is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Patients with feature_038 = 1 have longer pfs_months than patients with feature_038 = 0 (feature_038 appears treatment-like given its prevalence ~20% and direction of effect on the outcome).
rationale: Proposes a simple main effect of regorafenib on pfs_months and omits the four subgroup-defining variables (feature_043, feature_013, feature_109, feature_009), so it would not identify the subgroup-conditional relationship.

text: Higher levels of the ordinal feature_057 (values 0, 1, 2; modal value 1; plausibly an ECOG-like performance score) are associated with shorter pfs_months in a monotone, decreasing-trend manner.
rationale: feature_057 is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Higher values of the continuous lab-like feature_099 (range 0-24.6, mean 3.84) are associated with shorter pfs_months.
rationale: feature_099 is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Higher values of the continuous lab-like feature_092 (range 1.5-5.5, mean 3.80, plausibly serum albumin) are associated with longer pfs_months.
rationale: feature_092 is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Patients with feature_013 = 1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with feature_013 = 0.
rationale: Proposes a KRAS main effect on pfs_months alone and ignores regorafenib plus the other subgroup predicates, so it would not recover the subgroup-conditional treatment effect.

text: Patients with feature_043 = 1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with feature_043 = 0.
rationale: Proposes a sidedness main effect on pfs_months alone and omits regorafenib and the other subgroup-defining variables.

text: Higher values of the continuous lab-like feature_009 (range 0.02-777, heavy right tail, plausibly a tumor marker such as CEA) are associated with shorter pfs_months.
rationale: Proposes a CEA main effect on pfs_months alone and omits regorafenib plus the other subgroup-defining variables.

text: Patients with the rare binary feature_067 = 1 (~3% prevalence) have longer pfs_months than feature_067 = 0 patients.
rationale: feature_067 is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Patients with the rare binary feature_109 = 1 (~5% prevalence) have shorter pfs_months than feature_109 = 0 patients.
rationale: Proposes a main effect of feature_109 alone on PFS, not the four-way subgroup-conditional treatment effect involving feature_038.

text: Mean pfs_months differs across the five categories of feature_064 (white, hispanic, asian, black, other; plausibly self-reported race/ethnicity).
rationale: feature_064 (race) is not among the ground-truth variables; it tests an unrelated main effect.

text: Mean pfs_months differs across the four categories of feature_018 (medicare, private, uninsured, medicaid; plausibly insurance type).
rationale: feature_018 (insurance) is not among the ground-truth variables; it tests an unrelated main effect.

text: Among patients in feature_064, black patients have shorter pfs_months than white patients (a directional pairwise contrast addressing a clinically relevant disparity question).
rationale: Pairwise race contrast on feature_064 is unrelated to the regorafenib subgroup association.

text: Patients with feature_018 = uninsured have shorter pfs_months than feature_018 = private patients.
rationale: Insurance-status contrast on feature_018 is unrelated to the ground-truth subgroup effect.

text: After adjustment for feature_078, feature_057, feature_092, feature_099, feature_009, feature_064, and feature_018 in a multivariable OLS model, feature_051 remains independently associated with shorter pfs_months and feature_038 remains independently associated with longer pfs_months.
rationale: Tests an additive main effect of feature_038 in OLS rather than the subgroup-conditional interaction defining the ground truth, and includes feature_051 which is not in the spec.

text: The race variable feature_064 and the insurance variable feature_018 are NOT independently associated with pfs_months after adjusting for feature_078, feature_051, feature_038, feature_057, and other top covariates.
rationale: Concerns race/insurance covariates, not the regorafenib Ã— sidedness Ã— KRAS Ã— BRAF Ã— CEA subgroup.

text: The PFS benefit conferred by feature_038 (positive) is modified by the binary biomarker feature_013: patients with feature_013 = 0 derive a larger PFS benefit from feature_038 than patients with feature_013 = 1 (qualitative biomarker × treatment interaction).
rationale: Captures only the feature_038 Ã— feature_013 interaction and omits feature_043, feature_109, and feature_009, so it does not target the full conjunctive subgroup.

text: The PFS benefit conferred by feature_038 is also modified by the binary biomarker feature_043: feature_038 confers a larger PFS gain in feature_043 = 0 patients than in feature_043 = 1 patients.
rationale: Captures only the feature_038 Ã— feature_043 interaction and omits feature_013, feature_109, and feature_009, so it does not target the full conjunctive subgroup.

text: feature_038 effect is NOT modified by feature_051 (i.e., the negative prognostic effect of feature_051 is independent of the feature_038 treatment effect).
rationale: Asserts no interaction with feature_051, which is not part of the ground-truth subgroup variables.

text: A composite biomarker positivity flag (marker_pos = 1 if feature_013 = 1 OR feature_043 = 1; ~62% prevalence) abolishes the feature_038 PFS benefit: feature_038 confers a large PFS gain only in marker_pos = 0 patients (joint biomarker-negative subgroup).
rationale: Identifies only feature_013 and feature_043 in a composite, omitting feature_109 and feature_009 from the true subgroup definition.

text: feature_013 and feature_043 are positively correlated with each other (statistically associated rather than independent), suggesting overlapping biology or shared upstream cause; if so, treating them as a composite resistance signature is justifiable.
rationale: Asserts a marginal correlation between two predictors and does not involve treatment or pfs_months at all.

text: The PFS benefit of feature_038 does NOT vary by patient feature_078 (the strong age-like covariate): treatment effect is constant across the feature_078 range.
rationale: Concerns feature_078 rather than the ground-truth subgroup variables and proposes a null effect.

text: The PFS benefit of feature_038 is reduced (in absolute terms) at higher levels of feature_057 (the ECOG-like ordinal factor): feature_057 × feature_038 interaction is negative.
rationale: Posits an interaction with feature_057, which is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: The probability of receiving feature_038 (presumed therapy) differs by feature_064 (race): non-white patients are less likely than white patients to receive feature_038.
rationale: About treatment-assignment probability by feature_064 (race), not a PFS effect, and uses a non-ground-truth variable.

text: The probability of receiving feature_038 differs by feature_018 (insurance): uninsured/medicaid patients are less likely than privately insured patients to receive feature_038.
rationale: About treatment-assignment probability by feature_018 (insurance), not the PFS subgroup effect.

text: Among patients who receive feature_038, the PFS benefit is similar across feature_064 racial categories (no race × treatment interaction).
rationale: Tests treatment-by-race homogeneity using feature_064, which is not part of the ground-truth subgroup.

text: Among patients who receive feature_038, the PFS benefit is similar across feature_018 insurance categories (no insurance × treatment interaction).
rationale: Tests treatment-by-insurance homogeneity using feature_018, which is not part of the ground-truth subgroup.

text: The composite biomarker × treatment interaction is preserved within feature_051 strata, and within each (marker_pos, feature_051) cell, feature_038 = 1 patients have higher mean pfs_months than feature_038 = 0 patients only when marker_pos = 0.
rationale: Adds feature_051 stratification while still omitting feature_109 and feature_009 from the subgroup definition.

text: A consolidated multivariable OLS that includes feature_078, feature_057, feature_051, feature_092, feature_099, and the feature_038 × marker_pos interaction explains a very large fraction of the variance in pfs_months (R² > 0.85), and within this model the feature_038 main-effect coefficient (treatment benefit at marker_pos = 0) is approximately +2.5 months while the feature_038 × marker_pos interaction is approximately -2.5 months (i.e., total feature_038 effect ≈ 0 at marker_pos = 1).
rationale: OLS uses feature_078/057/051/092/099 with marker_pos and does not involve feature_109 or feature_009 from the true subgroup.

text: feature_051 does not act as a treatment-effect modifier of feature_038 even when conditioned on marker_pos: feature_051 × marker_pos is null.
rationale: The hypothesis concerns feature_051 as an effect modifier of feature_038, not the ground-truth subgroup defined by feature_043, feature_013, feature_109, and feature_009.

text: At least some of the 123 features in ds001_crc are individually associated with pfs_months. A univariate screen will identify a small set of candidate predictors with p < 1e-6 (signed direction to be confirmed).
rationale: A generic univariate screen would not capture a subgroup-conditional interaction across five specific variables, and the spec explicitly notes that none of the involved features individually predicts the benefit.

text: feature_078 (continuous, range 30-90, mean 65, granularity of 0.1) is positively associated with pfs_months: higher feature_078 values predict longer pfs_months in a monotonic dose-response.
rationale: feature_078 is not among the ground-truth variables (treatment_regorafenib, right_sided_primary, kras_mutation, braf_v600e, cea_ng_ml).

text: feature_057 (a 3-level ordinal feature with values 0/1/2) shows monotonic decrease in pfs_months as the level increases, consistent with a stage- or grade-like prognostic variable.
rationale: feature_057 is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: feature_051 (binary, prevalence 0.55) is associated with shorter pfs_months: patients with feature_051=1 have lower mean pfs_months than patients with feature_051=0, independent of feature_078 and feature_057.
rationale: feature_051 is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: feature_038 (binary, prevalence 0.20) is associated with longer pfs_months: patients with feature_038=1 have higher mean pfs_months than patients with feature_038=0, plausibly representing a positive prognostic biomarker.
rationale: Although feature_038 (regorafenib) is involved with the correct positive direction, the hypothesis proposes a main effect and ignores the four conditioning variables (feature_043, feature_013, feature_109, feature_009) that define the subgroup.

text: feature_013 (binary, prevalence 0.42) is associated with shorter pfs_months independent of stage and feature_078.
rationale: Hypothesis is a main-effect claim about feature_013 alone and does not reference regorafenib or the other subgroup-defining variables.

text: feature_043 (binary, prevalence 0.35) is associated with shorter pfs_months independent of stage and feature_078.
rationale: Hypothesis is a main-effect claim about feature_043 alone and does not reference regorafenib or the other subgroup-defining variables.

text: feature_109 (rare binary, prevalence 0.045) is associated with shorter pfs_months independent of other predictors.
rationale: Hypothesis is a main-effect claim about feature_109 alone and does not reference regorafenib or the other subgroup-defining variables.

text: feature_067 (rare binary, prevalence 0.030), which appeared as a positive univariate signal, will lose statistical significance after adjusting for feature_078 and feature_057 — i.e. the univariate effect is confounded.
rationale: feature_067 is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: feature_099 (continuous, range 0-24.6, with 27.5% of values exactly 0) is monotonically negatively associated with pfs_months independent of other predictors.
rationale: feature_099 is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: feature_092 (continuous, narrow range 1.5-5.5, mean 3.80) is positively associated with pfs_months and the relationship is approximately linear (no major curvature).
rationale: Names a different variable (feature_092) and a main-effect linear association unrelated to the regorafenib subgroup interaction.

text: feature_009 (continuous, heavy right-skew, range 0.02-777) is negatively associated with pfs_months — higher values predict shorter pfs_months — consistent with a tumor-burden or biomarker-burden lab.
rationale: Proposes a univariate main effect of CEA only; ignores regorafenib and the joint left-sided/KRAS-wt/BRAF-wt conditioning that defines the ground-truth association.

text: Mean pfs_months differs across racial groups represented in feature_064 (white, hispanic, black, asian, other), with non-white groups having shorter pfs_months than white patients.
rationale: Concerns race (feature_064), unrelated to the regorafenib biomarker subgroup.

text: Mean pfs_months differs across insurance categories represented in feature_018 (medicare, private, medicaid, uninsured), with medicaid and uninsured patients having shorter pfs_months than medicare/private.
rationale: Concerns insurance (feature_018), unrelated to the ground-truth variables.

text: Even if there is no main-effect disparity, race or insurance interacts with stage (feature_057) such that the most disadvantaged subgroups (e.g. uninsured at high stage, black at high stage) have differentially shorter pfs_months.
rationale: Race/insurance Ã— stage interaction; involves none of the ground-truth variables.

text: A multivariable linear regression of pfs_months on feature_078, feature_057 (categorical), feature_051, feature_038, feature_013, feature_043, feature_109, feature_099, feature_092, and feature_009 explains a large share of the variance in pfs_months (R^2 > 0.5).
rationale: Although all five ground-truth variables appear, a plain additive multivariable linear regression is not a method capable of recovering a 4-way conjunction subgroup-conditional treatment effect, and the hypothesis is about overall RÂ² rather than the regorafenib subgroup benefit.

text: Adding additional weak univariate signals (feature_028, feature_070) to the multivariable model does not materially improve fit — i.e. these weak univariate effects are confounded by the strong predictors.
rationale: About marginal contribution of feature_028/feature_070; does not address the regorafenib conjunction subgroup.

text: feature_038 (positive biomarker) interacts with feature_057 (stage): the absolute pfs_months benefit of feature_038=1 attenuates as stage increases.
rationale: Proposes regorafenib Ã— stage interaction, not the left-sided/KRAS-wt/BRAF-wt/low-CEA conjunction.

text: feature_051 and feature_038 have independent additive effects (no statistical interaction) — i.e. the negative impact of feature_051 is not modified by feature_038 status.
rationale: Proposes additivity of feature_051 and regorafenib; does not target the ground-truth subgroup variables or interaction.

text: feature_078 (the dominant predictor) interacts with feature_057 (stage): the positive effect of feature_078 on pfs_months is smaller at higher stages — consistent with severity dampening any prognostic gain.
rationale: Concerns feature_078 Ã— stage; none of the ground-truth subgroup variables (sidedness, KRAS, BRAF, CEA) and not the regorafenib effect.

text: feature_013 (binary, 42% prevalence) interacts with feature_038 (the positive biomarker): the impact of feature_013 differs by feature_038 status, suggesting feature_013 may be a treatment-like or co-condition variable whose effect depends on biomarker status.
rationale: Names only feature_013 and feature_038 and omits the required right_sided_primary, braf_v600e, and cea_ng_ml predicates that define the ground-truth subgroup.

text: Among biomarker-positive patients (feature_038=1), feature_013=1 is associated with substantially shorter pfs_months than feature_013=0, while in biomarker-negative patients (feature_038=0) feature_013 has essentially no association with pfs_months.
rationale: Captures the KRAS-wt direction within regorafenib-treated patients but misses the right_sided_primary, braf_v600e, and cea_ng_ml conjunction needed to identify the true subgroup.

text: Synthesis: a parsimonious 10-feature additive model with the feature_013 x feature_038 interaction included captures essentially all of the systematic variance in pfs_months for which any individual feature contributes >0.05 R^2.
rationale: An additive model with only a feature_013 x feature_038 interaction cannot represent the 5-way conjunction across feature_043, feature_109, feature_009 that drives the effect.

text: Across this cohort, demographic features (race feature_064 and insurance feature_018) contribute no detectable variance in pfs_months once clinical predictors are accounted for — a robust null finding for outcome disparities.
rationale: Concerns demographic features (feature_064, feature_018) and a null disparities finding, unrelated to the regorafenib subgroup association on pfs_months.

text: Patients with stage_iv=1 have shorter mean pfs_months than patients with stage_iv=0.
rationale: Uses stage_iv, which is not among the ground-truth variables, and proposes only a main effect.

text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative monotonic relationship across the ordinal scale).
rationale: Uses ecog_ps, which is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Patients with kras_mutation=1 have shorter mean pfs_months than kras_mutation=0 patients.
rationale: Tests only kras_mutation as a main effect, missing the regorafenib treatment, sidedness, BRAF, and CEA variables and the subgroup-conditional structure.

text: Patients with braf_v600e=1 have shorter mean pfs_months than braf_v600e=0 patients.
rationale: Tests only braf_v600e as a main effect, missing the other ground-truth variables and the subgroup-conditional structure.

text: Patients with msi_high=1 have different (longer) mean pfs_months than msi_high=0 patients.
rationale: Uses msi_high, which is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Patients with her2_amplified=1 have different mean pfs_months than her2_amplified=0 patients.
rationale: Uses her2_amplified, which is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Patients with right_sided_primary=1 have shorter mean pfs_months than right_sided_primary=0 patients.
rationale: Tests only right_sided_primary as a main effect, missing the regorafenib, KRAS, BRAF, and CEA variables and the subgroup structure.

text: treatment_regorafenib=1 patients have different mean pfs_months than treatment_regorafenib=0 patients (overall main effect).
rationale: Proposes only an overall main effect of regorafenib without the sidedness/KRAS/BRAF/CEA subgroup conditioning that the ground truth specifies as essential.

text: treatment_cetuximab=1 patients have different mean pfs_months than treatment_cetuximab=0 patients (overall main effect).
rationale: Uses treatment_cetuximab, not the regorafenib treatment indicated by the ground truth.

text: treatment_bevacizumab=1 patients have different mean pfs_months than treatment_bevacizumab=0 patients (overall main effect).
rationale: Uses treatment_bevacizumab, not the regorafenib treatment indicated by the ground truth.

text: treatment_pembrolizumab=1 patients have different mean pfs_months than treatment_pembrolizumab=0 patients (overall main effect).
rationale: Wrong drug (pembrolizumab) and wrong relationship type (main effect vs. subgroup-conditional regorafenib effect).

text: treatment_encorafenib=1 patients have different mean pfs_months than treatment_encorafenib=0 patients (overall main effect).
rationale: Wrong drug (encorafenib) and wrong relationship type (main effect).

text: treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib=1 patients have different mean pfs_months than treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib=0 patients (overall main effect).
rationale: Wrong drug (trastuzumab/tucatinib) and wrong relationship type (main effect).

text: Among RAS-wildtype patients (kras_mutation=0 AND nras_mutation=0), treatment_cetuximab=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than treatment_cetuximab=0; the cetuximab×RAS-status interaction is positive.
rationale: Wrong drug (cetuximab) and wrong subgroup variables (RAS status rather than the regorafenib left-sided/KRAS-wt/BRAF-wt/low-CEA conjunction).

text: Among msi_high=1 patients, treatment_pembrolizumab=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than treatment_pembrolizumab=0; pembrolizumab×MSI interaction is positive.
rationale: Wrong drug (pembrolizumab) and wrong subgroup variable (MSI status).

text: Among braf_v600e=1 patients, treatment_encorafenib=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than treatment_encorafenib=0; encorafenib×BRAF interaction is positive.
rationale: Wrong drug (encorafenib) and the BRAF interaction is in the opposite direction of the ground-truth subgroup, which requires BRAF-wildtype.

text: Among her2_amplified=1 patients, treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib=0; the interaction is positive.
rationale: Wrong drug (trastuzumab/tucatinib) and wrong subgroup variable (HER2 amplification).

text: The +0.97-month regorafenib main effect on pfs_months persists after multivariable adjustment for prognostic covariates (age, ECOG, stage_iv, biomarkers, labs).
rationale: Names regorafenib but proposes a main-effect-with-adjustment relationship rather than the subgroup-conditional effect, and does not identify the right_sided/KRAS/BRAF/CEA subgroup.

text: The regorafenib benefit on pfs_months differs between stage_iv=1 and stage_iv=0 (interaction).
rationale: Right drug but wrong moderator (stage_iv) â€” does not identify the right_sided_primary/KRAS/BRAF/CEA subgroup.

text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months (positive correlation).
rationale: Concerns albumin and PFS â€” none of the ground-truth variables (regorafenib, sidedness, KRAS, BRAF, CEA) are involved.

text: Greater weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
rationale: Uses weight_loss_pct_6mo, which is not among the ground-truth variables, and does not address the regorafenib subgroup effect.

text: Higher cea_ng_ml is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
rationale: Proposes a main-effect association of CEA with PFS rather than the regorafenib treatment benefit conditional on left-sided/KRAS-wt/BRAF-wt/low-CEA, and omits treatment_regorafenib, right_sided_primary, kras_mutation, and braf_v600e.

text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
rationale: Involves ldh_u_l, which is not part of the ground-truth variable set.

text: Inflammation markers crp_mg_l and nlr are inversely associated with pfs_months.
rationale: Involves crp_mg_l and nlr, which are not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Older age_years is associated with longer pfs_months (positive correlation), after adjustment for ECOG and stage.
rationale: Involves age_years, ECOG, and stage, none of which are part of the ground-truth variable set.

text: Mean pfs_months differs between sex_female=1 and sex_female=0.
rationale: Tests sex_female, which is not part of the ground-truth variable set.

text: Mean pfs_months differs across race_ethnicity categories.
rationale: Tests race_ethnicity, which is not part of the ground-truth variable set.

text: Mean pfs_months differs across insurance_type categories (medicaid/medicare/private/uninsured).
rationale: Tests insurance_type, which is not part of the ground-truth variable set.

text: Mean pfs_months differs between rural_residence=1 and rural_residence=0.
rationale: Tests rural_residence, which is not part of the ground-truth variable set.

text: Presence of liver_mets, bone_mets, adrenal_mets, pleural_effusion, pericardial_effusion, or contralateral_lung_mets is associated with shorter pfs_months.
rationale: Involves metastatic-site features that are not part of the ground-truth variable set and does not address the regorafenib subgroup effect.

text: Higher symptom-grade scores (fatigue_grade, pain_nrs, dyspnea_grade, cough_grade, appetite_loss_grade) are associated with shorter pfs_months.
rationale: Targets symptom-grade variables, none of which are in the ground-truth variable set.

text: Comorbidities such as diabetes_mellitus, copd, chronic_kidney_disease, heart_failure, atrial_fibrillation, prior_malignancy, depression_anxiety_diagnosis are associated with shorter pfs_months.
rationale: Targets comorbidity variables, none of which are in the ground-truth variable set.

text: At least one of the 27 SNP columns shows a Bonferroni-significant association with pfs_months (alpha = 0.05/27 = 1.85e-3).
rationale: Targets SNP columns, which are not part of the ground-truth variable set.

text: Greater prior_lines_of_therapy is associated with shorter pfs_months.
rationale: Targets prior_lines_of_therapy, which is not in the ground-truth variable set.

text: Prior treatment exposures (chemotherapy, radiation, surgery, immunotherapy, targeted therapy) are associated with shorter pfs_months.
rationale: Targets prior treatment exposures rather than treatment_regorafenib and the four subgroup-defining variables.

text: More years_since_diagnosis is associated with longer pfs_months.
rationale: Targets years_since_diagnosis, which is not in the ground-truth variable set.

text: In a multivariable OLS model of pfs_months including age, sex, ECOG, stage, sidedness, key biomarkers (kras, nras, braf_v600e, msi_high, her2_amplified), labs (cea, albumin, ldh, weight loss, crp, nlr), and all six treatments, the significant prognostic predictors are age (+), albumin (+), regorafenib (+) and ECOG, stage_iv, right_sided_primary, kras_mutation, braf_v600e, cea_ng_ml, ldh_u_l, weight_loss_pct_6mo (-).
rationale: Includes the right variables but specifies a main-effects OLS, which cannot capture the four-way subgroup-conditional effect required by the spec.

text: The regorafenib benefit on pfs_months is restricted to kras_mutation=0 patients; kras_mutation=1 patients derive no benefit (negative regorafenib×KRAS interaction).
rationale: Captures only the regorafenibÃ—KRAS slice and omits right_sided_primary, braf_v600e, and cea_ng_ml from the required subgroup conjunction.

text: The regorafenib benefit is absent or attenuated in braf_v600e=1 patients (negative regorafenib×BRAF interaction).
rationale: Captures only the regorafenibÃ—BRAF slice and omits right_sided_primary, kras_mutation, and cea_ng_ml from the required subgroup conjunction.

text: The regorafenib benefit is restricted to left-sided primaries (right_sided_primary=0); right-sided patients derive no benefit (negative regorafenib×right_sided interaction).
rationale: Captures only the regorafenibÃ—sidedness slice and omits kras_mutation, braf_v600e, and cea_ng_ml from the required subgroup conjunction.

text: The regorafenib benefit is similar in magnitude in msi_high=1 and msi_high=0 patients (no regorafenib×MSI interaction).
rationale: Concerns regorafenibÃ—MSI interaction (wrong moderator) and asserts no interaction, which does not identify the KRAS/BRAF/sidedness/CEA subgroup-conditional effect.

text: The regorafenib benefit is similar across ecog_ps strata (0,1,2).
rationale: Concerns regorafenibÃ—ECOG-PS strata, which is not among the ground-truth subgroup variables.

text: Within the joint subgroup kras_mutation=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 (left-sided RAS-WT), the regorafenib benefit on pfs_months exceeds 2 months and is the largest of any 2x2 KRAS×sidedness cell.
rationale: Identifies regorafenib benefit in left-sided KRAS-WT and the correct positive direction, but omits the required braf_v600e=0 and cea_ng_ml<=5 predicates that define the actual benefit subgroup.

text: Even in the canonical indication subgroup (kras_mutation=0 AND nras_mutation=0 AND right_sided_primary=0), treatment_cetuximab=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than treatment_cetuximab=0.
rationale: Targets cetuximab rather than regorafenib, so it is not the same association.

text: Within braf_v600e=1, the combination treatment_encorafenib=1 AND treatment_cetuximab=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than other regimens.
rationale: Targets encorafenib+cetuximab in BRAF-mutant patients, which is the opposite biomarker stratum and a different drug than the regorafenib effect.

text: After all probing, the dominant signals for pfs_months are: (1) older age (+), albumin (+), and treatment_regorafenib (+); (2) higher ECOG-PS (-), stage_iv (-), right_sided_primary (-), kras_mutation (-), braf_v600e (-), CEA (-), LDH (-), weight loss (-); (3) the regorafenib benefit is concentrated in KRAS-wildtype, BRAF-wildtype, left-sided patients; (4) cetuximab/pembrolizumab/encorafenib/trastuzumab+tucatinib show NO biomarker-targeted PFS benefit; (5) demographics, SNPs, comorbidities, mets sites and symptoms are not prognostic in this cohort.
rationale: Claim (3) captures regorafenib + KRAS-WT + BRAF-WT + left-sided with the correct positive direction, but treats CEA only as a main effect and omits the cea_ng_ml<=5 subgroup predicate, so it describes a coarser, distinct subgroup than the ground truth.

text: Patients receiving treatment_regorafenib have longer pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_regorafenib (positive main effect on PFS).
rationale: Proposes a main effect of regorafenib on PFS rather than a subgroup-conditional effect, and omits the required predicates (sidedness, KRAS, BRAF, CEA).

text: Patients receiving treatment_cetuximab have longer pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_cetuximab (positive main effect on PFS).
rationale: Wrong treatment (cetuximab) and frames a main effect; does not target the regorafenib-specific subgroup interaction.

text: Patients receiving treatment_bevacizumab have longer pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_bevacizumab (positive main effect on PFS).
rationale: Wrong treatment (bevacizumab); not the regorafenib subgroup-conditional relationship in the spec.

text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have longer pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_pembrolizumab (positive main effect on PFS).
rationale: Wrong treatment (pembrolizumab); does not match the regorafenib subgroup-conditional effect.

text: Patients receiving treatment_encorafenib have longer pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_encorafenib (positive main effect on PFS).
rationale: Wrong treatment (encorafenib); does not match the regorafenib subgroup-conditional effect.

text: Patients receiving treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib have longer pfs_months than patients not receiving it (positive main effect on PFS).
rationale: Wrong treatment (trastuzumab/tucatinib); does not match the regorafenib subgroup-conditional effect.

text: Patients with stage_iv=1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with stage_iv=0 (negative effect of metastatic disease).
rationale: Concerns stage_iv and PFS, none of which are the specified variables in the regorafenib subgroup interaction.

text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative monotonic correlation).
rationale: Concerns ecog_ps, which is not among the variables in the ground-truth association.

text: Patients with right_sided_primary=1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with right_sided_primary=0 (right-sided CRC has worse prognosis).
rationale: Only invokes right_sided_primary as a main-effect prognostic factor, missing regorafenib and the other subgroup predicates.

text: Patients with liver_mets=1 have shorter pfs_months than patients without liver_mets.
rationale: Concerns liver_mets, which is not part of the ground-truth variable set.

text: treatment_cetuximab improves pfs_months specifically in KRAS/NRAS/BRAF wild-type patients (positive interaction), reflecting standard anti-EGFR biology.
rationale: Proposes cetuximab in pan-RAS/BRAF wild-type, not regorafenib in the left-sided KRAS/BRAF-wildtype low-CEA subgroup.

text: treatment_pembrolizumab improves pfs_months specifically in msi_high=1 patients (positive interaction), reflecting standard ICI biology in dMMR CRC.
rationale: Proposes pembrolizumab in MSI-high, unrelated to regorafenib subgroup benefit.

text: treatment_encorafenib improves pfs_months specifically in braf_v600e=1 patients (positive interaction), reflecting BRAF-targeted therapy.
rationale: Proposes encorafenib in BRAF-mutant, opposite mutation status and different drug.

text: treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib improves pfs_months specifically in her2_amplified=1 patients (positive interaction).
rationale: Proposes HER2-directed therapy in HER2-amplified, unrelated to the regorafenib subgroup.

text: Higher cea_ng_ml is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
rationale: Main-effect CEA association does not capture the regorafenib-by-subgroup interaction.

text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months (positive correlation).
rationale: Albumin is not among the spec variables and this is a main-effect claim.

text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
rationale: Weight loss is not among the spec variables and this is a main-effect claim.

text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
rationale: LDH is not among the spec variables and this is a main-effect claim.

text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
rationale: CRP is not among the spec variables and this is a main-effect claim.

text: Higher nlr (neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
rationale: NLR is not among the spec variables and this is a main-effect claim.

text: Comorbidity diabetes_mellitus is associated with shorter pfs_months.
rationale: Concerns diabetes_mellitus, not any of the spec's variables (treatment_regorafenib, right_sided_primary, kras_mutation, braf_v600e, cea_ng_ml).

text: Comorbidity heart_failure is associated with shorter pfs_months.
rationale: Concerns heart_failure, not any of the spec's variables.

text: Comorbidity chronic_kidney_disease is associated with shorter pfs_months.
rationale: Concerns chronic_kidney_disease, not any of the spec's variables.

text: Older age_years is associated with longer pfs_months in this cohort (i.e., a positive correlation, atypical for oncology).
rationale: Concerns age_years, not any of the spec's variables.

text: After adjustment for age_years, ecog_ps, and stage_iv, sex_female has no independent effect on pfs_months.
rationale: Concerns sex_female with adjustments, none of which are the spec's variables.

text: After adjustment for age_years, ecog_ps, and stage_iv, right_sided_primary remains independently associated with shorter pfs_months.
rationale: Although it mentions right_sided_primary, it proposes a main-effect direction for that variable alone rather than the subgroup-conditional regorafenib effect involving treatment_regorafenib, kras_mutation, braf_v600e, and cea_ng_ml.

text: pfs_months differs across race_ethnicity groups (overall ANOVA test).
rationale: Concerns race_ethnicity, not any of the spec's variables.

text: pfs_months differs across insurance_type categories (overall ANOVA test).
rationale: Concerns insurance_type, not any of the spec's variables.

text: Patients with rural_residence=1 have shorter pfs_months than urban patients.
rationale: Concerns rural_residence, not any of the spec's variables.

text: Higher smoking_pack_years is associated with shorter pfs_months.
rationale: Concerns smoking_pack_years, not any of the spec's variables.

text: Higher education_years is associated with longer pfs_months.
rationale: Names education_years, not the regorafenib/sidedness/KRAS/BRAF/CEA variables in the spec.

text: After adjustment for age, ECOG, and stage, race_ethnicity has no independent effect on pfs_months (refined null after seeing unadjusted ANOVA).
rationale: Concerns race_ethnicity adjusted for age/ECOG/stage; none of these are the spec variables.

text: After adjustment for age, ECOG, and stage_iv, treatment_regorafenib retains a strong positive independent effect on pfs_months.
rationale: Asserts a main-effect of regorafenib after adjustment; the ground truth is subgroup-conditional, not a global main effect, and omits sidedness/KRAS/BRAF/CEA.

text: There is a positive interaction between treatment_regorafenib and stage_iv on pfs_months (regorafenib effect differs by stage).
rationale: Proposes interaction with stage_iv, which is not among the spec's subgroup variables (sidedness, KRAS, BRAF, CEA).

text: treatment_regorafenib improves pfs_months in left-sided (right_sided_primary=0) patients but not in right-sided patients (positive interaction with sidedness).
rationale: Captures only the sidedness slice of the four-way conjunction and omits KRAS, BRAF, and CEA, so it does not identify the same data-generating subgroup.

text: treatment_regorafenib improves pfs_months in KRAS wild-type patients but not in KRAS-mutant patients (positive interaction with kras_mutation).
rationale: Captures only the KRAS slice and omits sidedness, BRAF, and CEA, so it does not identify the full conjunction subgroup.

text: Treatment assignment for biomarker-targeted therapies (cetuximab, pembrolizumab, encorafenib, trastuzumab+tucatinib) is essentially independent of biomarker status (i.e., not enriched in target populations).
rationale: Concerns treatment-assignment independence from biomarkers, not the regorafenibâ†’pfs_months subgroup effect.

text: treatment_regorafenib improves pfs_months in BRAF-V600E wild-type patients but not in BRAF-V600E patients (interaction with braf_v600e).
rationale: Captures only the BRAF slice and omits sidedness, KRAS, and CEA, so it does not identify the full conjunction subgroup.

text: treatment_regorafenib improves pfs_months similarly regardless of msi_high status (no interaction with MSI).
rationale: Tests interaction with msi_high, which is not in the spec's subgroup variables.

text: treatment_regorafenib improves pfs_months similarly regardless of her2_amplified status (no interaction with HER2 amplification).
rationale: Tests interaction with her2_amplified, which is not in the spec's subgroup variables.

text: In the simultaneously KRAS-wild-type AND left-sided subgroup, treatment_regorafenib produces a larger PFS gain than its overall main effect.
rationale: Identifies regorafenib subgroup direction correctly but only specifies 2 of the 4 required subgroup predicates (missing braf_v600e and cea_ng_ml).

text: In a multivariable model that includes treatment_regorafenib*right_sided_primary and treatment_regorafenib*kras_mutation, both interactions are negative and significant.
rationale: Implies left-sided/KRAS-wt benefit for regorafenib but omits the required braf_v600e and cea_ng_ml predicates.

text: treatment_bevacizumab does not significantly modify pfs_months in any of the major subgroups (sidedness, KRAS, liver mets, MSI).
rationale: Concerns bevacizumab and a null finding, not the regorafenib subgroup effect.

text: treatment_bevacizumab differentially affects pfs_months in BRAF-V600E patients (interaction with braf_v600e).
rationale: Concerns bevacizumabÃ—BRAF, not regorafenib in the specified four-way subgroup.

text: treatment_cetuximab improves pfs_months in left-sided primaries (positive interaction with right_sided_primary=0), reflecting classical anti-EGFR sidedness biology.
rationale: Concerns cetuximab sidedness biology, not regorafenib's conjunctive subgroup effect.

text: Among the 27 SNP variables, none show a clinically meaningful main-effect association with pfs_months (multiple-testing-adjusted null).
rationale: Pertains to SNP main effects, unrelated to the regorafenib subgroup association.

text: In a comprehensive multivariable model, age_years, ecog_ps, stage_iv, albumin_g_dl, weight_loss_pct_6mo, and cea_ng_ml each retain independent significant associations with pfs_months in the directions identified earlier.
rationale: Lists main-effect associations for prognostic covariates; does not address the regorafenibÃ—subgroup interaction.

text: In a comprehensive multivariable model, the effect of treatment_regorafenib remains highly significant and is concentrated in the (KRAS wt, left-sided) subgroup; the other five treatments contribute no independent effect.
rationale: Right treatment and direction but identifies only KRAS-wt and left-sided, missing the braf_v600e and cea_ng_ml predicates.

text: prior_lines_of_therapy is associated with pfs_months (heavier-pretreated patients have shorter PFS).
rationale: Concerns prior_lines_of_therapy main effect, unrelated to the regorafenib subgroup association.

text: Symptom grades (fatigue_grade, pain_nrs, dyspnea_grade, cough_grade, appetite_loss_grade) are negatively associated with pfs_months.
rationale: Concerns symptom-grade main effects, unrelated to the regorafenib subgroup association.

text: There is a positive three-way interaction treatment_regorafenib x right_sided_primary x kras_mutation on pfs_months: the negative two-way interactions partially cancel each other when both adverse markers are present.
rationale: Identifies only a 3-way interaction among treatment_regorafenib, right_sided_primary, and kras_mutation, omitting the required braf_v600e and cea_ng_ml predicates, and frames the effect as cancellation of negative interactions rather than a positive conjunction-specific benefit.

text: The four-cell breakdown shows regorafenib benefit only in the (KRAS wt, left-sided) cell; the (KRAS wt, right), (KRAS mut, left), and (KRAS mut, right) cells show no benefit.
rationale: Stratifies only on KRAS and sidedness and ignores braf_v600e and cea_ng_ml, so it cannot isolate the four-way conjunction subgroup where the regorafenib benefit actually lives.

text: After adjusting for age/ECOG/stage_iv, individual metastatic site indicators (liver_mets, bone_mets, adrenal_mets, pleural_effusion) do not show independent associations with pfs_months.
rationale: Concerns metastatic-site covariates and pfs_months, none of which are the variables in the ground-truth association.

text: BMI and vital signs (systolic_bp, heart_rate, spo2) have no detectable association with pfs_months.
rationale: Concerns BMI and vital signs versus pfs_months, none of which are the variables in the ground-truth association.

text: Higher ECOG performance status (ecog_ps) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope) across all 50,000 metastatic colorectal cancer patients.
rationale: ECOG main effect does not involve regorafenib or the specified subgroup variables.

text: Stage IV disease (stage_iv = 1) is associated with shorter pfs_months than non-stage-IV disease (negative effect).
rationale: Stage IV main effect does not involve regorafenib or the specified subgroup variables.

text: Higher serum CEA (cea_ng_ml) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope), reflecting heavier tumor burden.
rationale: CEA is a spec variable but this is a whole-cohort main effect, not the regorafenib subgroup-conditional relationship.

text: Higher serum albumin (albumin_g_dl) is associated with longer pfs_months (positive slope), reflecting better nutritional/inflammatory state.
rationale: Albumin is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Higher serum LDH (ldh_u_l) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
rationale: LDH is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Greater 6-month weight loss (weight_loss_pct_6mo) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
rationale: Weight loss is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Cetuximab exposure (treatment_cetuximab = 1) is associated with longer pfs_months than no cetuximab in the whole cohort (positive effect).
rationale: Cetuximab is the wrong drug; ground truth involves regorafenib.

text: Bevacizumab exposure (treatment_bevacizumab = 1) is associated with longer pfs_months than no bevacizumab in the whole cohort (positive effect).
rationale: Bevacizumab is the wrong drug; ground truth involves regorafenib.

text: Pembrolizumab exposure (treatment_pembrolizumab = 1) is associated with longer pfs_months than no pembrolizumab in the whole cohort (positive effect).
rationale: Pembrolizumab is the wrong drug; ground truth involves regorafenib.

text: Encorafenib exposure (treatment_encorafenib = 1) is associated with longer pfs_months than no encorafenib in the whole cohort (positive effect).
rationale: Encorafenib is the wrong drug; ground truth involves regorafenib.

text: Trastuzumab+tucatinib exposure (treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib = 1) is associated with longer pfs_months than no trastuzumab+tucatinib in the whole cohort (positive effect).
rationale: Wrong drug (trastuzumab+tucatinib vs regorafenib) and wrong functional form (whole cohort main effect vs subgroup-conditional).

text: Regorafenib exposure (treatment_regorafenib = 1) is associated with longer pfs_months than no regorafenib in the whole cohort (positive effect).
rationale: Correct drug and direction but proposes a whole-cohort main effect rather than the subgroup-conditional relationship, and omits right_sided_primary, kras_mutation, braf_v600e, and cea_ng_ml.

text: Among RAS wild-type patients (kras_mutation = 0 and nras_mutation = 0), cetuximab is associated with longer pfs_months than no cetuximab (positive effect within RAS WT).
rationale: Wrong drug (cetuximab vs regorafenib) and wrong subgroup definition (missing sidedness, BRAF, and CEA predicates).

text: Among RAS-mutant patients (kras_mutation = 1 or nras_mutation = 1), cetuximab is associated with no PFS benefit or with shorter pfs_months (non-positive effect within RAS-mutant).
rationale: Wrong drug (cetuximab vs regorafenib) and unrelated subgroup.

text: There is a positive interaction between treatment_cetuximab and ras_mut on pfs_months — i.e., the cetuximab effect differs by RAS status, larger in RAS WT than RAS mutant.
rationale: Wrong drug and wrong variables â€” proposes a cetuximabÃ—RAS interaction unrelated to the regorafenib subgroup truth.

text: Among BRAF V600E-mutant patients (braf_v600e = 1), encorafenib is associated with longer pfs_months than no encorafenib (positive effect within BRAF V600E).
rationale: Wrong drug (encorafenib vs regorafenib) and opposite BRAF predicate (V600E mutant vs BRAF wild-type).

text: Among BRAF V600E wild-type patients, encorafenib has no PFS benefit (effect ~ 0 or negative).
rationale: Wrong drug (encorafenib vs regorafenib) and missing the other subgroup predicates.

text: There is a positive interaction between treatment_encorafenib and braf_v600e on pfs_months — encorafenib's effect is larger (more positive) in BRAF V600E than BRAF WT.
rationale: Wrong drug and wrong interaction structure compared to the regorafenib four-way subgroup.

text: Among MSI-high patients (msi_high = 1), pembrolizumab is associated with longer pfs_months than no pembrolizumab (positive effect within MSI-high).
rationale: Wrong drug (pembrolizumab vs regorafenib) and unrelated subgroup variable (MSI-high).

text: Among microsatellite-stable patients (msi_high = 0), pembrolizumab has no PFS benefit (effect ~ 0).
rationale: Wrong drug and unrelated subgroup variable; does not address the regorafenib subgroup truth.

text: There is a positive interaction between treatment_pembrolizumab and msi_high on pfs_months — pembro's effect is larger in MSI-high than MSS.
rationale: Different drug (pembrolizumab) and different variables (msi_high) than the regorafenib/sidedness/KRAS/BRAF/CEA ground truth.

text: Among HER2-amplified patients (her2_amplified = 1), trastuzumab+tucatinib is associated with longer pfs_months than no trastuzumab+tucatinib (positive effect within HER2-amplified).
rationale: Different drug (trastuzumab+tucatinib) and different subgroup variable (her2_amplified) than the ground truth.

text: Among HER2-non-amplified patients, trastuzumab+tucatinib has no PFS benefit.
rationale: Different drug and subgroup variable than the regorafenib left-sided/KRAS-WT/BRAF-WT/low-CEA association.

text: There is a positive interaction between treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib and her2_amplified on pfs_months.
rationale: Different drug and interaction variable (her2_amplified) than the ground truth.

text: Among RAS-WT patients with left-sided primary (right_sided_primary = 0), cetuximab is associated with longer pfs_months (positive effect within left-sided RAS WT).
rationale: Different drug (cetuximab vs regorafenib) and missing BRAF and CEA components of the ground-truth subgroup.

text: Among RAS-WT patients with right-sided primary (right_sided_primary = 1), cetuximab is associated with no PFS benefit.
rationale: Different drug (cetuximab) and only partial subgroup overlap; not the regorafenib association.

text: Within RAS-WT patients, there is a negative interaction between treatment_cetuximab and right_sided_primary on pfs_months — cetuximab benefit is smaller (or negative) in right-sided tumors.
rationale: Different drug (cetuximab) and missing BRAF/CEA conditions; not the regorafenib subgroup effect.

text: Right-sided primary tumor (right_sided_primary = 1) is associated with shorter pfs_months than left-sided in the whole cohort, after adjustment for stage and ECOG.
rationale: Proposes a main effect of sidedness in the whole cohort, not a regorafenib subgroup-conditional effect.

text: Bevacizumab exposure is associated with longer pfs_months after adjusting for RAS, BRAF V600E, and MSI status (positive adjusted effect).
rationale: Different drug (bevacizumab) and main-effect framing rather than the regorafenib conjunction subgroup.

text: There is an interaction between treatment_bevacizumab and ras_mut on pfs_months — bevacizumab effect differs by RAS status.
rationale: Different drug (bevacizumab) and different interaction variable; does not target the regorafenib subgroup.

text: Regorafenib's positive PFS main effect persists after adjustment for ECOG, stage, and other treatments.
rationale: Tests a main effect of regorafenib adjusted for unrelated covariates; does not propose investigating the joint subgroup defined by sidedness, KRAS, BRAF, and CEA.

text: Higher neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio (nlr) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope), reflecting systemic inflammation as a poor prognostic marker.
rationale: Concerns NLR as a prognostic marker; does not involve regorafenib or the specified subgroup-defining variables.

text: Higher CRP (crp_mg_l) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
rationale: Concerns CRP as a prognostic marker; does not involve regorafenib or the specified subgroup variables.

text: Lower hemoglobin (hemoglobin_g_dl) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope, i.e., positive coefficient on hemoglobin).
rationale: Concerns hemoglobin as a prognostic marker; does not involve regorafenib or the subgroup-defining variables.

text: Older age (age_years) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
rationale: Concerns age as a prognostic factor; does not involve regorafenib or the subgroup-defining variables.

text: In a multivariable OLS for pfs_months adjusting for age, sex, ECOG, stage IV, sidedness, RAS, BRAF V600E, MSI-H, HER2 amplification, albumin, LDH, CEA, NLR, and all six treatments: ECOG, stage IV, RAS, BRAF V600E, low albumin, high LDH, high CEA, and right-sided primary remain independent negative prognostic factors for PFS.
rationale: A main-effects multivariable OLS without interactions cannot capture a relationship that exists only within the joint left-sided/KRAS-wt/BRAF-wt/low-CEA subgroup.

text: In the same multivariable model, regorafenib remains an independent positive predictor of PFS (positive coefficient, p<0.05) after adjusting for prognostic features and other treatments.
rationale: Tests regorafenib as an independent main effect; does not propose the interaction or subgroup analysis with sidedness, KRAS, BRAF, and CEA needed to identify the conjunction-specific effect.

text: In the same multivariable model, none of cetuximab, bevacizumab, pembrolizumab, encorafenib, or trastuzumab+tucatinib has a statistically significant adjusted main effect on PFS.
rationale: Concerns adjusted main effects of other treatments; does not involve regorafenib's subgroup-conditional effect within the specified joint subgroup.

text: Patients receiving treatment_cetuximab=1 have longer mean pfs_months than those with treatment_cetuximab=0.
rationale: Main effect of cetuximab on PFS â€” wrong variable and ignores the regorafenib subgroup interaction.

text: Patients receiving treatment_bevacizumab=1 have longer mean pfs_months than those with treatment_bevacizumab=0.
rationale: Main effect of bevacizumab on PFS â€” wrong variable and not a subgroup-conditional regorafenib hypothesis.

text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab=1 have longer mean pfs_months than those with treatment_pembrolizumab=0.
rationale: Main effect of pembrolizumab on PFS â€” wrong variable and not the regorafenib subgroup relationship.

text: Patients receiving treatment_encorafenib=1 have longer mean pfs_months than those with treatment_encorafenib=0.
rationale: Main effect of encorafenib on PFS â€” wrong variable and not the regorafenib subgroup relationship.

text: Patients receiving treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib=1 have longer mean pfs_months than those with treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib=0.
rationale: Main effect of trastuzumab/tucatinib on PFS â€” wrong variable and not the regorafenib subgroup relationship.

text: Patients receiving treatment_regorafenib=1 have longer mean pfs_months than those with treatment_regorafenib=0.
rationale: Proposes only an unconditional main effect of regorafenib, missing the required left-sided/KRAS-wt/BRAF-wt/low-CEA subgroup conditioning.

text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months (each 1-unit increment shortens PFS).
rationale: Concerns ECOG performance status, not the regorafenib-by-subgroup interaction in the spec.

text: Patients with stage_iv=1 have shorter pfs_months than stage_iv=0.
rationale: Concerns stage IV, not regorafenib or the specified subgroup variables.

text: Patients with right_sided_primary=1 have shorter pfs_months than right_sided_primary=0.
rationale: Tests a main effect of right-sided primary on PFS rather than its role as a conditioning predicate within the regorafenib subgroup.

text: Older age_years is associated with shorter pfs_months (older patients fare worse, as in most oncology cohorts).
rationale: Concerns age, none of the spec's variables or the regorafenib subgroup interaction.

text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months.
rationale: Proposes a main effect of albumin on PFS; does not involve regorafenib or the specified subgroup variables.

text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months.
rationale: Proposes a main effect of weight loss on PFS; unrelated to the regorafenib subgroup association.

text: Higher cea_ng_ml is associated with shorter pfs_months.
rationale: Treats CEA as a main-effect predictor of PFS rather than as part of a regorafenib-by-subgroup interaction.

text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
rationale: LDH is not among the ground-truth variables and the hypothesis is a main effect, not a subgroup-conditional regorafenib effect.

text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
rationale: CRP is not in the ground-truth variable set and no regorafenib subgroup interaction is proposed.

text: Higher nlr (neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
rationale: NLR is not in the ground-truth variable set and the hypothesis is a simple main effect.

text: Refined: older age_years is associated with LONGER pfs_months in this cohort (counterintuitive direction noted in iteration 2).
rationale: Age is not part of the ground-truth association and no regorafenib subgroup effect is proposed.

text: Cetuximab benefit on pfs_months is larger in kras_mutation=0 (wild-type) patients than in kras_mutation=1 patients (canonical anti-EGFR predictive biomarker).
rationale: Targets cetuximab rather than regorafenib and only conditions on KRAS, missing the right-sided/BRAF/CEA subgroup conjunction.

text: Cetuximab benefit on pfs_months is larger in nras_mutation=0 patients than in nras_mutation=1 patients.
rationale: Targets cetuximab and NRAS, neither of which is in the ground-truth association.

text: Cetuximab benefit on pfs_months is larger in braf_v600e=0 patients than in braf_v600e=1 patients.
rationale: Targets cetuximab rather than regorafenib and only conditions on BRAF, missing the full left-sided/KRAS-wt/low-CEA subgroup.

text: Pembrolizumab benefit on pfs_months is larger in msi_high=1 patients than in msi_high=0 patients (canonical immunotherapy predictive biomarker).
rationale: Targets pembrolizumab and msi_high, which are not the treatment or variables in the ground truth.

text: Encorafenib benefit on pfs_months is larger in braf_v600e=1 patients than in braf_v600e=0 patients (targeted-therapy predictive biomarker).
rationale: Targets encorafenib in BRAF-mutant patients; ground truth is regorafenib in BRAF-wildtype, so both treatment and direction differ.

text: Trastuzumab+tucatinib benefit on pfs_months is larger in her2_amplified=1 patients than in her2_amplified=0 patients.
rationale: Targets trastuzumab+tucatinib and HER2 amplification, unrelated to the regorafenib subgroup association.

text: Cetuximab benefit on pfs_months is larger in left-sided (right_sided_primary=0) than in right-sided primaries.
rationale: Identifies left-sided as predictive but for cetuximab rather than regorafenib, so the treatment column differs.

text: After multivariable adjustment for age, ECOG, stage, sidedness, labs, and treatments, kras_mutation=1 and braf_v600e=1 are independently associated with shorter pfs_months (i.e., they act prognostically rather than predictively in this cohort).
rationale: Frames KRAS/BRAF as prognostic main effects with no regorafenib interaction or subgroup, missing the subgroup-conditional structure and the right_sided_primary and cea_ng_ml columns.

text: After multivariable adjustment, treatment_regorafenib remains the only treatment with a significant main effect on pfs_months; the other five treatments are null.
rationale: Captures a regorafenib main effect only, without the subgroup-conditional structure or the right_sided_primary, kras, braf, and CEA columns.

text: Refined: regorafenib's PFS benefit is concentrated in kras_mutation=0 patients and absent or markedly attenuated in kras_mutation=1 patients (i.e., KRAS is a predictive biomarker for regorafenib).
rationale: Identifies a regorafenibÃ—KRAS subgroup effect but omits right_sided_primary, braf_v600e, and cea_ng_ml from the ground-truth variable set.

text: Refined: regorafenib's PFS benefit is concentrated in braf_v600e=0 patients and absent in braf_v600e=1 patients.
rationale: Identifies a regorafenibÃ—BRAF subgroup but omits right_sided_primary, kras_mutation, and cea_ng_ml from the ground-truth variable set.

text: Regorafenib's PFS benefit varies by nras_mutation status.
rationale: Invokes nras_mutation, which is not in the ground-truth variable set, and omits the actual subgroup-defining variables.

text: Defining rasraf_wt = kras_mutation==0 AND nras_mutation==0 AND braf_v600e==0, regorafenib produces substantially larger PFS gains in rasraf_wt=1 patients than in rasraf_wt=0 patients.
rationale: Defines a RAS/RAF-wildtype subgroup that includes nras_mutation (not in ground truth) and omits right_sided_primary and cea_ng_ml, so the variable set does not match.

text: Within the rasraf_wt=1 subgroup, the other five treatments (cetuximab, bevacizumab, pembrolizumab, encorafenib, trastuzumab+tucatinib) still show no benefit, leaving regorafenib as the only effective drug in this subgroup.
rationale: Focuses on absence of benefit from other treatments within rasraf_wt subgroup; omits right_sided_primary and cea_ng_ml predicates required by the ground truth.

text: Regorafenib's PFS benefit is concentrated in left-sided primaries (right_sided_primary=0); right-sided patients gain little or none.
rationale: Identifies only the left-sided predicate; omits kras_mutation, braf_v600e, and cea_ng_ml from the conjunctive subgroup.

text: The largest regorafenib PFS gains occur in patients who are simultaneously kras_mutation=0 AND right_sided_primary=0; the 3-way interaction is significantly positive (rescuing some right-sided KRAS-mutant patients), but the dominant pattern is that regorafenib helps almost exclusively in left-sided KRAS WT.
rationale: Names left-sided and KRAS-wt predicates but omits braf_v600e and cea_ng_ml; does not identify the full four-way subgroup.

text: In the doubly-selected subgroup of left-sided AND RAS/RAF WT patients, the regorafenib PFS gain exceeds 2.5 months even after multivariable adjustment.
rationale: Captures left-sided + KRAS-wt + BRAF-wt subgroup with correct positive direction, but omits the cea_ng_ml<=5 predicate central to the ground-truth DGP.

text: Once treatment_regorafenib x kras_mutation, x braf_v600e, and x right_sided_primary interactions are included in a multivariable model, the apparent prognostic main effects of kras_mutation, braf_v600e, and right_sided_primary largely disappear, indicating that those biomarkers are not independently prognostic but rather identify patients in whom regorafenib is ineffective.
rationale: Includes regorafenib interactions with right_sided_primary, kras_mutation, and braf_v600e but omits cea_ng_ml, so it does not target the same data-generating process.

text: Pembrolizumab benefit on PFS is larger in MSI-H patients with low-burden disease (e.g., stage_iv=0) or low ECOG, which the simple interaction missed.
rationale: Concerns pembrolizumab and MSI-H/stage/ECOG variables, none of which are part of the ground-truth association.

text: Cetuximab benefit emerges in the left-sided RAS/RAF WT subgroup that mirrors the canonical clinical population.
rationale: Targets cetuximab, not regorafenib (treatment_038), so it cannot identify the same association.
