text: Concurrent treatment_encorafenib AND treatment_cetuximab in BRAF V600E patients gives a synergistic PFS benefit (BEACON-style combination).
rationale: Concerns encorafenib+cetuximab synergy in BRAF V600E patients; wrong treatment and wrong direction relative to the regorafenib subgroup effect.

text: One or more snp_* polymorphisms modify regorafenib efficacy; specifically, snp_rs1799983 is hypothesized to enhance regorafenib benefit (positive interaction).
rationale: Proposes SNP-by-regorafenib modification, introducing variables not in the ground truth and omitting all four required subgroup predicates.

text: Comorbidities (diabetes_mellitus, hypertension, copd, chronic_kidney_disease, heart_failure) shorten pfs_months independently.
rationale: Concerns comorbidity main effects on PFS; does not involve treatment_regorafenib or any of the subgroup-defining variables.

text: Race/ethnicity, insurance type, rural residence, and education_years are associated with pfs_months (disparities hypothesis).
rationale: Targets demographic/socioeconomic variables (race, insurance, rural residence, education) and not the regorafenib Ã— sidedness Ã— KRAS Ã— BRAF Ã— CEA subgroup.

text: tp53_mutation, pik3ca_mutation, pten_loss, cdkn2a_loss, met_exon14_skipping, ret_fusion, ros1_fusion, nrg1_fusion, fgfr_alteration, and ntrk_fusion each have main prognostic effects on pfs_months.
rationale: Proposes main effects of unrelated molecular alterations and does not involve regorafenib, sidedness, KRAS, BRAF, or CEA in the specified subgroup.

text: Final check: the +0.18 mo/year age slope is not driven by any subset and is not an artefact of treatment selection (the slope persists within every treatment arm and within the regorafenib-x-RAS/RAF subgroups).
rationale: Examines an age-slope effect on PFS rather than the regorafenib benefit in the left-sided/KRAS-wt/BRAF-wt/low-CEA subgroup, and only checks robustness rather than identifying that subgroup interaction.

text: Higher baseline ECOG performance status (ecog_ps) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative linear association).
rationale: Proposes a main effect of ECOG on PFS, not the regorafenib subgroup-conditional effect.

text: Patients with stage_iv = 1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with stage_iv = 0.
rationale: Stage IV is not among the spec's variables and the form is a main effect, not the targeted subgroup interaction.

text: Higher baseline albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months (positive linear association).
rationale: Albumin is not in the spec; this is an unrelated main effect.

text: Higher baseline ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative linear association).
rationale: LDH is not in the spec; this is an unrelated main effect.

text: Higher baseline cea_ng_ml is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative linear association).
rationale: Uses CEA but only as a linear main effect on PFS, omitting regorafenib and the left-sided/KRAS-wt/BRAF-wt conditioning.

text: Higher baseline neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio (nlr) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative linear association).
rationale: NLR is not in the spec; this is an unrelated main effect.

text: Greater weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative linear association).
rationale: Weight loss is not in the spec; this is an unrelated main effect.

text: Older age_years is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative linear association, expected from clinical priors).
rationale: Age is not in the spec; this is an unrelated main effect.

text: Patients receiving treatment_cetuximab have longer pfs_months than those who do not (anti-EGFR antibody, expected positive effect).
rationale: Targets cetuximab rather than regorafenib and ignores the molecular/sidedness/CEA subgroup.

text: Patients receiving treatment_bevacizumab have longer pfs_months than those who do not.
rationale: Targets bevacizumab rather than regorafenib and ignores the specified subgroup conditioning.

text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have longer pfs_months than those who do not.
rationale: Wrong drug (pembrolizumab) and main effect rather than the regorafenib subgroup-conditional effect.

text: Patients receiving treatment_encorafenib have longer pfs_months than those who do not.
rationale: Wrong drug (encorafenib) and main effect rather than the regorafenib subgroup-conditional effect.

text: Patients receiving treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib have longer pfs_months than those who do not.
rationale: Wrong drug (trastuzumab/tucatinib) and main effect rather than the regorafenib subgroup-conditional effect.

text: Patients receiving treatment_regorafenib have longer pfs_months than those who do not.
rationale: Right drug but proposes a main effect; the ground truth is explicitly subgroup-conditional and the spec notes no single-feature main effect captures the benefit.

text: There is a treatment_cetuximab x kras_mutation interaction such that cetuximab improves pfs_months in KRAS-wildtype but not in KRAS-mutant patients (KRAS-mut interaction effect <= 0).
rationale: Wrong drug (cetuximab) and only a single KRAS interaction, missing sidedness, BRAF, and CEA.

text: There is a treatment_cetuximab x nras_mutation interaction such that cetuximab does not benefit NRAS-mutant patients.
rationale: Wrong drug and wrong variable (NRAS instead of the regorafenib subgroup features).

text: There is a treatment_cetuximab x braf_v600e interaction such that cetuximab does not benefit BRAF V600E patients.
rationale: Wrong drug (cetuximab) and only a BRAF interaction, not the four-way regorafenib subgroup.

text: There is a treatment_cetuximab x right_sided_primary interaction such that cetuximab benefits left-sided more than right-sided primary tumors.
rationale: Wrong drug (cetuximab) and only a sidedness interaction, missing KRAS, BRAF, and CEA.

text: Restricting to RAS-wildtype, BRAF-wildtype, left-sided patients, treatment_cetuximab is associated with longer pfs_months.
rationale: Correct subgroup features (left-sided/RAS-wt/BRAF-wt) but the wrong drug (cetuximab) and missing the low-CEA predicate.

text: There is a treatment_pembrolizumab x msi_high interaction such that pembrolizumab prolongs pfs_months in MSI-high patients but not MSS patients.
rationale: Wrong drug (pembrolizumab) and wrong subgroup variable (MSI-high), unrelated to regorafenib.

text: MSI-high patients have different baseline pfs_months than MSS patients (direction unspecified).
rationale: Concerns MSI status and baseline PFS, not the regorafenib subgroup interaction.

text: There is a treatment_encorafenib x braf_v600e interaction such that encorafenib prolongs pfs_months in BRAF V600E-mutant patients but not in BRAF-wildtype patients.
rationale: Targets encorafenibÃ—BRAF interaction, not regorafenib in the left-sided/KRAS-wt/BRAF-wt/low-CEA subgroup.

text: BRAF V600E mutation is independently associated with shorter pfs_months (poor-prognosis biomarker).
rationale: Proposes BRAF V600E as an independent main effect, not the multi-feature regorafenib subgroup.

text: There is a treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib x her2_amplified interaction such that the regimen prolongs pfs_months in HER2-amplified patients but not in HER2-negative patients.
rationale: Concerns trastuzumab/tucatinibÃ—HER2, unrelated to the regorafenib subgroup association.

text: Using her2_amplification (separate column), the same interaction holds.
rationale: Same HER2 interaction hypothesis; does not involve regorafenib or the specified subgroup variables.

text: Patients with liver_mets = 1 have shorter pfs_months than those without liver metastases.
rationale: Liver metastases is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Patients with bone_mets = 1 have shorter pfs_months than those without bone metastases.
rationale: Bone metastases is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Patients with pleural_effusion = 1 have shorter pfs_months than those without.
rationale: Pleural effusion is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: pfs_months differs across race_ethnicity categories (omnibus comparison).
rationale: Race/ethnicity is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: pfs_months differs across insurance_type categories (omnibus comparison; medicaid/uninsured may have shorter PFS).
rationale: Insurance type is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Rural residence (rural_residence = 1) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
rationale: Uses rural_residence, not any of the spec variables (treatment_regorafenib, sidedness, KRAS, BRAF, CEA).

text: Female patients (sex_female = 1) have different pfs_months than male patients.
rationale: Uses sex_female, not any of the spec variables.

text: More prior_lines_of_therapy is associated with shorter pfs_months.
rationale: Uses prior_lines_of_therapy, not any of the spec variables.

text: In a multivariable OLS model of pfs_months that includes ECOG, stage, age, albumin, CEA, weight loss, LDH, NLR, CRP, hemoglobin, all six treatments, key biomarkers, sidedness, and metastatic sites, the established prognostics (ECOG, stage, age, albumin, CEA, weight loss, LDH) and the biomarkers KRAS, BRAF V600E, right-sided primary remain independent predictors, while only treatment_regorafenib retains a positive treatment effect.
rationale: An additive OLS with main effects cannot capture the four-way subgroup-conditional treatment effect that defines the ground truth.

text: kras_mutation is independently prognostic for shorter pfs_months after multivariable adjustment.
rationale: Frames KRAS as an independent prognostic main effect, not as part of a subgroup-conditional regorafenib benefit.

text: right_sided_primary is independently prognostic for shorter pfs_months after multivariable adjustment.
rationale: Frames right_sided_primary as an independent prognostic main effect, not as part of a subgroup-conditional regorafenib benefit.

text: Selected comorbidities (diabetes_mellitus, copd, chronic_kidney_disease, heart_failure, depression_anxiety_diagnosis) are individually associated with shorter pfs_months.
rationale: Uses comorbidity variables not in the spec.

text: At least one of the 30 SNP markers (snp_rs*) shows an association with pfs_months at the conventional 0.05 significance threshold (top hits to be reported, with the caveat of multiple testing).
rationale: Examines SNP markers, none of which are in the spec variable set.

text: The treatment_regorafenib main effect on pfs_months remains significantly positive after adjustment for ECOG, stage IV, age, albumin, weight loss, CEA, KRAS, BRAF V600E, and sidedness.
rationale: Tests regorafenib as a main effect with linear adjustment, which cannot recover the subgroup-conditional benefit confined to left-sided/KRAS-wt/BRAF-wt/low-CEA patients.

text: The treatment_regorafenib effect on pfs_months differs between stage_iv = 1 and stage_iv = 0 (interaction).
rationale: Tests interaction with stage_iv, which is not part of the ground-truth subgroup definition.

text: There is a treatment_regorafenib x kras_mutation interaction: regorafenib's PFS benefit is concentrated in KRAS-wildtype patients and is largely absent in KRAS-mutant patients.
rationale: Names only treatment_regorafenib and kras_mutation, missing right_sided_primary, braf_v600e, and cea_ng_ml; a single two-way interaction does not identify the four-predicate joint subgroup.

text: There is a treatment_regorafenib x braf_v600e interaction: regorafenib's PFS benefit is largely absent in BRAF V600E-mutant patients.
rationale: Names only treatment_regorafenib and braf_v600e, missing right_sided_primary, kras_mutation, and cea_ng_ml; cannot recover the joint subgroup-conditional effect.

text: There is a treatment_regorafenib x right_sided_primary interaction: regorafenib's PFS benefit is concentrated in left-sided primary tumors and absent in right-sided primaries.
rationale: Names only treatment_regorafenib and right_sided_primary, missing kras_mutation, braf_v600e, and cea_ng_ml; a sidedness-only interaction is not the same data-generating process.

text: Among patients who are KRAS-wildtype AND BRAF-wildtype, regorafenib produces a markedly larger PFS gain than in patients with either mutation.
rationale: Captures KRAS-wt AND BRAF-wt with regorafenib but omits right_sided_primary and cea_ng_ml, so it does not point at the same set of columns or the four-way joint subgroup.

text: There is a treatment_regorafenib x ecog_ps interaction (ECOG modifies the regorafenib benefit).
rationale: Involves ecog_ps, which is not part of the ground-truth variables, and omits all four required subgroup-defining features.

text: Among the 77 binary features, several have univariate associations with `objective_response` at p<1e-5, with `feature_013` (a 65%-prevalent indicator) showing the largest negative association: response rate is lower in patients with feature_013=1 than in patients with feature_013=0.
rationale: feature_013 is not among the ground-truth variables and the hypothesis describes an unrelated main effect.

text: Among the continuous and ordinal features, the ordinal `feature_051` (values 0/1/2) is the strongest predictor of `objective_response`: mean `feature_051` is lower in responders than in non-responders (i.e., response decreases monotonically with `feature_051`).
rationale: feature_051 is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: The binary `feature_006` (28% prevalent) is positively associated with `objective_response`: response rate is higher when feature_006=1 than when feature_006=0.
rationale: Mentions only feature_006 (tmb_high) as a univariate main effect; misses pembrolizumab, pdl1_tps, stk11_mutation, sex_female and the subgroup-conditional structure that the spec says no single feature captures.

text: `feature_011` (continuous, range 0-28) is negatively associated with `objective_response`: each unit increase in feature_011 lowers the odds of response.
rationale: feature_011 is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: `feature_067` (binary, 25% prevalent) is negatively associated with `objective_response`: patients with feature_067=1 have a lower response rate than patients with feature_067=0.
rationale: feature_067 is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: `feature_092` (continuous, range 0-0.8) has a small overall positive association with `objective_response`, with mean feature_092 higher in responders than in non-responders.
rationale: Mentions only feature_092 (pdl1_tps) as a univariate main effect; misses pembrolizumab, tmb_high, stk11_mutation, sex_female and the conjunctive subgroup structure.

text: Patient age (`feature_078`, range 30-90) is associated with `objective_response`, with response rates differing across age quintiles.
rationale: Age (feature_078) is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Self-reported race (`feature_123`) is associated with differential `objective_response` rates: white patients have a different response rate than non-white patients.
rationale: Race (feature_123) is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Insurance type (`feature_005`: medicare/medicaid/private/uninsured) is associated with `objective_response`, with private-insurance patients having higher response rates than medicaid/uninsured patients.
rationale: Insurance (feature_005) is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Histology (`feature_043`: adenocarcinoma vs squamous) modifies the association between `feature_006` and `objective_response`, with a larger positive feature_006 effect in adenocarcinoma than in squamous histology.
rationale: Histology (feature_043) is not among the ground-truth variables, and the proposed interaction omits pembrolizumab, pdl1_tps, stk11_mutation, and sex_female.

text: Smoking status (`feature_057`: current/former/never) modifies the association between `feature_007` and `objective_response`, with the positive feature_007 effect smaller in never-smokers than in current/former smokers.
rationale: Uses smoking (feature_057) which is not in the ground-truth variable set, and omits pembrolizumab, pdl1_tps, tmb_high, and stk11_mutation.

text: Smoking status (`feature_057`) modifies the association between `feature_039` and `objective_response`, with the positive feature_039 effect smaller in never-smokers than in current/former smokers.
rationale: Invokes smoking (feature_057) as the modifier rather than the ground-truth conjunction of pdl1_tps, tmb_high, stk11_mutation, and sex_female.

text: `feature_092` (continuous, 0-0.8) modifies the effect of `feature_006` on `objective_response`: the positive feature_006 effect is larger at higher feature_092 values than at lower values (positive interaction).
rationale: Targets only a feature_092 Ã— feature_006 interaction and omits pembrolizumab, sex_female, and stk11_mutation, so it does not point at the ground-truth variable set.

text: `feature_092` modifies the effect of `feature_007` on `objective_response`: the positive feature_007 effect is larger at higher feature_092 values (positive interaction).
rationale: Covers only feature_092 Ã— feature_007 and omits pembrolizumab, tmb_high, and stk11_mutation.

text: `feature_092` modifies the effect of `feature_039` on `objective_response`: the positive feature_039 effect is larger at higher feature_092 values (positive interaction).
rationale: Covers only feature_092 Ã— feature_039 and omits tmb_high, sex_female, and stk11_mutation.

text: Patients positive for all three putative-treatment indicators (feature_006=1 AND feature_007=1 AND feature_039=1) have a higher `objective_response` rate than patients with zero of the three positive.
rationale: Triple-positive conjunction includes pembrolizumab, tmb_high, and sex_female but omits pdl1_tps and stk11_mutation, so it does not target the ground-truth variable set.

text: The boost in `objective_response` from triple positivity (feature_006+feature_007+feature_039 = 3) is concentrated in the high-`feature_092` tertile, with a much smaller boost in the low/mid tertiles (three-way interaction).
rationale: Three-way interaction covers pembrolizumab, tmb_high, sex_female, and pdl1_tps but still omits stk11_mutation, so the variable set does not match the ground truth.

text: `feature_013` and `feature_067` are independently negatively associated with `objective_response` after multivariable adjustment, with neither effect explained by the other.
rationale: feature_013 and feature_067 are not among the ground-truth variables.

text: After multivariable adjustment for clinical features (feature_051, feature_011, feature_092, treatments, feature_013, feature_067, age, smoking, histology, and insurance), `feature_123` (race) is no longer significantly associated with `objective_response`.
rationale: feature_123 (race) is not in the ground-truth variable set.

text: After multivariable adjustment, `feature_078` (age) remains non-associated with `objective_response`, with coefficient near zero.
rationale: feature_078 (age) is not in the ground-truth variable set.

text: `feature_099` (range 1.7-5.5) is independently positively associated with `objective_response` after multivariable adjustment, consistent with a serum-protein-like marker (e.g., albumin).
rationale: Concerns feature_099, which is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: `feature_063` (range 0.03-296) is independently negatively associated with `objective_response` after multivariable adjustment, consistent with an inflammatory/marker analyte that worsens prognosis.
rationale: Concerns feature_063, which is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: The likelihood-ratio test for adding three feature_092 x treatment interactions (feature_092*feature_006, feature_092*feature_007, feature_092*feature_039) to the multivariable model is highly significant, supporting feature_092 as a predictive (effect-modifying) biomarker rather than a purely prognostic marker.
rationale: Tests pairwise feature_092 interactions but omits STK11 (feature_027), so it cannot identify the four-way conjunction subgroup.

text: After adding the three feature_092 x treatment interactions, the main effects of feature_006, feature_007, and feature_039 become near-zero or negative, while the interaction terms are all positive and significant - implying treatment benefit is essentially absent at feature_092=0 and grows linearly with feature_092.
rationale: Omits feature_027 and asserts a linear-in-feature_092 effect rather than the threshold conjunction defining the subgroup.

text: The joint best vs worst risk strata defined by clinical features (best: triple-positive, high feature_092, feature_051=0, feature_013=0, feature_067=0; worst: triple-negative, low feature_092, feature_051=2, feature_013=1, feature_067=1) differ in `objective_response` rate by at least 25 percentage points.
rationale: The contrasted strata are defined by feature_051/013/067 and 'triple-positive' rather than the specific feature_007/092/006/027 conjunction.

text: `feature_013` (the largest negative binary predictor) is approximately uncorrelated with histology (feature_043), smoking (feature_057), race (feature_123), insurance (feature_005), age, and the labs we have measured - i.e., it is not explained by other observed covariates and represents an independent risk factor.
rationale: About feature_013, which is not part of the ground-truth association.

text: Smoking status modifies the effect of `feature_006`: the feature_006 benefit is at least as large in never-smokers as in current/former smokers (in contrast to features_007/039, where the benefit disappears in never-smokers).
rationale: Concerns smoking's modification of feature_006 effects; wrong variable set and wrong relationship.

text: ECOG-like `feature_051` does not strongly modify the effect of `feature_006`: the absolute response-rate benefit of feature_006=1 is similar across feature_051 levels 0/1/2.
rationale: Examines feature_051 modification of feature_006, neither of which together represent the ground-truth subgroup conjunction.

text: The dominant pattern in this NSCLC cohort is: response is driven by (a) ECOG-like performance status (feature_051, monotonically negative); (b) two unexplained risk factors feature_013 and feature_067 (both strongly negative); (c) a putative treatment-eligibility/exposure cluster (features 006/007/039) whose benefit depends almost entirely on a continuous biomarker (feature_092) that resembles PD-L1 expression; and (d) two prognostic labs (feature_099 positive, feature_063 negative).
rationale: Describes treatment benefit as a continuous function of feature_092 alone and omits the STK11/female conjunction defining the true subgroup.

text: Demographic variables - age (feature_078), race (feature_123), and insurance (feature_005) - are NOT significant predictors of `objective_response` after adjusting for clinical/biomarker features, with adjusted p-values >0.1 for race and >0.7 for age.
rationale: Concerns null effects of demographic variables (age, race, insurance) unrelated to the ground-truth subgroup association.

text: feature_007 and feature_039 are essentially independent of each other (chi-square p > 0.5) and of feature_006, suggesting random/independent assignment - i.e., these three features are likely separate exposures (e.g., different lines of therapy or three independent treatment indicators) rather than overlapping codes for the same exposure.
rationale: This hypothesis only describes independence among feature_007, feature_039, and feature_006 and does not propose investigating the four-way subgroup-conditional effect of pembrolizumab on objective_response.

text: Among the binary features (feature_001 ... feature_122), at least one is associated with objective_response at p<0.001 in a univariable chi-square test.
rationale: Univariable chi-square on binary features cannot identify a 4-way subgroup-conditional relationship and does not point at the specific ground-truth variables.

text: Among the continuous features (e.g., feature_011, feature_063, feature_092, feature_099), at least one differs in mean between responders (objective_response=1) and non-responders (=0) at p<0.001 (Welch t-test).
rationale: Univariable Welch t-tests on continuous features do not target the subgroup-conditional interaction nor name the required variable set.

text: feature_051 (ordinal 0/1/2, plausibly performance-status-like) shows a monotonic decrease in objective_response across its three levels.
rationale: feature_051 is not among the ground-truth variables and the hypothesis posits a marginal monotonic effect, not a subgroup interaction.

text: feature_013, feature_067, feature_051, and feature_011 each remain independently associated with lower objective_response (negative log-odds coefficient, p<0.01) in a multivariable logistic regression adjusting for all other features.
rationale: None of feature_013/067/051/011 are ground-truth variables, and main-effects logistic regression cannot capture the 4-way subgroup effect.

text: feature_006, feature_007, feature_039, feature_099, and feature_092 each remain independently associated with higher objective_response (positive log-odds coefficient, p<0.01) in a multivariable logistic regression adjusting for all other features.
rationale: Although it lists four of the five ground-truth variables, it omits feature_027 (STK11), adds an irrelevant feature_099, and proposes only main-effects logistic regression that cannot capture the subgroup-conditional interaction.

text: After adjustment for the clinical predictors above, race (feature_123) and insurance (feature_005) categories are NOT associated with objective_response (all category contrasts p>=0.05).
rationale: Concerns demographic confounding rather than the pembrolizumab subgroup interaction.

text: The favorable effect of feature_006 on objective_response is stronger in patients with lower feature_051 (i.e., interaction coefficient for feature_006 x feature_051 is negative, p<0.05).
rationale: feature_051 is not in the ground truth; this two-way interaction does not target the female/PD-L1/TMB/STK11-WT subgroup.

text: The negative effect of feature_013 on objective_response varies with feature_051 (interaction p<0.05).
rationale: Neither feature_013 nor feature_051 is among the ground-truth variables.

text: The negative effect of feature_011 (likely metastatic burden) on objective_response is amplified at higher feature_051 (interaction coefficient negative, p<0.05).
rationale: Neither feature_011 nor feature_051 is among the ground-truth variables.

text: feature_006 (positive predictor) and feature_013 (negative predictor) interact on the log-odds of objective_response (interaction p<0.05).
rationale: feature_013 is not a ground-truth variable and a single two-way interaction does not capture the 4-way subgroup-conditional effect.

text: The effect of feature_006 on objective_response differs by histology feature_043 (interaction p<0.05); specifically, the response benefit is larger in adenocarcinoma than squamous.
rationale: Involves feature_043 (histology), which is not in the ground-truth variable set.

text: The effects of feature_006 and feature_013 on objective_response differ between never-smokers and ever-smokers (interaction p<0.05 for at least one).
rationale: Involves smoking status and feature_013, which are not in the ground-truth variable set.

text: Among the positive predictors feature_006, feature_007, and feature_039, at least one pairwise binary x binary interaction is positive and statistically significant (p<0.05): in other words, having two of these features together increases response more than the sum of individual effects.
rationale: Covers only 3 of 5 ground-truth variables (missing feature_092 PDL1 and feature_027 STK11), so it cannot identify the same 4-way subgroup-conditional effect.

text: Patients positive for ALL THREE of feature_006, feature_007, and feature_039 have a substantially higher response rate than predicted by additive main effects (3-way interaction term coefficient > 0, p<0.05).
rationale: Three-way interaction omits feature_092 and feature_027, so it does not point at the same variable set or recover the specified 4-way subgroup.

text: After adjusting for the strongest clinical predictors (feature_013, feature_067, feature_006, feature_007, feature_039, feature_011, feature_051, feature_063), Black patients (feature_123='black') have HIGHER objective_response than White patients (positive coefficient, p<0.05).
rationale: Tests a race main effect (feature_123), which is unrelated to the ground-truth association.

text: After adjusting for the strongest clinical predictors, Medicaid-insured patients (feature_005='medicaid') have LOWER objective_response than privately insured patients (negative coefficient, p<0.05).
rationale: Tests an insurance main effect (feature_005), which is unrelated to the ground-truth association.

text: feature_092 (continuous, range 0-0.8, near-zero overall main effect) interacts with feature_006: among patients with feature_006=1, higher feature_092 is associated with higher objective_response (positive interaction coefficient, p<0.05).
rationale: Only involves feature_092 and feature_006; misses pembrolizumab, sex, and STK11, so cannot identify the ground-truth subgroup effect.

text: feature_092 interacts negatively with feature_013: in feature_013-negative patients, higher feature_092 is associated with higher response, while in feature_013-positive patients the effect is attenuated (interaction p<0.05).
rationale: Involves feature_013, which is not in the ground-truth variable set, and omits pembrolizumab, sex, TMB, and STK11.

text: The relationship between feature_011 (range 0-28, plausibly metastatic-site count) and objective_response is non-monotonic at the upper extreme (a quadratic term improves model fit, p<0.05).
rationale: Tests a quadratic term in feature_011, which is not part of the ground-truth association.

text: There exists a positive three-way interaction among feature_006, feature_007, and feature_092: patients positive for both feature_006 and feature_007 with high feature_092 derive disproportionate benefit (3-way interaction coef > 0, p<0.05).
rationale: Three-way interaction omits feature_039 (pembrolizumab) and feature_027 (STK11), so it does not match the ground-truth variable set or its 4-way subgroup structure.

text: There is a positive three-way interaction among feature_006, feature_092, and (the absence of) feature_013: the feature_006 x feature_092 synergy is largest in feature_013-negative patients (3-way interaction coef > 0, p<0.05).
rationale: Uses feature_013 in place of the spec's feature_007/feature_027/feature_039; wrong variable set.

text: The triple synergy of feature_006/feature_007/feature_039 is concentrated in patients with high feature_092 (above-median): triple-positive AND high-feature_092 patients have markedly higher response than triple-positive low-feature_092 patients.
rationale: Captures feature_006/feature_007/feature_039/feature_092 but omits feature_027 (STK11), so it does not target the full 5-variable subgroup.

text: Within feature_006=1 patients, the relationship between feature_092 and objective_response is not strictly linear; a threshold at feature_092 ~ 0.5-0.6 produces a sharp jump in response rate (response in 092>=0.6 subgroup > response in 092<0.6 subgroup by >=0.10 absolute).
rationale: Only invokes feature_006 and feature_092; lacks feature_039 (pembrolizumab), feature_007, and feature_027.

text: Among feature_006=0 patients, feature_092 has no monotonic association with objective_response (rank-quintile trend p>0.05).
rationale: Restricted to feature_006 and feature_092; does not address pembrolizumab, sex, or STK11.

text: The negative feature_011 effect is attenuated in patients with high feature_092 (feature_011 x feature_092 interaction > 0, p<0.05).
rationale: Posits a feature_011 x feature_092 interaction; feature_011 is not part of the ground-truth variable set.

text: A multivariable logistic model that includes (i) the 5 negative main effects (feature_013, feature_067, feature_011, feature_051, feature_063), (ii) the 5 positive main effects (feature_006, feature_007, feature_039, feature_092, feature_099), (iii) the feature_006 x feature_092 interaction, (iv) the feature_006 x feature_007 x feature_039 triple interaction, AND (v) race and insurance dummies, will retain ALL of the following at p<0.05: feature_013, feature_067, feature_011, feature_051, feature_006 x feature_092, feature_006 x feature_007 x feature_039, race=black, ins=medicaid.
rationale: Includes a feature_006 x feature_007 x feature_039 triple and a separate feature_006 x feature_092 term, but feature_027 (STK11) is absent from the interactions, so it does not encode the spec's 5-variable subgroup.

text: In the same final model, smoking-status (current, never) and histology (squamous) coefficients are NOT statistically significant after adjustment.
rationale: Concerns smoking and histology nuisance covariates, not the pembrolizumab/PD-L1/TMB/STK11/sex association.

text: feature_013 and feature_067 (the two largest negative main effects) act roughly additively on the log-odds, i.e., patients positive for both have the lowest response rate but no super-additive interaction.
rationale: About additivity of feature_013 and feature_067 negative effects; unrelated to the ground-truth variables.

text: Patients with the predictor profile (feature_006=1, feature_007=1, feature_039=1, feature_092 above median, feature_013=0, feature_067=0, feature_051=0) have the highest empirical response rate in the cohort -- at least 30%, more than double the cohort average of 16.9%.
rationale: Subgroup profile uses feature_006/007/039/092 but omits feature_027 (STK11=0) and adds unrelated features, so it is not the spec's subgroup.

text: Patients with the inverse profile (feature_013=1, feature_067=1, feature_051=2, feature_006=0) have empirical response rate below 10%.
rationale: Describes a low-response inverse profile built from feature_013/067/051/006, which is not the ground-truth high-response subgroup or its variables.

text: At least one of the 123 anonymized features in ds001_nsclc is associated with objective_response (overall ORR ~16.9%) at p<0.05 after a univariate screen using chi-square tests for categorical features and Welch t-tests for continuous features.
rationale: A generic univariate p<0.05 screen does not point at the specific 5-variable subgroup-conditional relationship and a univariate test would not be expected to capture a 4-way conjunction.

text: The three-level categorical feature_051 (levels 0/1/2 with counts 17557/24962/7481) is associated with objective_response, with ORR differing across the three levels.
rationale: feature_051 is not among the ground-truth variables (treatment_pembrolizumab/feature_039, pdl1_tps/feature_092, tmb_high/feature_006, stk11_mutation/feature_027, sex_female/feature_007).

text: Higher values of the continuous feature_011 (range 0.0-28.0) are associated with lower probability of objective_response.
rationale: feature_011 is not among the ground-truth variables, and the proposed direction is negative versus the ground-truth positive effect.

text: The binary feature_013 is negatively associated with objective_response: ORR is lower in patients with feature_013=1 than in patients with feature_013=0.
rationale: feature_013 is not among the ground-truth variables and the direction is wrong.

text: The effect of feature_051 on objective_response is monotone-decreasing across its three ordered levels (0 -> 1 -> 2), consistent with an ordered clinical variable such as a PD-L1 / treatment-line / stage gradient.
rationale: feature_051 is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: The negative association between continuous feature_011 and objective_response is approximately monotone across deciles of feature_011 (highest decile lower ORR than lowest decile).
rationale: feature_011 is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: After mutual adjustment in a logistic regression including feature_051 (categorical), feature_011, feature_013, feature_067, feature_006, feature_007, feature_039, race (feature_123), insurance (feature_005), smoking (feature_057), histology (feature_043), and age (feature_078), each of feature_051, feature_011, feature_013, feature_067, feature_006, feature_007, and feature_039 retains a significant independent association with objective_response.
rationale: Although it includes feature_039/006/007, it omits pdl1_tps (feature_092) and stk11_mutation (feature_027), and a main-effects logistic regression cannot capture the 4-way subgroup-conditional interaction the ground truth specifies.

text: After multivariable adjustment, age (feature_078, years) shows no significant association with objective_response.
rationale: Age (feature_078) is not part of the ground-truth association.

text: After multivariable adjustment, race (feature_123) and smoking status (feature_057) are not significantly associated with objective_response.
rationale: Race and smoking are not part of the ground-truth association.

text: The negative effect of feature_013 on objective_response is larger (more negative log-odds) at higher feature_051 levels (interaction feature_051 x feature_013).
rationale: Neither feature_051 nor feature_013 is among the ground-truth variables, and the proposed interaction is not the specified 4-way subgroup with pembrolizumab.

text: The negative effect of feature_067 on objective_response differs across feature_051 levels (interaction feature_051 x feature_067).
rationale: References feature_067 and feature_051, neither of which is among the ground-truth variables (treatment_pembrolizumab, pdl1_tps, tmb_high, stk11_mutation, sex_female).

text: The negative slope of feature_011 on objective_response differs across feature_051 levels (interaction feature_051 x feature_011).
rationale: References feature_011 and feature_051, neither of which is among the ground-truth variables.

text: The distribution of feature_051 (a candidate treatment / PD-L1 / stage variable) differs across self-reported race (feature_123) categories - i.e., the cohort shows allocation disparities by race.
rationale: Concerns allocation of feature_051 by race (feature_123); the outcome is not objective_response and the variables are not in the ground-truth set.

text: The distribution of feature_051 differs across insurance categories (feature_005), suggesting differential utilization by payer.
rationale: Concerns allocation of feature_051 by insurance; outcome is not objective_response and variables are not in the ground-truth set.

text: The effect of feature_051 on objective_response (responsiveness) differs by self-reported race (interaction feature_051 x feature_123).
rationale: Interaction of feature_051 with feature_123 â€” neither variable is among the ground-truth treatment/biomarker/sex set.

text: The effect of feature_051 on objective_response differs by insurance category (interaction feature_051 x feature_005).
rationale: Interaction of feature_051 with feature_005 â€” neither variable is among the ground-truth variables.

text: The five 0-4 ordinal features (feature_026, feature_033, feature_018, feature_045, feature_042) are each monotonically associated with objective_response (e.g., higher value -> lower ORR).
rationale: Names ordinal features (026, 033, 018, 045, 042) that are not in the ground-truth variable set (039, 092, 006, 027, 007).

text: Smoking status (feature_057: never/former/current) is associated with objective_response, with never-smokers having different ORR than current/former smokers.
rationale: Smoking status (feature_057) is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Histology (feature_043: adenocarcinoma vs squamous) is associated with objective_response.
rationale: Histology (feature_043) is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Among the 36 continuous features, feature_011 has the strongest negative association with objective_response per standard deviation, and at least three additional continuous features (e.g., feature_099, feature_063, feature_092) show significant per-SD associations.
rationale: Although feature_092 (pdl1_tps) appears, this is a main-effect per-SD association hypothesis that omits treatment_pembrolizumab, tmb_high, stk11_mutation, and sex_female and explicitly cannot capture the four-way subgroup-conditional treatment effect (which by spec is invisible in marginal main effects).

text: Higher feature_099 (range 1.7-5.5, plausibly serum albumin in g/dL) is associated with higher probability of objective_response.
rationale: feature_099 is not among the ground-truth variables and the hypothesis proposes a marginal main effect, not the four-way subgroup-conditional pembrolizumab effect.

text: Higher feature_063 (right-skewed, range 0.03-296.9, plausibly an inflammatory marker) is associated with lower probability of objective_response, and the association is better described on the log scale.
rationale: feature_063 is not among the ground-truth variables (treatment_pembrolizumab, pdl1_tps, tmb_high, stk11_mutation, sex_female).

text: feature_013 and feature_067 (both negative independent predictors of ORR) interact super-additively: the joint negative effect when both = 1 is more negative than the sum of the individual effects.
rationale: feature_013 and feature_067 are not among the ground-truth variables and the proposed two-way interaction does not target the pembrolizumab subgroup.

text: The slope of feature_011 on log-odds of ORR is steeper (more negative) in the feature_013=1 subgroup than in feature_013=0 (interaction).
rationale: feature_011 and feature_013 are not among the ground-truth variables; this interaction is unrelated to the pembrolizumab/PD-L1/TMB/STK11/sex subgroup.

text: The slope of feature_011 on log-odds of ORR differs by feature_067 status (interaction feature_011 x feature_067).
rationale: feature_011 and feature_067 are not among the ground-truth variables; this interaction does not concern the specified subgroup.

text: Among ~50/50-split binary features (potential demographic indicators), feature_039 is associated with objective_response while feature_086, feature_115, feature_037, feature_095, feature_093, feature_012, and feature_110 are not.
rationale: Although feature_039 (pembrolizumab) is mentioned, the hypothesis claims only a marginal main effect and omits the required conditioning on PD-L1, TMB, STK11, and sex subgroup.

text: In a consolidated multivariable logistic regression that includes feature_051, feature_011, feature_013, feature_067, feature_006, feature_007, feature_039, feature_099, log1p(feature_063), feature_092, feature_076, feature_112, feature_021, race, insurance, smoking, histology, and age, all the strongest features (feature_051, feature_011, feature_013, feature_067) remain significant at p<0.001 with their univariate signs preserved.
rationale: Although the model includes feature_039, feature_092, feature_006, and feature_007, it omits feature_027 and tests only main effects (not the four-way subgroup interaction), so it is not reasonably expected to recover the subgroup-conditional effect.

text: feature_076 is positively and feature_112 is negatively associated with objective_response after multivariable adjustment (both seen as borderline-significant in univariate screen).
rationale: feature_076 and feature_112 are not among the ground-truth variables.

text: feature_092 (continuous, range 0-0.80, mean 0.31) is positively associated with objective_response after adjusting for the dominant predictor feature_051.
rationale: Only feature_092 from the ground-truth set is involved, and the hypothesis proposes a marginal main effect rather than the four-way subgroup-conditional pembrolizumab effect.

text: Predicted ORR for a typical white / medicare / former-smoker / adenocarcinoma / age-65 patient at mean lab values is between 9% and 22%, varying primarily as a function of feature_051 level (highest ORR at level 0, lowest at level 2).
rationale: feature_051 is not among the ground-truth variables and the hypothesis describes a main-effect prediction unrelated to the pembrolizumab subgroup.

text: Within feature_051 = 1, switching feature_013 from 1 to 0 (with all other variables at the typical-patient profile) raises predicted ORR by an absolute >=4 percentage points.
rationale: References feature_051 and feature_013, which are not the ground-truth variables (treatment_pembrolizumab, pdl1_tps, tmb_high, stk11_mutation, sex_female).

text: Predicted ORR is not materially different (absolute |delta| < 2 percentage points) when toggling self-reported race or insurance category in the typical-patient profile.
rationale: Concerns race and insurance category, not the ground-truth variables or the female/PD-L1/TMB/STK11 subgroup interaction with pembrolizumab.

text: At least one feature in the dataset is associated with objective_response at p<0.05 in a univariate test (chi-square for categorical/binary features, Welch t-test for continuous features comparing responders vs. non-responders).
rationale: A generic univariate screen does not target the specific 5-variable conjunction or its subgroup-conditional form.

text: Increasing values of feature_051 (ordinal 0/1/2; resembles ECOG performance status) are associated with monotonically lower objective_response rate.
rationale: feature_051 is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Increasing values of feature_011 (continuous, range 0-28; resembles a comorbidity or pack-year burden index) are associated with monotonically lower objective_response rate.
rationale: feature_011 is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Patients with feature_013==1 have lower objective_response rate than those with feature_013==0.
rationale: feature_013 is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Patients with feature_067==1 have lower objective_response rate than those with feature_067==0.
rationale: feature_067 is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Patients with feature_006==1 have higher objective_response rate than those with feature_006==0 (suggesting feature_006 may be an effective therapy or favorable biomarker).
rationale: Proposes only a main effect of feature_006 alone, not the joint female/PD-L1-high/TMB-high/STK11-wildtype pembrolizumab subgroup.

text: Higher values of feature_099 (continuous, range 1.7-5.5; resembles serum albumin g/dL) are associated with higher objective_response rate.
rationale: feature_099 is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Higher values of feature_063 (right-skewed continuous, range 0.03-296.88; resembles LDH or an inflammatory marker) are associated with lower objective_response rate.
rationale: feature_063 is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: After mutual adjustment in a multivariable logistic model, feature_051 (PS-like), feature_011, feature_013, feature_067 each retain a significant negative association with objective_response, and feature_006, feature_099, feature_007, feature_039, feature_092 each retain a significant positive association with objective_response.
rationale: A main-effects multivariable logistic model omits feature_027 and cannot capture the 4-way subgroup-conditional interaction the ground truth specifies.

text: Patient age (feature_078) is not independently associated with objective_response after adjustment for performance status, comorbidity burden, treatment, and laboratory features.
rationale: feature_078 (age) is not among the ground-truth variables and the hypothesis concerns its non-association rather than the pembrolizumab subgroup effect.

text: There is a positive interaction between feature_006 (probable therapy) and feature_092 (continuous, range 0-0.8; resembles a fractional biomarker such as PD-L1 expression) on the log-odds scale: i.e., the response benefit of feature_006 increases as feature_092 increases.
rationale: Only references feature_006 and feature_092; misses treatment_pembrolizumab, stk11_mutation, and sex_female required by the 5-way subgroup-conditional ground truth.

text: The effect of feature_006 on objective_response differs by tumor histology (feature_043 adenocarcinoma vs. squamous).
rationale: Invokes histology (feature_043), which is not among the ground-truth variables, and omits pdl1_tps, stk11_mutation, sex_female, and treatment_pembrolizumab.

text: The effect of feature_006 on objective_response differs by performance status (feature_051).
rationale: Invokes performance status (feature_051), which is not in the ground truth, and omits the required pembrolizumab, PD-L1, STK11, and sex variables.

text: There is a positive interaction between feature_013 and feature_092 on the log-odds of objective_response.
rationale: Includes feature_013, which is not among the ground-truth variables, and omits treatment_pembrolizumab, tmb_high, stk11_mutation, and sex_female.

text: feature_006 and feature_013 show a multiplicative interaction (i.e., the joint effect of receiving both differs from the product of their individual effects).
rationale: Pairs feature_006 with feature_013 (not in the ground truth) and omits pembrolizumab, PD-L1, STK11, and sex variables.

text: Tumor histology (feature_043 squamous vs. adenocarcinoma) is associated with objective_response in unadjusted analysis.
rationale: Histology (feature_043) is not in the ground truth and the hypothesis ignores all five required variables.

text: Smoking status (feature_057: current/former/never) is associated with objective_response in unadjusted analysis.
rationale: Smoking status (feature_057) is not part of the ground-truth association and none of the required subgroup variables are referenced.

text: feature_006 confers an absolute ORR benefit in every tumor histology subgroup (adenocarcinoma and squamous).
rationale: Asserts a histology-invariant main effect of feature_006, which does not target the female/PD-L1-high/TMB-high/STK11-wildtype Ã— pembrolizumab subgroup interaction.

text: feature_006 confers an absolute ORR benefit in every smoking subgroup (current, former, never).
rationale: Asserts a smoking-invariant main effect of feature_006 and does not implicate pembrolizumab, PD-L1, STK11, or sex in a subgroup-conditional way.

text: Race (feature_123) is associated with objective_response in unadjusted analysis, with non-white patients having different ORR than white patients.
rationale: Race (feature_123) is not among the ground-truth variables and the hypothesis ignores the pembrolizumab/PD-L1/TMB/STK11/sex subgroup.

text: Insurance type (feature_005) is associated with objective_response, with privately-insured patients having higher ORR than Medicaid patients after adjustment for clinical features.
rationale: Concerns insurance (feature_005) vs ORR; not the pembrolizumab/PD-L1/TMB/STK11/sex variable set.

text: Receipt of feature_006 (probable therapy) is approximately equal across racial and insurance subgroups (i.e., no major treatment access disparity).
rationale: Treats feature_006 as a therapy and examines access disparities by race/insurance, not its conjunctive effect on objective_response.

text: After full adjustment for clinical features, race (feature_123) does not have a significant independent association with objective_response.
rationale: Concerns race (feature_123) and ORR; none of the ground-truth variables are involved.

text: feature_063 (right-skewed continuous, possibly LDH) shows a significant negative association with objective_response when modeled as log(feature_063), supporting its plausible interpretation as an inflammatory/tumor burden marker.
rationale: Concerns feature_063 (LDH-like) and ORR; not a ground-truth variable.

text: feature_011 shows a monotonic dose-response decrease in ORR across deciles (no clear threshold or plateau).
rationale: Concerns feature_011 and ORR; not a ground-truth variable.

text: Among other binary features (prevalence 5-50%) not yet examined, feature_007 retains an independent positive association with objective_response after adjustment for the established predictors.
rationale: Proposes an independent main effect of feature_007 on ORR after adjustment, contradicting the ground truth that no single feature individually predicts the joint-subgroup benefit.

text: Among other binary features (prevalence 5-50%) not yet examined, feature_112 has a negative independent association with objective_response after adjustment.
rationale: Concerns feature_112 and ORR; not a ground-truth variable.

text: Within the highest quartile of feature_092, feature_006==1 produces a clinically meaningful absolute ORR increase compared with feature_006==0, while in the lowest quartile of feature_092 feature_006 produces no detectable benefit (i.e., feature_092 is a predictive biomarker for the feature_006 effect).
rationale: Captures only a feature_092 Ã— feature_006 interaction on ORR and omits pembrolizumab, STK11 wildtype, and female sex required for the four-way subgroup.

text: Performance status (feature_051) does not modify the relative effect of feature_006 on objective_response (no interaction).
rationale: Asserts no interaction and centers on feature_051 as effect modifier, neither matching the four-way positive subgroup with pembrolizumab.

text: Performance status (feature_051) does not modify the slope of feature_011 on objective_response (no interaction).
rationale: Concerns feature_051 and feature_011; neither is a ground-truth variable.

text: feature_013 and feature_067 (both negative predictors) act independently on the log-odds scale (no significant interaction).
rationale: Targets feature_013 and feature_067, not the pembrolizumab/PD-L1/TMB/STK11/sex variables, and does not describe the four-way subgroup interaction.

text: A final multivariable logistic model with the feature_006 x feature_092 interaction, the seven main-effect clinical predictors (feature_051, feature_011, feature_013, feature_067, feature_099, feature_063, feature_007, feature_039), age, histology, smoking, race, and insurance reproduces the predictive-biomarker pattern: predicted ORR is essentially identical for treated vs. untreated patients at low feature_092 but rises ~6 percentage points higher for treated patients at high feature_092.
rationale: Models only a feature_006 x feature_092 interaction with main effects; it does not capture the four-way subgroup conditional on STK11-wildtype and female sex required by the ground truth.

text: In the fully adjusted model, the private-insurance vs. medicaid disparity (private patients have higher adjusted ORR) persists.
rationale: Concerns insurance status and ORR, unrelated to the pembrolizumab/PD-L1/TMB/STK11/sex subgroup association.

text: Age (feature_078) is independent of objective_response in the fully adjusted model (no association).
rationale: Concerns age and ORR, not the specified subgroup-conditional pembrolizumab effect.

text: Tumor histology (feature_043 adenocarcinoma vs squamous) is independent of objective_response in the fully adjusted model.
rationale: Concerns histology and ORR, not the specified subgroup-conditional pembrolizumab effect.

text: Smoking status (feature_057) is independent of objective_response in the fully adjusted model.
rationale: Concerns smoking status and ORR, not the specified subgroup-conditional pembrolizumab effect.

text: Higher levels of feature_051 (an ordinal 0/1/2 variable) are associated with a lower probability of objective_response.
rationale: feature_051 is not among the ground-truth variables and the hypothesis posits a main effect, not the subgroup-conditional pembrolizumab benefit.

text: Higher values of the continuous variable feature_011 are associated with a lower probability of objective_response.
rationale: feature_011 is not among the ground-truth variables involved in the association.

text: Patients with feature_013 = 1 have a lower probability of objective_response than patients with feature_013 = 0.
rationale: feature_013 is not among the ground-truth variables involved in the association.

text: Patients with feature_067 = 1 have a lower probability of objective_response than patients with feature_067 = 0.
rationale: feature_067 is not among the ground-truth variables involved in the association.

text: Patients with feature_006 = 1 have a higher probability of objective_response than patients with feature_006 = 0.
rationale: Although feature_006 (tmb_high) is one of the GT variables, this proposes a simple main effect rather than the four-way subgroup-conditional pembrolizumab effect.

text: feature_051, feature_011, feature_013, feature_067, and feature_006 each remain significant predictors of objective_response when entered together in a single multivariable logistic regression, indicating they each carry independent information.
rationale: Only feature_006 overlaps with the GT variables; the multivariable main-effects framing does not capture the subgroup-conditional treatment effect.

text: feature_007 (binary) and feature_039 (binary) are positively and independently associated with objective_response after adjustment for feature_051, feature_011, feature_013, feature_067, feature_006.
rationale: Captures only feature_007 and feature_039 of the five GT variables, omits pdl1_tps/tmb_high/stk11_mutation, and frames the effect as additive main effects rather than a four-way conditional subgroup.

text: feature_092 and feature_099 (continuous) remain positively associated with objective_response and feature_063 remains negatively associated after adjustment for the leading categorical/binary features.
rationale: Only feature_092 overlaps with the GT variable set; the hypothesis describes main-effect adjustments, not the subgroup-conditional pembrolizumab benefit.

text: Objective response rates differ by smoking status (feature_057: never vs former vs current).
rationale: feature_057 (smoking) is not among the ground-truth variables involved in the association.

text: Objective response rates differ by self-reported race (feature_123: asian/black/hispanic/other/white).
rationale: feature_123 (race) is not among the ground-truth variables involved in the association.

text: Objective response rates differ by insurance category (feature_005: medicaid/medicare/private/uninsured).
rationale: Hypothesis targets feature_005 (insurance), which is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Objective response rates differ between adenocarcinoma and squamous histology (feature_043).
rationale: Hypothesis targets feature_043 (histology), which is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: There is a positive multiplicative interaction between feature_006 and feature_039 on objective_response: patients with both =1 have higher response than expected from main effects alone.
rationale: Pairwise interaction between only tmb_high and pembrolizumab omits pdl1_tps, stk11_mutation, and sex_female, so it does not point at the same variable set as the four-way subgroup.

text: There is a positive multiplicative interaction between feature_007 and feature_039 on objective_response.
rationale: Pairwise interaction between only sex_female and pembrolizumab omits pdl1_tps, tmb_high, and stk11_mutation, so it does not identify the full ground-truth subgroup.

text: There is a positive multiplicative interaction between feature_006 and feature_007 on objective_response.
rationale: Interaction between tmb_high and sex_female omits pembrolizumab, pdl1_tps, and stk11_mutation, and would not surface the treatment-conditional subgroup effect.

text: Among the leading binary predictors (feature_006, feature_007, feature_013, feature_039, feature_067), no other pairwise interaction reaches significance after the three top pairs above are accounted for.
rationale: A negative claim about additional pairwise interactions does not identify the four-way subgroup-conditional pembrolizumab effect.

text: feature_011 shows a monotonic dose-response with objective_response across quartiles, with response decreasing from Q1 to Q4.
rationale: Hypothesis targets feature_011, which is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: feature_099 shows a monotonic dose-response with objective_response across quartiles, with response increasing from Q1 to Q4.
rationale: Hypothesis targets feature_099, which is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: feature_063 shows a monotonic dose-response with objective_response across quartiles, with response decreasing from Q1 to Q4.
rationale: Hypothesis targets feature_063, which is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: feature_092 shows a positive trend across quartiles such that the highest quartile has the highest response rate.
rationale: A marginal monotonic trend in pdl1_tps alone does not capture the conjunction of pembrolizumab, sex_female, tmb_high, and stk11_wildtype that defines the ground-truth subgroup.

text: feature_026 (ordinal 0-4) is monotonically associated with objective_response.
rationale: feature_026 is not among the ground-truth variables (treatment_pembrolizumab, pdl1_tps, tmb_high, stk11_mutation, sex_female).

text: feature_033 (ordinal 0-4) is negatively monotonically associated with objective_response.
rationale: feature_033 is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: feature_018 (ordinal 0-4) is monotonically associated with objective_response.
rationale: feature_018 is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: feature_045 (ordinal 0-4) is monotonically associated with objective_response.
rationale: feature_045 is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: feature_042 (ordinal 0-4) is monotonically associated with objective_response.
rationale: feature_042 is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: The negative effect of feature_051 (PD-L1-like ordinal) on objective_response is modified by smoking status (feature_057 ever vs never): the effect is steeper in ever-smokers.
rationale: Uses feature_051 and feature_057, neither of which is in the ground-truth variable set; pdl1_tps is feature_092 not feature_051.

text: The negative effect of feature_051 on objective_response differs by histology (feature_043 squamous vs adenocarcinoma).
rationale: Uses feature_051 and feature_043; neither corresponds to the ground-truth variables, and histology is not part of the subgroup.

text: The effect of ever-smoking on objective_response differs by self-reported race (feature_123).
rationale: Involves smoking and race (feature_123); none of these correspond to the ground-truth variables.

text: A composite biomarker score formed by sign-corrected z-scores of the top 5 continuous features (feature_011, feature_099, feature_063, feature_092, feature_044) is positively associated with objective_response and shows monotonic dose-response across quartiles.
rationale: Although feature_092 (pdl1_tps) is one of five components, the composite dilutes it with unrelated features and proposes a main-effect monotonic association rather than the treatment-conditional 4-way subgroup interaction with pembrolizumab.

text: After adjusting for the composite biomarker score, feature_051 (PD-L1-like) remains an independent predictor of objective_response.
rationale: Centers on feature_051 as a PD-L1-like predictor, but pdl1_tps is feature_092; also frames an independent main effect, not the pembrolizumab-conditional subgroup interaction.

text: In a parsimonious multivariable model that includes feature_051 (PD-L1-like), feature_011, feature_013, feature_067, feature_006, feature_007, feature_039, feature_092, and feature_099, every term retains the same direction of effect as in univariate analysis and remains statistically significant; ever_smoker (feature_057 != 'never') drops to non-significance once these clinical/biomarker features are controlled.
rationale: Proposes a main-effects multivariable model and omits stk11_mutation (feature_027); does not investigate the four-way subgroup-conditional interaction defining the ground truth.

text: After adjusting for clinical/biomarker features (feature_051, feature_011, feature_013, feature_067, feature_006, ever_smoker), Black patients have a higher probability of objective_response than white patients (i.e., the small unadjusted gap by race becomes a positive Black vs white coefficient).
rationale: Concerns race as a predictor, which is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: After adjusting for the same clinical/biomarker features, Medicaid insurance is associated with a lower probability of objective_response compared with private insurance.
rationale: Concerns Medicaid insurance, which is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: After adjustment, Asian, Hispanic and 'other' race categories do not differ significantly from white patients in objective_response.
rationale: Concerns race categories, which are not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have a higher rate of objective_response than those not receiving treatment_pembrolizumab.
rationale: Proposes a main effect of pembrolizumab, not the four-way subgroup-conditional effect, and omits pdl1_tps, tmb_high, stk11_mutation, and sex_female.

text: Patients receiving treatment_sotorasib have a higher rate of objective_response than those not receiving treatment_sotorasib.
rationale: Wrong treatment (sotorasib) and unrelated variables; does not target the pembrolizumab subgroup association.

text: Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have a higher rate of objective_response than those not receiving treatment_olaparib.
rationale: Wrong treatment (olaparib) and unrelated variables; does not target the pembrolizumab subgroup association.

text: keap1_mutation=1 reduces the magnitude of the treatment_pembrolizumab benefit on objective_response (negative interaction).
rationale: keap1_mutation is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Among treatment_pembrolizumab=1 patients, higher pdl1_tps is associated with higher objective_response (dose-response).
rationale: Captures only pembrolizumab Ã— pdl1_tps; omits the required tmb_high, stk11_mutation, and sex_female conditions of the four-way subgroup.

text: Among treatment_pembrolizumab=0 patients, pdl1_tps is not associated with objective_response (specificity check).
rationale: Specificity check on the non-pembrolizumab arm; does not propose the joint female/PD-L1-high/TMB-high/STK11-wildtype subgroup.

text: treatment_pembrolizumab benefit on objective_response increases with continuous pdl1_tps (positive multiplicative interaction on the logit scale).
rationale: Two-way pembrolizumab Ã— pdl1_tps interaction; does not include tmb_high, stk11_mutation, or sex_female required by the spec.

text: treatment_pembrolizumab provides no measurable benefit when pdl1_tps is below 0.5 (PD-L1-low subgroup).
rationale: Addresses only the PD-L1 threshold piece; omits TMB, STK11, and sex conditions needed for the joint subgroup.

text: Smoking_status modifies treatment_pembrolizumab benefit such that ever-smokers (current/former) derive larger benefit than never-smokers.

text: Histology (squamous vs adenocarcinoma) modifies treatment_pembrolizumab benefit on objective_response.
rationale: Modifier is histology rather than the specified four-way clinical/molecular subgroup.

text: Smoking_pack_years is associated with objective_response (positively, reflecting mutational burden).
rationale: Main-effect hypothesis on smoking_pack_years; involves none of the spec variables and no pembrolizumab interaction.

text: Race_ethnicity is associated with objective_response (overall test of heterogeneity).
rationale: Tests race_ethnicity main effect; unrelated to the pembrolizumab subgroup variables in the ground truth.

text: Insurance_type is associated with objective_response.
rationale: Tests insurance_type, a variable not in the ground-truth association.

rationale: Targets rural_residence and outcome, not the pembrolizumab Ã— PD-L1 Ã— TMB Ã— STK11 Ã— female subgroup.

text: Common comorbidities (diabetes, hypertension, COPD, CKD, HF, CAD, AF, VTE, autoimmune, hep B, hep C, HIV, prior malignancy, depression/anxiety, ILD) are individually associated with objective_response.
rationale: Targets comorbidities, none of which are part of the ground-truth variables.

text: More prior lines of therapy is associated with lower objective_response, and prior_chemotherapy specifically lowers response.
rationale: Targets prior lines of therapy and prior_chemotherapy, not the pembrolizumab Ã— biomarker Ã— sex subgroup.

text: After multivariable adjustment for age, sex, ECOG, stage, brain/liver/bone mets, key biomarkers, albumin, CRP, and weight loss, treatment_pembrolizumab still increases objective_response.
rationale: Proposes a main-effect adjusted model for pembrolizumab and omits the required subgroup interaction with PD-L1, TMB, STK11, and sex.

text: After multivariable adjustment, treatment_sotorasib, treatment_olaparib, and treatment_osimertinib still show no benefit on objective_response.
rationale: Concerns sotorasib, olaparib, and osimertinib rather than pembrolizumab, so it does not address the ground-truth association.

text: After adjustment plus interactions, the treatment_pembrolizumab x pdl1_tps and treatment_pembrolizumab x tmb_high interactions remain positive while pembrolizumab x stk11_mutation remains negative.
rationale: Proposes pairwise interactions with pdl1_tps, tmb_high, and stk11_mutation but omits sex_female and does not capture the four-way conjunction subgroup.

text: Sex_female remains independently positive for objective_response after adjustment.
rationale: Asserts an independent main effect of sex_female, whereas the ground truth is that sex alone is not predictive â€” only the joint subgroup matters.

text: treatment_pembrolizumab benefit is largest in the joint subgroup pdl1_tps>=0.5 AND tmb_high=1, i.e. PD-L1 and TMB act multiplicatively.
rationale: Identifies only a two-variable subgroup (pdl1_tps and tmb_high) and omits stk11_mutation=0 and sex_female=1 from the required conjunction.

text: treatment_pembrolizumab benefit on objective_response is essentially absent in stk11_mutation=1 patients regardless of PD-L1 status.
rationale: Identifies only stk11_mutation as the effect modifier and omits pdl1_tps, tmb_high, and sex_female from the joint subgroup.

text: Within the 'best ICI candidate' triple subgroup (pdl1_tps>=0.5 AND tmb_high=1 AND stk11_mutation=0), treatment_pembrolizumab markedly increases objective_response.
rationale: Captures three of the four subgroup conditions but omits sex_female=1, so it does not isolate the same conjunction subgroup as the ground truth.

text: After adjustment for ECOG, stage, brain mets, albumin, and weight loss, treatment_sotorasib still has no effect on objective_response within kras_g12c=1.

text: After the same adjustment, treatment_osimertinib has no effect on objective_response within egfr_mutation=1.
