text: Among patients with brca2_mutation=1, those receiving treatment_olaparib have a higher rate of objective_response than those not receiving olaparib.
rationale: Wrong drug and biomarker; involves treatment_olaparib and brca2_mutation, none of which appear in the ground-truth variable set.

text: Among patients with tmb_high=1, those receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have a higher rate of objective_response than those not receiving pembrolizumab.
rationale: Captures only treatment_pembrolizumab and tmb_high but omits pdl1_tps, stk11_mutation, and sex_female that jointly define the ground-truth subgroup.

text: Higher pdl1_tps amplifies the effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on objective_response (positive pembrolizumab x pdl1_tps interaction in logistic regression).
rationale: Proposes only a pembrolizumab x pdl1_tps interaction and omits tmb_high, stk11_mutation, and sex_female required by the ground-truth conjunction.

text: Among patients with pdl1_tps >= 0.5, treatment_pembrolizumab raises objective_response rate; among those with pdl1_tps < 0.5 it does not.
rationale: Stratifies on pdl1_tps alone and does not condition on tmb_high, stk11_mutation, and sex_female that jointly define the true responsive subgroup.

text: objective_response rate differs across smoking_status categories (never vs former vs current).
rationale: Concerns smoking_status and objective_response, variables that are not part of the ground-truth association.

text: The benefit of treatment_pembrolizumab on objective_response is greater in current/former smokers than in never-smokers.
rationale: Treatment effect modifier is smoking_status, which is not among the ground-truth variables (sex_female, pdl1_tps, tmb_high, stk11_mutation).

text: objective_response rate differs between adenocarcinoma and squamous histology.
rationale: Concerns histology and objective_response, variables that are not part of the ground-truth association.

text: stk11_mutation reduces (or abolishes) the benefit of treatment_pembrolizumab on objective_response (negative pembrolizumab x stk11 interaction).
rationale: Captures only treatment_pembrolizumab and stk11_mutation (with consistent direction) but omits pdl1_tps, tmb_high, and sex_female required for the ground-truth subgroup.

text: keap1_mutation reduces the benefit of treatment_pembrolizumab on objective_response (negative pembrolizumab x keap1 interaction).
rationale: Proposes a keap1Ã—pembrolizumab interaction; keap1_mutation is not in the ground-truth variable set.

text: tp53_mutation modifies the benefit of treatment_pembrolizumab on objective_response.
rationale: Proposes tp53_mutation as the effect modifier; tp53 is not in the ground-truth variable set.

text: In PD-L1 high (>=0.5) patients without stk11_mutation, pembrolizumab strongly increases response; in PD-L1 high patients with stk11_mutation the benefit is attenuated/absent.
rationale: Captures pembrolizumabÃ—PD-L1Ã—STK11 but omits the required tmb_high and sex_female predicates, so it does not identify the full four-way conjunction subgroup.

text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with higher objective_response rate (positive logistic coefficient).
rationale: Main-effect claim about albumin; does not involve pembrolizumab or the specified subgroup variables.

text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with lower objective_response rate (negative logistic coefficient).
rationale: Main-effect claim about CRP; unrelated to the pembrolizumab subgroup interaction.

text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with lower objective_response rate.
rationale: Main-effect claim about LDH; unrelated to the ground-truth variables.

text: Higher nlr (neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio) is associated with lower objective_response rate.
rationale: Main-effect claim about NLR; unrelated to the ground-truth variables.

text: Across remaining routine labs (hemoglobin, alkaline phosphatase, AST, ALT, bilirubin, creatinine, electrolytes, blood counts), no lab will show a strong main-effect association with objective_response after the inflammatory/nutritional labs are accounted for.
rationale: Negative claim about routine labs; does not address the pembrolizumabÃ—PDL1Ã—TMBÃ—STK11Ã—sex subgroup effect.

text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with lower objective_response (negative logistic coefficient).
rationale: Main-effect claim about ECOG PS; unrelated to the ground-truth variables.

text: Greater weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with lower objective_response.
rationale: Main-effect claim about weight loss; unrelated to the ground-truth variables.

text: Higher symptom grades (fatigue_grade, pain_nrs, dyspnea_grade, cough_grade, appetite_loss_grade) are each associated with lower objective_response.
rationale: Symptom grade variables are not part of the ground-truth feature set.

text: The benefit of treatment_pembrolizumab on objective_response is smaller in patients with higher ecog_ps (negative ecog x pembrolizumab interaction).
rationale: Uses ecog_ps as the modifier rather than the sex/PD-L1/TMB/STK11 conjunction.

text: stage_iv=1 is associated with lower objective_response than non-stage IV.
rationale: stage_iv is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: has_brain_mets=1 is associated with lower objective_response.
rationale: has_brain_mets is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Liver, bone, adrenal, contralateral lung mets, and pleural/pericardial effusions each individually depress objective_response rate.
rationale: Metastatic site features are not part of the ground-truth association.

text: The number of metastatic sites (sum of brain/liver/bone/adrenal/contralateral) is inversely related to objective_response.
rationale: Number of metastatic sites is unrelated to the ground-truth feature set.

text: objective_response rate differs across race_ethnicity categories.
rationale: race_ethnicity is not part of the ground-truth association.

text: objective_response rate differs across insurance_type categories (medicaid/medicare/private/uninsured).
rationale: insurance_type is not part of the ground-truth association.

text: rural_residence=1 patients have lower objective_response than urban patients.
rationale: rural_residence is not part of the ground-truth association.

text: Female patients (sex_female=1) have higher objective_response rate than males.
rationale: Proposes a sex_female main effect but omits pembrolizumab, PD-L1, TMB, and STK11, so it cannot capture the four-way subgroup-conditional treatment effect.

text: Across age_years, education_years, smoking_pack_years, and bmi, none has a meaningful main-effect association with objective_response.
rationale: Targets demographic/lifestyle main effects, not the pembrolizumab subgroup interaction or any of the spec's variables.

text: In a multivariable logistic regression, the pembrolizumab x pdl1_tps and pembrolizumab x tmb_high interactions remain positive, the pembrolizumab x stk11_mutation interaction remains negative, while the targeted therapy x matched biomarker interactions (osimertinib x egfr, sotorasib x kras_g12c, olaparib x brca2) remain null after adjustment for prognostic covariates.
rationale: Includes pembrolizumab, PD-L1, TMB, and STK11 but omits sex_female, and proposes only pairwise interactions rather than the four-way subgroup conjunction.

text: ECOG, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, weight_loss, albumin, and CRP each retain independent prognostic signal in a multivariable model.
rationale: Concerns prognostic covariates (ECOG, stage, brain mets, etc.), none of which are in the ground-truth variable set.

text: Across the 23 SNPs (snp_rs*), no SNP has a Bonferroni-significant association with objective_response (Bonferroni alpha = 0.05/23 = 0.00217).
rationale: Tests SNP main effects, none of which are part of the ground-truth association.

text: Prior treatments (chemotherapy, radiation, surgery, immunotherapy, targeted therapy) and number of prior_lines_of_therapy do not have main-effect associations with objective_response on this line.
rationale: Addresses prior treatment variables, not pembrolizumab benefit conditional on PD-L1/TMB/STK11/sex.

text: Common comorbidities (diabetes, hypertension, COPD, CKD, heart failure, autoimmune disease, ILD history, prior malignancy, depression/anxiety) do not affect objective_response rate.
rationale: Concerns comorbidities, which are not part of the ground-truth variable set.

text: Pembrolizumab benefit on objective_response is greater in females (sex_female=1) than in males (positive pembrolizumab x sex_female interaction).
rationale: Proposes only a pembrolizumab x sex_female interaction, missing PD-L1, TMB, and STK11 â€” not the four-way subgroup.

text: Within PD-L1 high (pdl1_tps>=0.5) patients, pembrolizumab raises response in current and former smokers but not in never-smokers (smoking modifies the pembrolizumab benefit even within high-PD-L1).
rationale: Conditions on PD-L1 but introduces smoking and omits TMB, STK11, and sex_female from the conjunction.

text: There is a three-way pembrolizumab x pdl1_tps x stk11_mutation interaction such that the PD-L1-driven pembrolizumab benefit is greatest in STK11 wildtype and absent in STK11 mutant.
rationale: A three-way pembrolizumab x PD-L1 x STK11 interaction misses TMB and sex_female from the four-way subgroup.

text: A refined multivariable model containing pembrolizumab and its PD-L1, TMB, and STK11 interactions plus prognostic covariates (ECOG, stage_iv, brain_mets, weight_loss, albumin, CRP, sex_female) preserves all interaction signs and significances and provides the most coherent description of objective_response in this cohort.
rationale: Names all five variables but treats sex_female as a main-effect covariate and uses only pairwise interactions, which would not identify the four-way conjunction subgroup.

text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have a higher objective_response rate than those not receiving treatment_pembrolizumab.
rationale: Tests only the main effect of pembrolizumab; omits the required pdl1_tps, tmb_high, stk11_mutation, and sex_female variables defining the subgroup.

text: Patients receiving treatment_sotorasib have a higher objective_response rate than those not receiving treatment_sotorasib.
rationale: Wrong treatment (sotorasib) and wrong variable set; does not address pembrolizumab's subgroup-conditional effect.

text: Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have a higher objective_response rate than those not receiving treatment_olaparib.
rationale: Wrong treatment (olaparib) and wrong variable set; unrelated to the pembrolizumab subgroup association.

text: Patients receiving treatment_osimertinib have a higher objective_response rate than those not receiving treatment_osimertinib.
rationale: Wrong treatment (osimertinib) and wrong variable set; unrelated to the pembrolizumab subgroup association.

text: The benefit of treatment_osimertinib on objective_response is larger in patients with egfr_mutation than in those without (positive treatment_osimertinib x egfr_mutation interaction on the log-odds scale).
rationale: Wrong treatment and unrelated variable (egfr_mutation); does not target the pembrolizumab subgroup.

text: The benefit of treatment_sotorasib on objective_response is larger in patients with kras_g12c than in those without (positive treatment_sotorasib x kras_g12c interaction on the log-odds scale).
rationale: Wrong treatment (sotorasib) and unrelated variable (kras_g12c); does not target the pembrolizumab subgroup.

text: The benefit of treatment_olaparib on objective_response is larger in patients with brca2_mutation than in those without (positive treatment_olaparib x brca2_mutation interaction on the log-odds scale).
rationale: Wrong treatment (olaparib) and unrelated variable (brca2_mutation); does not target the pembrolizumab subgroup.

text: The benefit of treatment_pembrolizumab on objective_response is larger in patients with tmb_high than in those without (positive treatment_pembrolizumab x tmb_high interaction on the log-odds scale).
rationale: Includes pembrolizumab and tmb_high but omits pdl1_tps, stk11_mutation, and sex_female required by the joint subgroup.

text: Higher pdl1_tps is associated with greater treatment_pembrolizumab benefit on objective_response (positive treatment_pembrolizumab x pdl1_tps interaction).
rationale: Includes pembrolizumab and pdl1_tps but omits tmb_high, stk11_mutation, and sex_female required by the joint subgroup.

text: Patients with pdl1_tps >= 0.5 derive substantially greater objective_response benefit from treatment_pembrolizumab than patients with pdl1_tps < 0.5.
rationale: Targets a pdl1_tps-only subgroup; omits tmb_high, stk11_mutation, and sex_female required by the ground-truth subgroup.

text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with lower objective_response.
rationale: Uses ecog_ps, not any of the spec variables.

text: stage_iv disease is associated with lower objective_response than non-stage-IV.
rationale: Uses stage_iv, not any of the spec variables.

text: has_brain_mets is associated with lower objective_response.
rationale: Uses has_brain_mets, not any of the spec variables.

text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with lower objective_response.
rationale: Uses weight_loss_pct_6mo, not any of the spec variables.

text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with higher objective_response.
rationale: Uses albumin_g_dl, not any of the spec variables.

text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with lower objective_response.
rationale: Uses crp_mg_l, not any of the spec variables.

text: sex_female is associated with higher objective_response than males.
rationale: Proposes a sex_female main effect, but the spec explicitly states no individual feature predicts the joint-subgroup benefit.

text: treatment_pembrolizumab benefit is larger in females than males (positive treatment_pembrolizumab x sex_female interaction on log-odds scale).
rationale: Proposes only a 2-way pembrolizumab x sex_female interaction, omitting pdl1_tps, tmb_high, and stk11_mutation required by the 4-way subgroup.

text: treatment_pembrolizumab benefit is larger in squamous than adenocarcinoma histology (positive treatment_pembrolizumab x squamous interaction).
rationale: Involves squamous histology, which is not among the spec variables.

text: treatment_pembrolizumab benefit is smaller (or absent) in never smokers compared with ever smokers (negative treatment_pembrolizumab x never_smoker interaction).
rationale: Involves never_smoker, which is not among the spec variables.

text: treatment_pembrolizumab benefit is reduced in patients with stk11_mutation (negative treatment_pembrolizumab x stk11_mutation interaction).
rationale: Captures only the stk11Ã—pembro piece and misses the required pdl1_tps, tmb_high, and sex_female components of the four-way subgroup.

text: treatment_pembrolizumab benefit is reduced in patients with keap1_mutation (negative treatment_pembrolizumab x keap1_mutation interaction).
rationale: keap1_mutation is not among the spec variables.

text: treatment_pembrolizumab benefit is reduced in patients with higher ecog_ps (negative treatment_pembrolizumab x ecog_ps interaction on log-odds scale).
rationale: ecog_ps is not among the spec variables.

text: treatment_pembrolizumab benefit is reduced in patients with stage_iv disease.
rationale: stage_iv is not among the spec variables.

text: treatment_pembrolizumab benefit is reduced in patients with has_brain_mets.
rationale: has_brain_mets is not among the spec variables.

text: Higher pdl1_tps is associated with higher objective_response (main effect, regardless of treatment).
rationale: Proposes a treatment-independent main effect of pdl1_tps, not the four-way subgroup-conditional pembrolizumab effect.

text: tmb_high is associated with higher objective_response (main effect, regardless of treatment).
rationale: Proposes a treatment-independent main effect of tmb_high, not the four-way subgroup-conditional pembrolizumab effect.

text: After Bonferroni correction across 23 SNPs, at least one snp_* variant shows a significant association with objective_response (alpha=0.05/23=2.17e-3).
rationale: SNP variants are not among the spec variables.

text: objective_response differs by race_ethnicity, with at least one non-white group differing significantly from white.
rationale: race_ethnicity is not among the spec variables.

text: objective_response differs by insurance_type, with privately insured patients having higher ORR than medicaid (positive private-vs-medicaid contrast).
rationale: insurance_type is not among the spec variables.

text: rural_residence is associated with lower objective_response than non-rural.
rationale: rural_residence is not among the ground-truth variables; wrong feature.

text: Higher education_years is associated with higher objective_response.
rationale: education_years is not among the ground-truth variables; wrong feature.

text: treatment_pembrolizumab receipt differs by race_ethnicity (i.e., disparity in IO assignment).
rationale: Treats treatment_pembrolizumab as the outcome (assignment disparity), not objective_response, and involves race_ethnicity which is not in the ground truth.

text: In a multivariable logistic model adjusting for ECOG, stage_iv, brain mets, age, sex, albumin, weight loss, CRP, race, the treatment_pembrolizumab x pdl1_tps interaction remains a positive, significant predictor of objective_response.
rationale: Captures only a 2-way pembrolizumab x pdl1_tps interaction and omits the required tmb_high, stk11_mutation, and sex_female variables that define the subgroup.

text: In the same multivariable model, treatment_osimertinib x egfr_mutation interaction is NOT a significant positive predictor of objective_response (refuting the conventional matched-targeted-therapy expectation).
rationale: Concerns osimertinib and EGFR, which are not part of the ground-truth association.

text: In the same multivariable model, treatment_sotorasib x kras_g12c interaction is NOT a significant positive predictor of objective_response.
rationale: Concerns sotorasib and KRAS G12C, which are not part of the ground-truth association.

text: In the same multivariable model, treatment_olaparib x brca2_mutation interaction is NOT a significant positive predictor of objective_response.
rationale: Concerns olaparib and BRCA2, which are not part of the ground-truth association.

text: Among comorbidities (diabetes, hypertension, COPD, CKD, heart failure, CAD, AF, VTE, autoimmune, hepatitis B/C, HIV, prior_malignancy, depression/anxiety, ILD), at least one is significantly associated (p<0.05) with objective_response.
rationale: Targets comorbidity variables, none of which appear in the ground-truth feature set.

text: Higher fatigue_grade is associated with lower objective_response (a patient-reported symptom prognostic).
rationale: fatigue_grade is not among the ground-truth variables; wrong feature.

text: There is a 3-way interaction such that pembrolizumab benefit is concentrated in pdl1_hi patients without stk11_mutation (negative treatment_pembrolizumab:pdl1_hi:stk11_mutation term).
rationale: Captures pembrolizumab x PD-L1-high x STK11-wt directionally but omits tmb_high and sex_female, so it does not specify the full ground-truth subgroup.

text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have a higher objective_response rate than patients not receiving treatment_pembrolizumab.
rationale: Proposes only a main effect of pembrolizumab without referencing the pdl1_tps/tmb_high/stk11/sex_female subgroup variables that define the ground-truth relationship.

text: Patients receiving treatment_sotorasib have a different objective_response rate than patients not receiving treatment_sotorasib.
rationale: Wrong treatment (sotorasib) and unrelated variables to the ground-truth association.

text: Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have a different objective_response rate than patients not receiving treatment_olaparib.
rationale: Wrong treatment (olaparib) and unrelated variables to the ground-truth association.

text: Patients receiving treatment_osimertinib have a different objective_response rate than patients not receiving treatment_osimertinib.
rationale: Wrong treatment (osimertinib) and unrelated variables to the ground-truth association.

text: treatment_osimertinib increases objective_response specifically among patients with egfr_mutation=1 (positive treatment x biomarker interaction on the log-odds scale).
rationale: Concerns osimertinib x EGFR, neither of which is part of the ground-truth pembrolizumab subgroup association.

text: treatment_sotorasib increases objective_response specifically among patients with kras_g12c=1 (positive interaction).
rationale: Concerns sotorasib x KRAS G12C, unrelated to the ground-truth pembrolizumab subgroup association.

text: treatment_olaparib increases objective_response specifically among patients with brca2_mutation=1 (positive interaction).
rationale: Concerns olaparib x BRCA2, unrelated to the ground-truth pembrolizumab subgroup association.

text: treatment_pembrolizumab increases objective_response specifically among patients with tmb_high=1 (positive interaction).
rationale: Identifies only a pembrolizumab x TMB-high interaction and omits the required pdl1_tps, stk11_mutation, and sex_female components of the joint subgroup.

text: Higher pdl1_tps is associated with higher objective_response across all patients (positive main effect).
rationale: Proposes a pdl1_tps main effect with no treatment, missing pembrolizumab and the other subgroup-defining variables.

text: The benefit of treatment_pembrolizumab on objective_response increases with higher pdl1_tps (positive interaction).
rationale: Identifies only a pembrolizumab x pdl1_tps interaction and omits the required tmb_high, stk11_mutation, and sex_female components of the joint subgroup.

text: Among patients with pdl1_tps >= 0.5 ('PD-L1 high'), treatment_pembrolizumab raises objective_response, while in pdl1_tps < 0.5 patients it does not.
rationale: Targets only PD-L1 high subgroup but omits the required TMB-high, STK11-wildtype, and female conjunction that defines the ground-truth subgroup.

text: Higher ecog_ps (worse performance status) is associated with lower objective_response.
rationale: ECOG performance status is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: stage_iv=1 patients have lower objective_response than non-stage_iv patients.
rationale: Stage IV is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Patients with has_brain_mets=1 have lower objective_response than those without.
rationale: Brain metastases is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Patients with liver_mets=1 or bone_mets=1 have lower objective_response than those without.
rationale: Liver/bone metastases are not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with higher objective_response.
rationale: Albumin is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Higher crp_mg_l (inflammation marker) is associated with lower objective_response.
rationale: CRP is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with lower objective_response.
rationale: Weight loss is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Higher nlr (neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio) is associated with lower objective_response.
rationale: NLR is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with lower objective_response.
rationale: LDH is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Higher hemoglobin_g_dl is associated with higher objective_response.
rationale: Hemoglobin is not among the ground-truth variables and no subgroup interaction with pembrolizumab is proposed.

text: Female patients (sex_female=1) have a higher objective_response rate than male patients.
rationale: Proposes a marginal main effect of sex_female alone, not the four-way subgroup interaction with pembrolizumab, PD-L1, TMB, and STK11.

text: Older age_years is associated with lower objective_response.
rationale: Age is not among the ground-truth variables and no relevant subgroup interaction is proposed.

text: Patients with smoking_status='never' have a different objective_response rate than 'former' or 'current' smokers.
rationale: Smoking status is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Black, Hispanic, Asian, or 'other' patients have a different objective_response rate than white patients.
rationale: Race/ethnicity is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Medicaid or uninsured patients have a lower objective_response rate than privately insured patients.
rationale: Insurance status is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Major comorbidities (chronic_kidney_disease, heart_failure, copd, coronary_artery_disease, diabetes_mellitus) reduce objective_response.
rationale: Comorbidities are not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Greater prior_lines_of_therapy is associated with lower objective_response.
rationale: Prior lines of therapy is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: prior_chemotherapy=1 is associated with lower objective_response than prior_chemotherapy=0.
rationale: Prior chemotherapy is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: autoimmune_disease=1 patients have a lower objective_response (since they may receive less aggressive immunotherapy or have altered immune milieu).
rationale: Autoimmune disease is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: At least one of the 23 germline SNPs (snp_rs* columns) is associated with objective_response after Bonferroni correction (p < 0.05/23 = 0.00217).
rationale: Concerns germline SNP columns, none of which are part of the ground-truth variable set.

text: Beyond the matched-target biomarkers, additional somatic alterations (tp53_mutation, keap1_mutation, pik3ca_mutation, msi_high) have a main effect on objective_response.
rationale: Targets tp53/keap1/pik3ca/msi_high main effects, not the pembrolizumab/PD-L1/TMB/STK11/sex variable set.

text: stk11_mutation reduces benefit of treatment_pembrolizumab on objective_response (negative pembrolizumab x stk11_mutation interaction).
rationale: Only proposes a pembrolizumab x stk11_mutation interaction and omits pdl1_tps, tmb_high, and sex_female required by the 4-way subgroup.

text: treatment_pembrolizumab benefit is larger in females (positive treatment_pembrolizumab x sex_female interaction).
rationale: Only proposes a pembrolizumab x sex_female interaction and omits pdl1_tps, tmb_high, and stk11_mutation required by the 4-way subgroup.

text: treatment_osimertinib provides differential benefit in patients with has_brain_mets=1 (interaction term).
rationale: Concerns osimertinib and brain metastases, neither of which are in the ground-truth variable set.

text: In a multivariable logistic regression for objective_response with adjustment for ECOG, stage IV, brain mets, albumin, weight loss, CRP, sex, age, PD-L1, TMB, STK11 and pembrolizumab interactions, the predictors that remain independently associated with response are: ecog_ps (negative), stage_iv (negative), has_brain_mets (negative), albumin_g_dl (positive), weight_loss_pct_6mo (negative), crp_mg_l (negative), sex_female (positive), and the pembrolizumab x pdl1_tps, pembrolizumab x tmb_high (positive), and pembrolizumab x stk11_mutation (negative) interactions.
rationale: Specifies only main effects and pairwise pembrolizumab interactions in a logistic model, which would not capture the 4-way female/PD-L1-high/TMB-high/STK11-wildtype subgroup-conditional effect, and explicitly claims independent associations contrary to the truth that none individually predicts the joint-subgroup magnitude.

text: Higher feature_078 (a continuous variable with range 30-90 and mean ~65, consistent with patient age in years) is positively associated with longer pfs_months.
rationale: Targets feature_078 (age), which is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Higher feature_057 (ordinal with values 0/1/2, consistent with ECOG performance status) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
rationale: Targets feature_057 (ECOG), which is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: feature_007 is constant across all patients and contributes no variance to pfs_months.
rationale: Concerns feature_007 being constant; unrelated to the ground-truth variables or relationship.

text: Higher feature_013 (continuous, mean 42.7, max 3622, heavy right-skewed — consistent with serum PSA in a prostate cancer cohort) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
rationale: Targets feature_013 (PSA), which is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Higher feature_009 (continuous, mean 3.80, range ~2-5.5 — consistent with serum albumin in g/dL) is associated with longer pfs_months.
rationale: Targets feature_009 (albumin), which is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Higher feature_006 (continuous, mean 3.84, max 24.6) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
rationale: Targets feature_006, which is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Patients with feature_051 = 1 (a binary indicator with ~55% prevalence) have shorter pfs_months than patients with feature_051 = 0.
rationale: Targets feature_051, which is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: feature_051 = 1 is associated with substantially higher feature_013 (PSA-like) values than feature_051 = 0, suggesting feature_051 marks a more advanced or treatment-resistant disease state.
rationale: Concerns association between feature_051 and feature_013, neither of which is in the ground truth, and does not involve pfs_months.

text: Higher feature_067 (integer values 6-10, consistent with Gleason score) is associated with shorter pfs_months independent of age and ECOG.
rationale: Proposes a main effect of gleason_score on pfs_months, omitting treatment_olaparib, brca2_mutation, psma_high, and visceral_mets and the subgroup-conditional interaction.

text: Patients with feature_109 = 1 (binary, ~10% prevalence) have longer pfs_months than feature_109 = 0 patients.
rationale: Proposes a marginal main effect of brca2_mutation on pfs_months, missing the treatment_olaparib interaction and the psma_high/gleason/visceral_mets subgroup conjunction.

text: Patients with feature_039 = 1 (binary, ~10% prevalence) have longer pfs_months than feature_039 = 0 patients.
rationale: Proposes a main effect of olaparib only; misses the four subgroup-defining variables (BRCA2, PSMA, Gleason, visceral mets) and the subgroup-conditional functional form.

text: feature_109 and feature_039 act synergistically: patients with BOTH features = 1 have substantially longer pfs_months than patients with either feature alone, beyond an additive expectation. Either feature alone provides little to no benefit.
rationale: Captures only the BRCA2 Ã— olaparib interaction; omits the required PSMA-high, Gleason â‰¤7, and no-visceral-mets predicates that define the active subgroup.

text: feature_018 (categorical with values white/black/hispanic/asian/other, consistent with self-reported race/ethnicity) is associated with pfs_months: at least one race category differs from the others.

text: feature_078 has a significant quadratic component beyond its linear effect (the marginal age benefit increases at older ages).
rationale: Concerns a quadratic age effect, unrelated to the olaparib subgroup interaction.

text: After adjustment, feature_067 (Gleason-like, range 6-10) is associated with shorter pfs_months despite a null univariable effect.
rationale: Proposes a Gleason main effect on pfs_months, not the four-way subgroup-conditional olaparib effect.

text: feature_067 is not associated with feature_051 prevalence or with feature_013 levels.
rationale: Tests Gleason's association with other features, not pfs_months or the olaparib subgroup.

text: feature_009 has a small significant quadratic component.
rationale: Quadratic effect of an unrelated feature; does not address the spec variables or interaction.

text: feature_016, feature_071, feature_026, feature_096, feature_033, and feature_064 (other ordinal features) have no significant adjusted effect on pfs_months.
rationale: Concerns null effects of unrelated ordinal features; does not address the olaparib subgroup.

text: A 10-feature multivariable model with feature_078, feature_078^2, feature_057, log feature_013, feature_006, feature_009, feature_109, feature_039, feature_092, and feature_067 explains over 97% of the variance in pfs_months.
rationale: Main-effects-only multivariable model omits feature_027 and feature_043 and proposes no interaction, so it cannot capture the 4-way subgroup-conditional effect.

text: After fitting the 10-feature model, feature_027 (binary, prevalence 0.60) shows a small positive residual association with pfs_months.
rationale: Proposes a small residual main effect of feature_027, not the conjunction-defined subgroup interaction with olaparib.

text: After fitting the 10-feature model, feature_043 (binary, prevalence 0.20) shows a small negative residual association with pfs_months.
rationale: Proposes a residual main effect of feature_043, not the four-way subgroup-conditional olaparib effect on pfs_months.

text: After fitting the 10-feature model, feature_022 (binary, prevalence 0.05) shows a small negative residual association with pfs_months.
rationale: feature_022 is not among the ground-truth variables and the hypothesis describes a main-effect residual association, not the olaparib subgroup interaction.

text: Adding feature_027, feature_043, and feature_022 to the 10-feature multivariable model retains all prior coefficient signs/magnitudes and yields R^2 above 0.973, with the new binaries each remaining significant.
rationale: Adding psma_high and visceral_mets as main-effect covariates does not capture the subgroup-conditional olaparib effect requiring brca2_mutation, gleason_score, and treatment_olaparib together.

text: feature_027's positive association with pfs_months is detectable only after multivariable adjustment (univariable p>0.25), indicating it is masked by other features in the unadjusted analysis.
rationale: Hypothesis describes a marginal/adjusted main effect of psma_high alone, not the conjunctive BRCA2/PSMA/Gleason/visceral-mets subgroup modifying olaparib's effect on PFS.

text: feature_022's negative association is also detectable only after adjustment (univariable p=0.57), marking another suppressor relationship.
rationale: feature_022 is not part of the ground-truth variable set, and a suppressor main effect does not correspond to the olaparib subgroup-conditional relationship.

text: Higher feature_078 (a continuous demographic-like variable, range 30-90, plausibly age in years) is associated with longer pfs_months.
rationale: feature_078 is not among the ground-truth variables (treatment_olaparib, brca2_mutation, psma_high, gleason_score, visceral_mets).

text: Mean pfs_months differs across the five categories of feature_018 (race: white, hispanic, black, asian, other).
rationale: feature_018 (race) is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Mean pfs_months differs across the four categories of feature_045 (insurance: medicare, private, medicaid, uninsured).
rationale: feature_045 (insurance) is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: feature_057 (3-level integer 0/1/2) is monotonically associated with pfs_months, with higher categories showing shorter pfs_months (consistent with a stage- or performance-status-like severity score).
rationale: feature_057 is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: feature_067 (5-level integer 6-10, plausibly Gleason score for prostate cancer) is negatively associated with pfs_months: higher Gleason -> shorter pfs_months.
rationale: Although feature_067 (gleason_score) is one of the ground-truth variables, this hypothesis proposes a main-effect association rather than the subgroup-conditional olaparib treatment effect, and omits the other required variables.

text: feature_051 (binary, prevalence 55%) is associated with shorter pfs_months when the indicator is positive.
rationale: feature_051 is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Among other binary features with prevalence 40-60% (potentially treatments or major comorbidities), several beyond feature_051 will have detectable associations with pfs_months after adjustment for feature_078.
rationale: Proposes vague adjusted main-effect screening over unspecified binary features and does not identify the specific olaparib Ã— BRCA2 Ã— PSMA Ã— Gleason Ã— visceral-mets subgroup interaction.

text: feature_013 (right-skewed continuous, range 0.12-3622) is negatively associated with pfs_months (higher values -> shorter PFS), consistent with a tumor-burden marker.
rationale: feature_013 is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: feature_006 (continuous, range 0-24.6, mean 3.84) is negatively associated with pfs_months.
rationale: feature_006 is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: feature_009 (continuous, range 1.5-5.5, mean 3.80, plausibly serum albumin) is POSITIVELY associated with pfs_months (higher -> longer PFS, consistent with a protective nutritional/general-health marker).
rationale: feature_009 (albumin) is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: feature_092 (continuous, range 48-830, mean 224) is negatively associated with pfs_months on the log scale.
rationale: Names feature_092 only; does not reference treatment_olaparib, BRCA2, PSMA, Gleason, or visceral mets.

text: After adjusting for feature_078 (the dominant correlate), only a small subset of features (feature_057, feature_051, feature_006, feature_013, feature_009, feature_092) retain large, clinically meaningful effects on pfs_months; most of the 122 features will be null contributors.
rationale: Lists feature_078/057/051/006/013/009/092 â€” none of the ground-truth variables (feature_039/109/027/067/043) appear.

text: The positive feature_078 (age-like) effect on pfs_months is attenuated in patients with worse feature_057 categories: feature_078 x feature_057 interaction is negative.
rationale: Concerns a feature_078 x feature_057 interaction; none of the ground-truth columns are involved.

text: The negative effect of feature_013 on pfs_months is concentrated in feature_051=0 patients and is attenuated when feature_051=1 (feature_051 x feature_013 interaction is positive).
rationale: Concerns feature_013 x feature_051; unrelated to the olaparib/BRCA2/PSMA/Gleason/visceral-mets subgroup.

text: The protective effect of feature_009 differs between feature_051=0 and feature_051=1 patients (feature_051 x feature_009 interaction is non-zero).
rationale: Concerns feature_009 x feature_051; unrelated to any ground-truth variable.

text: The negative feature_006 effect differs by feature_051 status (interaction non-zero).
rationale: Concerns feature_006 x feature_051; unrelated to any ground-truth variable.

text: feature_109 (binary, 10% prevalence) and feature_039 (binary, 10% prevalence) interact synergistically: the combination feature_109=1 AND feature_039=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than the sum of their individual effects (positive multiplicative interaction).
rationale: Captures the olaparib x BRCA2 synergy with correct positive direction, but omits the required PSMA-high, Gleason<=7, and no-visceral-mets conditioning variables, so it does not point at the same column set.

text: The negative feature_051 effect differs across feature_057 severity strata.
rationale: Concerns feature_051 x feature_057; none of the ground-truth columns are involved.

text: The feature_051 effect on pfs_months differs by feature_018 (race), with at least one race showing a meaningfully different magnitude.
rationale: Concerns feature_051 x feature_018 (race); none of the ground-truth columns are involved.

text: The feature_057 severity effect on pfs_months differs by feature_018 (race), reflecting heterogeneous prognostic gradients across racial groups.
rationale: Concerns feature_057 x feature_018 (race); none of the ground-truth columns are involved.

text: feature_045 (insurance) has a residual effect on pfs_months after adjusting for feature_018 (race) and feature_078 (age).
rationale: Targets insurance/race/age covariates and ignores the olaparib Ã— BRCA2/PSMA/Gleason/visceral-mets subgroup interaction on pfs_months.

text: The race effect on pfs_months differs across insurance strata (feature_018 x feature_045 interaction non-zero).
rationale: Proposes a race Ã— insurance interaction unrelated to the olaparib treatment effect within the BRCA2/PSMA/Gleason/visceral-mets subgroup.

text: A multivariable model containing feature_078, feature_057, feature_051, feature_006, feature_013, feature_009, feature_092, feature_109, feature_039, feature_043, feature_027, feature_018, feature_045, and feature_067 will explain the great majority (>=90%) of variance in pfs_months, confirming that the prognostic signal is dominated by feature_078 plus a sparse panel of biomarkers.
rationale: Although the variable list includes the relevant biomarkers and treatment, the hypothesis is about overall variance explained and a sparse main-effect panel, not the subgroup-conditional olaparib effect with the required positive direction.

text: Within the worst-prognosis subgroup (feature_057=2), there is no detectable racial disparity in pfs_months.
rationale: Examines racial disparity within a prognostic subgroup, not the olaparib benefit conditional on BRCA2-mutant, PSMA-high, Gleason â‰¤7, no visceral mets.

text: Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have longer mean pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_olaparib (positive direction).
rationale: Proposes a main effect of olaparib on PFS, missing the BRCA2/PSMA/Gleason/visceral_mets subgroup conditioning that defines the ground truth.

text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have longer mean pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_pembrolizumab.
rationale: Wrong treatment (pembrolizumab) and no mention of the BRCA2/PSMA/Gleason/visceral_mets subgroup.

text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
rationale: Involves ecog_ps, none of the spec's variables.

text: Higher psa_ng_ml is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
rationale: Involves psa_ng_ml, none of the spec's variables.

text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months (positive correlation).
rationale: Involves albumin_g_dl, none of the spec's variables.

text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
rationale: Involves ldh_u_l, none of the spec's variables.

text: Patients with mcrpc=1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with mcrpc=0 (negative direction).
rationale: Involves mcrpc, not in the spec's variable set.

text: Patients with visceral_mets=1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with visceral_mets=0 (negative direction).
rationale: Tests a main effect of visceral_mets on PFS, not the olaparib subgroup-conditional effect, and omits treatment_olaparib, brca2_mutation, psma_high, gleason_score.

text: There is a positive interaction between brca2_mutation and treatment_olaparib on pfs_months: BRCA2-mutated patients receiving olaparib have substantially longer PFS than expected from main effects alone.
rationale: Captures only a BRCA2 Ã— olaparib interaction; omits psma_high, gleason_score, and visceral_mets, which are required predicates of the ground-truth subgroup.

text: There is a positive interaction between msi_high and treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months: MSI-high patients benefit from pembrolizumab more than MSI-low patients.
rationale: Wrong treatment and biomarker (pembrolizumab Ã— MSI-high); none of the spec's variables are involved.

text: There is a negative interaction between ar_v7_positive and treatment_enzalutamide on pfs_months: AR-V7+ patients derive less benefit (or harm) from enzalutamide than AR-V7- patients.
rationale: Targets ar_v7_positive Ã— enzalutamide, not the olaparib/BRCA2/PSMA/Gleason/visceral_mets subgroup.

text: There is a negative interaction between ar_v7_positive and treatment_abiraterone on pfs_months: AR-V7+ patients derive less benefit from abiraterone than AR-V7- patients.
rationale: Targets ar_v7_positive Ã— abiraterone, unrelated to the olaparib subgroup ground truth.

text: There is a positive interaction between psma_high and treatment_lu177_psma on pfs_months: PSMA-high tumors respond better to PSMA-targeted radioligand therapy.
rationale: Uses psma_high but pairs it with lu177_psma rather than olaparib, and omits BRCA2/Gleason/visceral_mets.

text: Higher age_years is associated with longer pfs_months (unexpected positive correlation in this dataset).
rationale: Concerns age_years, none of the ground-truth variables.

text: After multivariable adjustment for age, ECOG, mCRPC, visceral_mets, PSA, albumin, LDH, the BRCA2 x olaparib interaction term remains a strong positive predictor of pfs_months.
rationale: Captures only BRCA2 Ã— olaparib; omits psma_high and gleason_score, and adjusts for (rather than conditions on) visceral_mets, so it does not point at the full 4-way subgroup.

text: After multivariable adjustment, age_years remains positively associated with pfs_months.
rationale: About age_years, not the ground-truth variables.

text: After multivariable adjustment, none of the targeted-therapy main effects (enzalutamide, abiraterone, docetaxel, lu177_psma, pembrolizumab) is materially associated with pfs_months.
rationale: Asserts null effects for treatments and does not even include olaparib among them, contradicting and not identifying the ground truth.

text: There is no clinically meaningful interaction between mcrpc and any treatment on pfs_months.
rationale: Concerns mcrpc Ã— treatment interactions, not the BRCA2/PSMA/Gleason/visceral_mets subgroup of olaparib effect.

text: None of the listed comorbidities (diabetes_mellitus, hypertension, copd, chronic_kidney_disease, heart_failure, coronary_artery_disease, atrial_fibrillation, venous_thromboembolism_history, autoimmune_disease, hepatitis_b/c_history, hiv_positive, prior_malignancy, depression_anxiety_diagnosis, interstitial_lung_disease_history) is associated with pfs_months.
rationale: Concerns comorbidities, none of the ground-truth variables.

text: Among the routine lab values, alkaline_phosphatase_u_l and total_bilirubin_mg_dl are inversely associated with pfs_months (markers of liver/bone disease burden).
rationale: Concerns alkaline phosphatase and bilirubin, none of the ground-truth variables.

text: Symptom grades (fatigue_grade, pain_nrs, dyspnea_grade, cough_grade, appetite_loss_grade) are inversely associated with pfs_months.
rationale: Symptom grades are unrelated to the olaparib/BRCA2/PSMA/Gleason/visceral-mets subgroup interaction on pfs_months.

text: Prior therapies (prior_chemotherapy, prior_radiation, prior_surgery, prior_immunotherapy, prior_targeted_therapy) and prior_lines_of_therapy are inversely associated with pfs_months.
rationale: Prior therapy variables are not the spec's variables and don't capture the four-way subgroup-conditional olaparib effect.

text: Mean pfs_months differs across race_ethnicity categories.
rationale: Race/ethnicity is not among the spec variables and does not address the olaparib subgroup interaction.

text: Mean pfs_months differs across insurance_type categories (uninsured shorter than private/medicare).
rationale: Insurance type is not in the spec and doesn't address the BRCA2/PSMA/Gleason/visceral-mets subgroup effect of olaparib.

text: Patients with rural_residence=1 have shorter pfs_months than urban residents.
rationale: Rural residence is not a spec variable and doesn't capture the olaparib subgroup effect.

text: Among the 25 SNP variables, none shows a real association with pfs_months beyond what is expected by chance.
rationale: SNP variables are unrelated to the olaparib subgroup association.

text: Restricting analysis to mcrpc=1, the AR-V7 x enzalutamide interaction predicts pfs_months (negative interaction).
rationale: AR-V7 x enzalutamide is a different treatment/biomarker pair than the olaparib/BRCA2/PSMA/Gleason/visceral-mets effect.

text: Restricting analysis to mcrpc=1, the AR-V7 x abiraterone interaction predicts pfs_months (negative interaction).
rationale: AR-V7 x abiraterone is unrelated to the olaparib subgroup-conditional effect specified.

text: Restricting analysis to mcrpc=1, the PSMA-high x lu177-PSMA interaction predicts pfs_months (positive interaction).
rationale: PSMA-high x Lu177-PSMA targets a different treatment than olaparib and omits BRCA2/Gleason/visceral-mets conditions.

text: Restricting analysis to mcrpc=1, the BRCA2 x olaparib interaction remains strongly positive.
rationale: Identifies BRCA2 x olaparib but does not condition on PSMA-high, Gleason<=7, and absence of visceral metastases, so it captures only the marginal BRCA2 effect rather than the spec's four-way subgroup.

text: The age x pfs_months relationship holds within every ECOG stratum (not confounded by performance status).
rationale: Concerns age and ECOG, none of which are the ground-truth variables (olaparib/BRCA2/PSMA/Gleason/visceral).

text: ECOG x treatment_olaparib interaction predicts pfs_months (poor PS dampens benefit).
rationale: Uses ECOG as the effect modifier rather than the BRCA2/PSMA/Gleason/visceral subgroup defining the true effect.

text: Albumin x treatment_olaparib interaction predicts pfs_months.
rationale: Albumin is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Number of concomitant treatments (n_tx) is associated with pfs_months.
rationale: n_tx is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Among non-prostate-canonical gene alterations (her2_amplification, met_exon14_skipping, ret_fusion, ros1_fusion, braf_v600e, ntrk_fusion, nrg1_fusion, fgfr_alteration, cdkn2a_loss, tp53_mutation, keap1_mutation, pik3ca_mutation, pten_loss), none is independently associated with pfs_months.
rationale: Concerns unrelated gene alterations, not the BRCA2/PSMA/Gleason/visceral/olaparib structure.

text: Sex_female=1 patients differ from sex_female=0 patients in pfs_months.
rationale: Sex is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: The BRCA2 x olaparib effect on pfs_months is similar across all three ECOG strata (i.e., does not require performance-status restriction).
rationale: Asserts no subgroup restriction across ECOG and ignores the actual PSMA/Gleason/visceral conditioning that defines the true effect.

text: The BRCA2 x olaparib effect is larger in older patients (because PFS itself is longer at older ages, the absolute benefit may scale).
rationale: Proposes age as the modifier of BRCA2Ã—olaparib instead of PSMA, Gleason, and visceral metastases.

text: BRCA2_mutation has no independent prognostic effect on pfs_months among patients NOT receiving olaparib.
rationale: Targets BRCA2's prognostic effect in non-olaparib patients, not olaparib's subgroup-conditional benefit, and omits PSMA/Gleason/visceral.

text: Olaparib has no independent treatment effect on pfs_months among BRCA2-NEGATIVE patients.
rationale: Only states a null effect in BRCA2-negative patients and omits the PSMA-high/Gleasonâ‰¤7/no-visceral conditions that define the true subgroup effect.

text: Visceral_mets is more strongly associated with shorter pfs_months in non-mCRPC patients than in mCRPC patients.
rationale: Targets visceral_mets Ã— mCRPC interaction on PFS; does not implicate olaparib or the BRCA2/PSMA/Gleason subgroup.

text: In a comprehensive multivariable OLS model of pfs_months including age, ECOG, mCRPC, visceral_mets, PSA, albumin, LDH, BRCA2, treatment_olaparib, BRCA2 x olaparib interaction, AR-V7, MSI-high, PSMA-high, and the other five treatments, the dominant predictors are age (positive), ECOG (negative), mCRPC (negative), albumin (positive), LDH (negative), PSA (negative), and the BRCA2 x olaparib interaction (positive).
rationale: Names BRCA2 Ã— olaparib with correct positive direction but specifies only a 2-way interaction in OLS, omitting the PSMA-high/Gleason â‰¤ 7/no-visceral conditioning needed to identify the 4-way subgroup-conditional effect.

text: After full multivariable adjustment, treatments enzalutamide, abiraterone, docetaxel, lu177-psma, pembrolizumab show no individual main effect on pfs_months.
rationale: Concerns null main effects of unrelated treatments; does not address olaparib's subgroup-conditional benefit.

text: After multivariable adjustment, ar_v7_positive itself is associated with slightly shorter pfs_months (residual prognostic effect, not modified by treatment).
rationale: AR-V7 is not part of the ground-truth variable set, and it claims a non-treatment-modifying main effect.

text: After multivariable adjustment, psma_high is associated with slightly longer pfs_months as a prognostic marker (independent of Lu177 use).
rationale: Treats psma_high as a stand-alone prognostic main effect rather than as part of the olaparib subgroup interaction; explicitly says independent of treatment.

text: Across all hypotheses tested, the only large, replicable, treatment-relevant signal is the BRCA2_mutation x treatment_olaparib interaction increasing pfs_months by ~1.5 months.
rationale: Names BRCA2 Ã— olaparib with correct positive direction but states this is the only signal, omitting PSMA/Gleason/visceral conditioning and thus failing to identify the true 4-way subgroup-conditional structure.

text: The strongest prognostic signals for pfs_months are age_years (positive), ECOG_PS (negative), mCRPC (negative), albumin_g_dl (positive), and LDH_u_l (negative); together these explain >90% of PFS variance.
rationale: Lists baseline prognostic factors only; no reference to olaparib or the BRCA2/PSMA/Gleason/visceral subgroup.

text: Several biomarker-treatment pairings biologically expected to show interaction (msi_high x pembrolizumab, ar_v7_positive x enzalutamide/abiraterone, psma_high x lu177-psma) show no detectable effect-modification on pfs_months in this cohort.
rationale: Considers only MSI/AR-V7/PSMA Ã— non-olaparib treatment pairings and asserts null effect-modification, neither addressing the ground-truth variable set nor matching its direction.

text: Race_ethnicity, insurance_type, rural_residence, education_years, and the 25 SNP variables show no clinically meaningful association with pfs_months.
rationale: Variables (race, insurance, rural residence, education, SNPs) are entirely outside the ground-truth variable set.

text: Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have longer mean pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_olaparib (positive main effect).
rationale: Proposes a main effect of olaparib only and omits the required subgroup variables (BRCA2, PSMA, Gleason, visceral mets) that define the conditional relationship.

text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have longer mean pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_pembrolizumab (positive main effect).
rationale: Names pembrolizumab rather than olaparib, so the treatment variable does not match the ground truth.

text: Patients receiving treatment_docetaxel have shorter mean pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_docetaxel (negative main effect, reflecting selection of docetaxel for higher-risk disease).
rationale: Names docetaxel rather than olaparib, so the treatment variable does not match the ground truth.

text: Patients receiving treatment_enzalutamide have a different mean pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_enzalutamide.
rationale: Names enzalutamide rather than olaparib, so the treatment variable does not match the ground truth.

text: Patients receiving treatment_abiraterone have a different mean pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_abiraterone.
rationale: Names abiraterone rather than olaparib, so the treatment variable does not match the ground truth.

text: Patients receiving treatment_lu177_psma have a different mean pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_lu177_psma.
rationale: Names lu177-PSMA rather than olaparib, so the treatment variable does not match the ground truth.

text: Higher age_years is associated with shorter pfs_months (older patients have worse prognosis).
rationale: Concerns age, which is not among the ground-truth variables driving the olaparib subgroup effect.

text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months.
rationale: Concerns ECOG performance status, which is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Higher psa_ng_ml is associated with shorter pfs_months.
rationale: Concerns PSA, which is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months.
rationale: Concerns albumin, which is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months.
rationale: Names a different feature (weight_loss_pct_6mo) and an unrelated main-effect direction; does not target the olaparib/BRCA2/PSMA/Gleason/visceral-mets subgroup.

text: mcrpc=1 (castration-resistant disease) is associated with shorter pfs_months than mcrpc=0.
rationale: Concerns mcrpc as a prognostic factor, none of the ground-truth variables or the subgroup-conditional olaparib effect.

text: treatment_olaparib improves pfs_months specifically in patients with brca2_mutation=1 (positive treatment-by-biomarker interaction).
rationale: Identifies olaparib and BRCA2 but omits psma_high, gleason_score, and visceral_mets, so it does not specify the same subgroup-conditional relationship.

text: treatment_pembrolizumab improves pfs_months specifically in patients with msi_high=1 (positive treatment-by-biomarker interaction).
rationale: Targets pembrolizumab and MSI-high, completely different variables from the olaparib/BRCA2/PSMA/Gleason/visceral-mets ground truth.

text: treatment_lu177_psma improves pfs_months specifically in patients with psma_high=1 (positive treatment-by-biomarker interaction).
rationale: Concerns Lu-177-PSMA and psma_high; wrong treatment and missing brca2_mutation, gleason_score, and visceral_mets.

text: After adjusting for prognostic covariates (age_years, ecog_ps, psa_ng_ml, albumin_g_dl, weight_loss_pct_6mo, mcrpc), treatment_olaparib has a significant positive interaction with brca2_mutation on pfs_months, while its main effect in brca2_mutation=0 patients is null.
rationale: A 2-way olaparibÃ—BRCA2 interaction model with prognostic adjustment still ignores psma_high, gleason_score, and visceral_mets, so it cannot identify the conjunctive subgroup.

text: In a multivariable OLS model, the marginal main effects of treatment_enzalutamide, treatment_abiraterone, treatment_docetaxel, and treatment_lu177_psma on pfs_months are not significantly different from zero (treatments roughly balanced across prognostic risk).
rationale: Discusses main effects of unrelated treatments and balance, not the olaparib subgroup-conditional effect.

text: After adjusting for prognostic covariates, brca2_mutation has no main effect on pfs_months independent of olaparib treatment (its prognostic value is conditional on therapy).
rationale: Addresses BRCA2's main effect, not olaparib's effect within the BRCA2/PSMA-high/Gleasonâ‰¤7/no-visceral-mets subgroup.

text: In BRCA2-mutated patients (brca2_mutation=1), treatment_olaparib increases pfs_months by approximately 1.4 months after adjustment for prognostic covariates.
rationale: Restricts only on BRCA2 and ignores psma_high, gleason_score, and visceral_mets, so it does not isolate the actual subgroup where olaparib helps.

text: In BRCA2-wildtype patients (brca2_mutation=0), treatment_olaparib has no significant effect on pfs_months after adjustment (i.e., no off-target benefit, and no off-target harm).
rationale: A null-effect claim in BRCA2-wildtypes does not name or stratify on psma_high, gleason_score, or visceral_mets and so does not characterize the ground-truth subgroup.

text: In MSI-high patients, treatment_pembrolizumab does not significantly change pfs_months after adjustment for prognostic covariates.
rationale: Concerns pembrolizumab and MSI-high, not olaparib in the BRCA2/PSMA/Gleason/visceral_mets subgroup.

text: treatment_enzalutamide has a smaller (less positive or more negative) effect on pfs_months in ar_v7_positive=1 patients than in ar_v7_positive=0 patients (negative interaction).
rationale: Concerns enzalutamide and AR-V7, not olaparib in the specified subgroup.

text: treatment_abiraterone has a smaller effect on pfs_months in ar_v7_positive=1 patients than in ar_v7_positive=0 patients (negative interaction).
rationale: Concerns abiraterone and AR-V7, not olaparib in the specified subgroup.

text: treatment_docetaxel has a more positive effect on pfs_months in patients with visceral_mets=1 than visceral_mets=0 (chemotherapy preferred for high-burden visceral disease).
rationale: Concerns docetaxel and visceral_mets only, missing olaparib, BRCA2, PSMA, and Gleason variables.

text: Mean pfs_months differs across race_ethnicity categories (white, black, hispanic, asian, other).
rationale: Concerns race_ethnicity main effect, unrelated to the olaparib subgroup association.

text: Mean pfs_months differs across insurance_type categories (medicare, private, medicaid, uninsured).

text: Patients with insurance_type='uninsured' have shorter mean pfs_months than patients with insurance_type='private'.
rationale: Tests insurance_type contrast, unrelated to the olaparib/BRCA2/PSMA/Gleason/visceral_mets subgroup.

text: Among the 25 SNP variants in the dataset, at least one is associated with pfs_months at the Bonferroni-corrected significance threshold (overall screen).
rationale: Genome-wide SNP screen does not target the olaparib treatment subgroup interaction with BRCA2/PSMA/Gleason/visceral_mets.

text: snp_rs1045642 (ABCB1) is associated with pfs_months.
rationale: Tests a specific ABCB1 SNP main effect, unrelated to the ground-truth variables.

text: snp_rs429358 (APOE) is associated with pfs_months.
rationale: APOE SNP is unrelated to the olaparib/BRCA2/PSMA/Gleason/visceral-mets variables in the ground-truth subgroup.

text: snp_rs1800629 (TNF-alpha) is associated with pfs_months.
rationale: TNF-alpha SNP is unrelated to the variables specified in the ground-truth association.

text: In a multivariable OLS adjusting for ecog_ps, mcrpc, visceral_mets, log_psa, albumin_g_dl, hemoglobin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, nlr, ecog_ps remains independently associated with shorter pfs_months.
rationale: ECOG_PS main effect is not among the ground-truth variables and does not target the olaparib subgroup interaction.

text: In the same multivariable model, albumin_g_dl is independently associated with longer pfs_months.
rationale: Albumin main effect is unrelated to the olaparib/BRCA2/PSMA/Gleason/visceral-mets ground-truth association.

text: The benefit of treatment_olaparib in brca2_mutation=1 patients vs brca2_mutation=0 patients (interaction effect on pfs_months) persists after adjusting for ecog_ps and mcrpc.
rationale: Tests only the olaparibÃ—BRCA2 interaction and omits the required psma_high, gleason_score, and visceral_mets conjunction that defines the true subgroup.

text: The benefit of treatment_pembrolizumab in msi_high=1 vs msi_high=0 patients (interaction) persists after adjusting for ecog_ps and mcrpc.
rationale: Targets pembrolizumab and MSI-high, not the olaparib/BRCA2/PSMA/Gleason/visceral-mets relationship.

text: The insurance_type disparity (uninsured vs private patients) in pfs_months persists after adjusting for ecog_ps, mcrpc, visceral_mets, and log_psa.
rationale: Concerns insurance_type disparity, none of which are the ground-truth variables.

text: After multivariable adjustment, treatment_docetaxel remains negatively associated with pfs_months (consistent with selection of sicker patients for docetaxel rather than direct harm).
rationale: Concerns docetaxel rather than the olaparib subgroup-conditional effect.

text: Mean pfs_months differs between patients receiving treatment_enzalutamide and those who are not.
rationale: Wrong treatment variable (enzalutamide vs olaparib) and lacks the BRCA2/PSMA/Gleason/visceral-mets subgroup conditioning.

text: Mean pfs_months differs between patients receiving treatment_abiraterone and those who are not.
rationale: Wrong treatment variable (abiraterone vs olaparib) and lacks the required subgroup conditioning.

text: Mean pfs_months differs between patients receiving treatment_docetaxel and those who are not.
rationale: Wrong treatment variable (docetaxel vs olaparib) and lacks the required subgroup conditioning.

text: Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have higher mean pfs_months than patients not receiving it (PARP inhibitor activity in BRCA2-enriched prostate cohort).
rationale: Identifies the correct treatment and direction but proposes a main-effect comparison without conditioning on PSMA-high, Gleason<=7, and no-visceral-mets, so it would not capture the subgroup-conditional ground truth.

text: Patients receiving treatment_lu177_psma have higher mean pfs_months than patients not receiving it.
rationale: Wrong treatment variable (Lu-177 PSMA vs olaparib).

text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have higher mean pfs_months than patients not receiving it.
rationale: Wrong treatment variable (pembrolizumab vs olaparib).

text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months (ECOG PS 1 and 2 each have lower mean PFS than ECOG 0).
rationale: Targets ECOG PS, which is not part of the ground-truth variable set.

text: Patients with mcrpc=1 (castration-resistant disease) have shorter pfs_months than mcrpc=0 patients.
rationale: Targets mcrpc, which is not part of the ground-truth variable set.

text: Patients with visceral_mets=1 have shorter pfs_months than visceral_mets=0.
rationale: Proposes a marginal effect of visceral_mets on PFS, but ground truth uses visceral_mets only as part of a conjunctive subgroup modifying the olaparib effect, not as a main effect.

text: Patients with liver_mets=1 have shorter pfs_months than liver_mets=0.
rationale: Targets liver_mets, which is not among the ground-truth variables.

text: Patients with bone_mets=1 have shorter pfs_months than bone_mets=0.
rationale: Bone metastases is not among the spec's variables and no subgroup-conditional olaparib effect is proposed.

text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months (positive linear relation).
rationale: Albumin is not in the ground-truth variable set and the hypothesis is a main-effect linear claim, not the BRCA2/PSMA/Gleason/visceral subgroup.

text: Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative linear relation).
rationale: LDH is not a ground-truth variable and no olaparib subgroup interaction is proposed.

text: Higher baseline psa_ng_ml is associated with shorter pfs_months.
rationale: PSA is not in the spec's variable set and the hypothesis ignores the olaparib subgroup-conditional effect.

text: Higher alkaline_phosphatase_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
rationale: Alkaline phosphatase is not among the ground-truth variables and the hypothesis is unrelated to the olaparib subgroup.

text: Higher hemoglobin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months.
rationale: Hemoglobin is not a ground-truth variable and no olaparib subgroup interaction is described.

text: Older age_years is associated with shorter pfs_months.
rationale: Age is not in the spec's variable set and the claim is a main effect, not the multi-variable subgroup-conditional olaparib effect.

text: Greater weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months.
rationale: Weight loss is not in the ground-truth variables and the hypothesis does not address the olaparib subgroup.

text: Higher gleason_score is associated with shorter pfs_months.
rationale: Although gleason_score is in the spec, this hypothesis proposes only a main effect and does not involve olaparib, BRCA2, PSMA, or visceral mets in a subgroup-conditional way.

text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
rationale: CRP is not in the ground-truth variable set and no olaparib subgroup interaction is proposed.

text: Higher nlr (neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
rationale: NLR is not among the ground-truth variables and the hypothesis does not address olaparib/BRCA2/PSMA/Gleason/visceral mets.

text: There is a positive interaction between treatment_olaparib and brca2_mutation on pfs_months: BRCA2-mutated patients receiving olaparib have substantially longer PFS than expected from the additive effects (PARP-inhibitor synthetic lethality).
rationale: Captures only the olaparibÃ—BRCA2 piece and omits the required psma_high, gleason_score, and visceral_mets subgroup conditions, so it does not identify the same multi-variable data-generating process.

text: There is a positive interaction between treatment_lu177_psma and psma_high on pfs_months: PSMA-high patients receiving Lu177-PSMA have longer PFS than expected.
rationale: Concerns Lu177-PSMA rather than olaparib and does not reference brca2/gleason/visceral mets.

text: There is a positive interaction between treatment_pembrolizumab and msi_high on pfs_months: MSI-high patients receiving pembrolizumab have longer PFS than expected.
rationale: Concerns pembrolizumab and MSI-high, none of which are ground-truth variables.

text: ar_v7_positive is associated with shorter pfs_months (poor prognostic).
rationale: AR-V7 is not part of the ground-truth variables and no treatment effect on the specified subgroup is proposed.

text: There is a negative interaction between treatment_enzalutamide and ar_v7_positive on pfs_months: AR-V7-positive patients gain less PFS from enzalutamide than AR-V7-negative patients (AR-V7 is a known mechanism of resistance to AR-targeted therapy).
rationale: Involves enzalutamide and AR-V7, not olaparib/BRCA2/PSMA/Gleason/visceral mets.

text: There is a negative interaction between treatment_abiraterone and ar_v7_positive on pfs_months: AR-V7-positive patients gain less PFS from abiraterone.
rationale: Involves abiraterone and AR-V7, none of which are ground-truth variables.

text: There is an interaction between treatment_enzalutamide and mcrpc on pfs_months: mCRPC patients respond differently to enzalutamide than non-mCRPC patients.
rationale: Involves enzalutamide and mCRPC status, unrelated to the ground-truth variables.

text: There is an interaction between treatment_abiraterone and mcrpc on pfs_months.
rationale: Involves abiraterone and mCRPC status, unrelated to the ground-truth variables.

text: There is an interaction between treatment_docetaxel and mcrpc on pfs_months.
rationale: Involves docetaxel and mCRPC status, unrelated to the ground-truth variables.

text: There is an interaction between treatment_enzalutamide and gleason_score on pfs_months.
rationale: Wrong treatment (enzalutamide) and missing the BRCA2/PSMA/visceral_mets predicates that define the ground-truth subgroup.

text: There is an interaction between treatment_abiraterone and gleason_score on pfs_months.

text: After adjustment for ECOG, albumin, LDH and treatment, higher crp_mg_l remains associated with shorter pfs_months.

text: After adjustment, higher nlr remains associated with shorter pfs_months.

text: Docetaxel benefit on pfs_months differs in bone_mets vs non-bone patients (interaction).

text: After full multivariable adjustment, no positive interaction of treatment_lu177_psma with psma_high on pfs_months emerges (confirming null from iter 3).

text: Gleason score becomes a significant negative prognostic factor for pfs_months after adjustment for the other prognostic features (despite null univariate effect).

text: ECOG, mCRPC, albumin, LDH, PSA, weight loss, visceral mets and Gleason score are each independently significant prognostic factors for pfs_months in the multivariable model (R^2 ~ 0.95).
rationale: Lists independent main-effect prognostic factors, not the olaparib subgroup interaction.
